He doesn't want His children sick any more than you'd want your own child to be ill. This is clear from Mt. 7:7-11; Mk. 11:22-24; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26.

Your life will overflow with blessings you may have been taught aren't available today. You're receiving new light—walk in it, and all things will be yours (1 Jn. 1:7; 1 Cor. 3:21-23).

People are sick, unhappy, and defeated simply because they've been taught wrongly, haven't surrendered their lives to God, and don't understand how to pray effectively. These conditions aren't God's will.

If everyone conformed to God's will, we could banish want, poverty, sickness, sin, and the entire curse. God would create the New Heavens and New Earth immediately. Because not everyone will do this, God must forcibly put down rebellion before making all things new—that's the purpose of the Millennium.

But as an individual, you can receive the benefits of the promises here and now. That's exactly what you're going to do as you learn how simple God's will really is and discover that His provision includes these blessings for you.

We can't give you everything you need to know in one lesson—or even several. But you can start receiving benefits right now if you follow these instructions. The conditions of defeat didn't come upon humanity in a day, and they can't be overcome until you know the truth that sets you free.

Jesus said, "If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed; and ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free" (Jn. 8:31-36).

## XI. What Faith Is and How to Use It

If you want to please God, you must have faith: "But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him" (Heb. 11:6).

Here's a sure way to please God. If something this simple makes God happy, why wouldn't everyone who loves Him put forth maximum effort to have faith?

It's similar to how we feel when friends trust us. None of us would be pleased if our friends constantly acted like we were lying every time we spoke. We wouldn't feel like doing anything for people who treated us that way.

The same is true with God. He's a person, and we should treat Him as one. Don't insult Him by questioning and doubting every promise He makes. How can we expect answered prayers while maintaining such an attitude? How can we repeatedly ask God for something while our actions and innermost feelings communicate that we don't believe or trust Him?

Is it any wonder we get so few prayers answered this way? God is under no obligation to answer when we lack implicit faith for what we ask.

Faith is simple. It's believing God without wavering, doubting, or questioning what He says. It's taking God at His Word and believing He's able to perform what He promised. It's believing not only that He's able, but that He *will* do it.

Faith counts things that "be not as though they were" (Rom. 4:17). Faith is absolute conviction that what God promised and what we've asked according to His Word is already done. It's "the substance of things hoped for"—the first payment on what we desire from God (Heb. 11:1-3).

**How do you get faith?** By hearing God's Word (Rom. 10:17).

**How do you use faith?** Take God at His Word and judge Him faithful to grant what you ask in Jesus' name. Ask, then believe it's done. Thank God as if it's already done. Expect the complete answer whether you see it immediately or not.

Ignore symptoms, feelings, impossibilities, or anything contrary. It shall be done regardless of how impossible it appears outwardly. Faith laughs at impossibilities and cries, "It is done!"

Faith leaves all results and answers with God—that's His part—and assumes it's done. Our part is to cooperate with faith and not hinder its work by doubting and questioning the "how" and "when" of the answer.

When a Christian truly exercises faith, there's no question about receiving an answer. You can receive healing, health, business success, prosperity, happiness—anything that brings victory in any area of life (Mt. 17:20; 21:22; Mk. 11:22-24; Jn. 15:7, 16; Rom. 8:32; Heb. 11:6; Jas. 1:5-8).

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement One. Do You Know:

- **That the Earth is more than 6,000 years old and that there were inhabitants on this Earth before Adam?** This is true. From Adam to 1948, there have already been 6,172 years, as we'll see in future lessons. The Bible also teaches that a social system existed on Earth that was destroyed by a great flood long before Adam. This flood is pictured in Gen. 1:2 as covering the Earth before the six days when Adam was created. It's also mentioned in Ps. 104:5-9. Peter speaks of it as "the world [Greek, *kosmos*, social order] that then was, being overflowed with water, perished: but the heavens and the earth, which are now [since the six days of restoration], by the same word are kept in store, reserved unto fire" to be purified, resulting in "a new heavens and a new earth" (2 Pet. 3:4-13). The cause of this first flood was Lucifer's fall and the rebellion of one-third of God's angels, along with the people living on Earth whom Satan ruled. That Satan ruled Earth and led an invasion into Heaven to overthrow God is clear from Isa. 14:12-14; Ezek. 28:11-17; Lk. 10:18; 1 Tim. 3:6. The devil was defeated, and the Earth was cursed and placed under water, as in Gen. 1:2; Ps. 104:5-9; 2 Pet. 3:5-7. All life was totally destroyed—birds, men, vegetation, and all the cities where pre-Adamites lived (Jer. 4:23-26). In the Earth's restoration during six days, as in Gen. 1:3–2:25, God told Adam to "replenish" the Earth. Adam and Eve were the first inhabitants of this present creation. This will be fully proven in Lesson Seven.

- **That giants fifteen to thirty feet tall lived on Earth during two different periods?** In Gen. 6:4 we read, "There were giants in the earth in those days [before the flood]; and also after that [after the flood], when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare children to them." Giants were born and grew up in those times. That these giants exceeded fifteen feet is clear from Dt. 3:11, where Og's bedstead was eighteen feet, nine inches long and eight feet, four inches wide. In Lesson Eleven, we'll see that some giants were even taller than Og. Giants are mentioned many times in Scripture (Num. 13:33; Dt. 2:11, 20; 3:11, 13; Josh. 12:4; 13:12; 5:8; 17:15; 18:16).

- **That God is a real person with a spirit-body, personal soul, and personal spirit?** (Job 13:8; Heb. 1:3; Dan. 7:9-14; 10:5-7; Ezek. 1:26-28; Jn. 5:37; Rev. 4:2-4; 5:1-7; Acts 7:54-59). God has a soul (Heb. 10:38; Isa. 42:1); spirit (Ps. 143:10; Isa. 30:1); hands and fingers (Exod. 31:18; Ps. 8:3-6; Rev. 5:1-7); hair, face, and other bodily parts (Dan. 7:9-14; 10:5-19; Exod. 24:10; 33:20-23; Ezek. 1:26-28). God eats (Gen. 18; Exod. 24:10-11); lives in a city and sits on a throne (Jn. 14:1-3; Rev. 4:1-5; 21:1-27; 22:3-5); walks (Gen. 18:1-22, 33); rides upon cherubs, chariots, and other things (Ps. 18:10; 69:17; 104:2; Ezek. 1:1-28); and can do anything man can do. This will be fully proven in Lesson Four.

- **That Noah was not 120 years building the ark?** In Gen. 6:8-10, when Noah was told to build the ark, he already had three sons. According to Gen. 6:18, these sons were already grown and married before God gave the building command. Gen. 11:10 tells us that two years after the flood, Shem was only 100 years old. Noah's family was in the ark one year and 17 days, making Shem 96 when he entered. If he was grown and married before Noah began building, and Shem was only 96 when it was finished, it obviously didn't take 120 years. The 120 years of Gen. 6:3 refers to Adam, not Noah, as we'll see in Lesson Eleven.

- **That it rained from time to time before the flood?** The scripture used to prove otherwise is Gen. 2:5-6. Reading these verses carefully shows they refer to the time before plants were in the ground and before man was created to till the soil. God created clouds "to cause it to rain on the earth" (Job 38:9, 26-28). Clouds existed throughout Lucifer's kingdom on Earth before Adam (Isa. 14:12-14) and ever since the second day's work (Gen. 1:6-8). Since there's no statement that mist watering the ground was God's permanent method for growing vegetation, and since clouds were made to give rain, and they existed after the second day, we can logically conclude it rained ever since clouds were formed to fulfill God's creative purpose.

- **That there is no end of the world taught in Scripture?** In all the references in Lesson One, Point 1, about "the end of the world," the Greek word is *aion*, meaning *age*. There will be an end to this age, but not an end of the world. The common theory that the world is ending is based on misunderstanding this Greek word. "The earth abideth for ever," we're told in Eccl. 1:4; Ps. 104:5 and other passages. As we'll see in our next lesson, eternal generations of natural people will live on Earth for resurrected humanity to rule.

- **That Heaven and the planets are inhabited?** This is clear from many scriptures: "Rejoice, ye heavens, and ye that dwell in them . . . he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, to blaspheme . . . them that dwell in heaven" (Rev. 12:12; 13:6). See also Col. 1:16-18; Rom. 1:20; 1 Cor. 15:35-50.

- **That man will have access to the planets in the future?** The Bible teaches that redeemed humanity will travel between Heaven and other planets in the New Heavens and New Earth. This was man's privilege before the fall, but sin caused humanity to fall short of God's glory (Rom. 3:23). Man was originally given dominion over the sun, moon, and stars—which would require access to rule them (Ps. 8:3-6). In the eternal future, believers will become heirs of God and joint-heirs with Christ, able to be with them wherever they are throughout eternity (Rom. 8:17-18; 1 Cor. 15:24-28; Heb. 2:5-11; Rev. 21:1–22:5).

- **That men with natural bodies have gone to Heaven and lived there for thousands of years?** Enoch has been in Heaven over 5,185 years and Elijah over 2,600 years—yet neither has died (Gen. 5:24; Heb. 11:5; 2 Ki. 2; Mal. 4:4-5; Zech. 4:11-14; Rev. 11:3-7). The old theory that flesh and blood cannot enter Heaven isn't biblical. While it says "flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God," it doesn't say man cannot enter it. We enter God's kingdom at the New Birth (Jn. 3:3-5; Rom. 14:17). Paul said he went to Heaven (2 Cor. 12:1-3) and came back to tell us about it (Rom. 1:19; Heb. 11:8-16; 13:14). John was also taken to Heaven and returned to describe it (Rev. 4:1–5:7; 7:9-17;

supplements.title: Supplement 2
supplements.content:
## Supplement Two

**For Lessons Three and Four**

---

## I. What This New Conception of God Will Mean to You

The idea that the Godhead consists of three separate and distinct persons—each with a personal spirit-body, a personal soul, and a personal spirit—will mean so much to you as you learn to know and love God and follow the divine plan for your life. At the very least, this conception of God will make the whole Godhead comprehensible to you. It will no longer be a mystery beyond all possible understanding, as you may have been taught.

Because God the Father is a person, He expects you to respect Him as you would any other great personage—and as you should the Sovereign Moral Governor of all free moral agents. God has a personal plan for your life that fits perfectly into His larger plan for humanity. In other words, He has invited and made it possible for each of you to have a personal part in fulfilling His plan for man.

You should enter wholeheartedly into cooperation with God's plan for your life. God's plan is a mutual one for the best and highest good of all creation. He wants to take you into partnership for eternity. As Paul expressed it, "workers together with him" (2 Cor. 6:1).

Think of it like this: God is inviting you to become a junior partner in the most important enterprise in the universe. This should be the thrill of your life. You should count it the greatest honor ever bestowed upon you to have a part with Him in bringing about the highest good of the universe. You should hunger daily to know Him better as a person and to know His plan, so that you may become an integral part of God and His program.

The plan of God for man includes both the means and the ends—prayer and its answer, the labor and its fruit. There is a single plan that embraces all things for the good of all creation. It is part of the perfection of God that He would have a plan for man—the best plan possible. This plan is already predestinated according to the eternal purpose of Him that works all things after the pleasure of His own will (Isa. 46:10-11; Dan. 4:35; Eph. 1:3-11; 2:10; 3:10-11).

This personal concept of God will enable you to work with God as a person, pray to Him as you would talk to any person in whom you put trust and confidence, and obey Him in all things—realizing that everything is done in the presence of God. As you continue these lessons, the plan of God will be unfolded to you, and you will get to know God in a real, personal way.

As you conform to the plan, you will get to know the power of God and learn how to use it to get the many blessings of this life for yourself and for others. God's plan will become a personal part of your life as you seek to know and follow it. God will become your best friend and a personal Father and Partner in your everyday affairs. You will come to know the glory and the blessing of a secret relationship with God such as few people know.

---

## II. All Men Are Sinners

The first thing to do in getting acquainted with God is to know yourself and your relation to God, as well as to know what you can receive from Him. The Bible declares that "all have sinned and come short of the glory of God" (Rom. 3:23).

If you haven't yet realized your condition of sinfulness, or if you've been deceived by false religions into believing that you are not a sinner, the thing to do is throw out that false teaching. You know in your heart that you have sinned and been condemned in your own conscience for many acts you've committed in life. The Bible makes it clear that "sin is transgression of the law" (1 Jn. 3:4) and that "all unrighteousness is sin" (1 Jn. 5:17).

Anything that the Bible forbids is sinful. Lists of sins that damn the soul are plainly recorded, so there can be no excuse for not knowing what God's will is concerning sin.

In Mk. 7:21-23 we read:

> "For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders, thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness: all these evil things come from within, and defile the man."

In Rom. 1:29-32 we read of other sins that condemn people before God:

> "Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers, backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventers of evil things, disobedient to parents, without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful: who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them."

Again we read:

> "Know ye not that the unrighteous shall not inherit the kingdom of God? Be not deceived: neither fornicators, nor idolators, nor adulterers, nor effeminate, nor abusers of themselves with mankind, nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor extortioners, shall inherit the kingdom of God" (1 Cor. 6:9-11).

Paul, in Gal. 5:19-21, gives another catalog of sins that will damn the soul:

> "Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness, idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies, envyings, murders, drunkenness revellings and such like . . . they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God."

All these scriptures prove beyond doubt that God condemns all men as sinners, for all people outside of Christ commit some of these sins in varying degrees. If anyone has committed even one of these sins at any time in their life, they have incurred the death penalty and must accept Jesus Christ as a personal Savior or be eternally condemned and lost—according to Scripture, which says, "The soul that sinneth, it shall die," and "they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God" (Ezek. 18:4; Gal. 5:21; 1 Cor. 6:9-11).

---

## III. The Key That Unlocks All Promises

Jesus taught that a man "must be born again" and "Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God . . . Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God . . . Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven . . . Except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish" (Jn. 3:1-8; Mt. 18:3; Lk. 13:1-5).

The New Birth is absolutely necessary to being saved and having the right relationship with God so that you can claim the benefits of the Bible. The New Birth is *not* confirmation, church membership, water baptism, taking the sacrament, observing religious duties, an intellectual reception of Christianity, orthodoxy of faith, going to church, saying prayers, reading the Bible, memorizing creeds, being moral, being cultured or refined, doing good deeds or doing your best, being as good as the next fellow, or any of the other things that people are trusting in to save them.

Neither do these things bring about the New Birth. Nicodemus, whom Jesus addressed concerning the New Birth, had most of these qualifications—but Jesus said to him, "Ye must be born again." The thief on the cross and many others whom Jesus forgave while on Earth were saved without these things. They simply did the one necessary thing: they became "converted" and "born again" by accepting Jesus Christ as their personal Savior and by repenting and turning to God with their whole heart as a little child—as proven in Mt. 9:1-7; Lk. 7:48-50; 18:9-14; 23:43; Jn. 8:1-10; Acts 3:19; 8:36-37; 16:31.

Every person who is born again will automatically have the above external evidences of a good life as a result of the New Birth. However, it is sad to say that millions of people are trusting these outward things to save them, believing that they are the evidences of a right relationship with God. Millions will die and be lost without the New Birth because they have been misled about what it actually means to be born again.

There is no excuse for being deceived if you will read your own Bible instead of just listening to some religious teachers.

It is all-important that you pay personal attention to your eternal welfare and not simply trust even the best of men. If you permit others to mislead you in eternal matters and you are lost, it will be too late then to personally see after your welfare—so let's take care of it now.

Don't take the attitude that you cannot be deceived and that your church is the only right one and cannot mislead you. This may be true, but it would be wise for you to make sure of it by going to the Bible for yourself. See with your own eyes. Know from your own heart that you are right with God and have had the real New Birth experience and are living right with God every day.

There is no use fooling yourself—that will not get you anywhere. You are either born again or you are not. You are either really saved, or you are being deceived into thinking that you are, and you are lost.

If you're not getting answers to your prayers, it's either that you are not saved or you are doubting God. If your obstacle is doubt, then you should quit it. If it's not doubt, then it will pay for you to examine yourself and see if you're really right with God. He is obligated to answer you if you are His child. If you have once been saved, it may be that you have become cold in your experience and need to reconsecrate your life to God.

You know your own life and true relationship to God, so get the fact settled that you are genuinely saved and in present contact with God. Then you will be in the right position to get what God has promised.

Culture, refinement, and outward correctness of life—in the church or out of it—will not take the place of the New Birth. The trouble is in the heart (Mk. 7:18-23), and to merely reform the outward life will not save you.

It's similar to this: An artist could put a coating of wax and the most beautiful colors on the outside of a rotten apple, but it would still be rotten at heart. One bite into it would be a bite into decay. The fact is that outside of Christ, every person is rotten in the heart, and mere culture, refinement, outward respectability, morality, and outward correctness of life are simply artificial—the practice of hypocrites who, like whitewashed tombs, make clean the outside but within are full of extortion and excess, "dead men's bones, and all uncleanness" (Mt. 23:25-28).

---

## IV. The Evidences of the New Birth

**1.** You will be changed down in the depths of your being and be made a new creature:

> "Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new. And all things are of God, who hath reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ" (2 Cor. 5:17-18).

If you have never been changed like this, then it is certain that you have never been born again.

**2.** You will have the witness of the Spirit in your life that you are a child of God:

> "For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God. For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba Father. The Spirit himself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God" (Rom. 8:14-16).

**3.** You will have freedom from condemnation and from the law of sin and death, and you will live and walk in the Spirit:

> "There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit. For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death . . . That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit. For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit, the things of the Spirit . . . And if Christ be in you the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness" (Rom. 8:1-13; Gal. 5:16-26).

**4.** You will be cleansed from all sin and delivered from the power and dominion of sin as well as from the love of sin:

> "He shall save his people from their sins" (Mt. 1:21).

> "The blood of Jesus Christ cleanseth us from all sin . . . If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness" (1 Jn. 1:7-9).

**5.** You will be free from sin:

> "Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free . . . If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed" (Jn. 8:31-36).

> "Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound? God forbid. How shall we that are dead to sin, live any longer therein? . . . our old man was crucified with him, that the body of sin might be destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve sin. For he that is dead is freed from sin . . . reckon yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin . . . sin shall not have dominion over you . . . Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness? . . . ye were the servants of sin . . . being made free from sin, ye became the servants of righteousness . . . even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness. For when ye were the servants of sin, ye were free from righteousness . . . but now being made free from sin, and become servants to God, ye have your fruit unto holiness, and the end everlasting life" (Rom. 6:1-23).

> "The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death" (Rom. 8:1-4).

> "Everyone that doeth righteousness is born of him . . . he that doeth righteousness is righteous even as he is righteous. He that committeth sin is of the devil . . . whosoever is born of God doth not commit sin . . . whosoever is born of God overcometh the world . . . whosoever is born of God sinneth not; but he keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not" (1 Jn. 2:29; 3:7-10; 5:1-4, 18).

Many other scriptures say that when you are born again and in Christ, you receive power to become a son of God (Jn. 1:12); you have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts (Gal. 5:24); your sins are blotted out (Acts 3:19); you are washed, sanctified, and justified (1 Cor. 6:9-11; Rev. 1:5); you have turned from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to the power of God (Acts 26:18); you have salvation (Rom. 1:16; 2 Thess. 2:13); you are created in righteousness and true holiness (Eph. 4:22-24); you are God's elect (Eph. 1:7; 1 Pet. 1:2-4; Col. 3:12); and you depart from all iniquity (2 Tim. 2:4, 19-22).

I am fully aware that literally thousands of religious teachers are constantly saying that no one can live free from sin, and that we sin every day. You can see that this teaching is entirely out of harmony with the Bible by reading the scriptures above.

If these teachers sin every day, then they are sinners every day—and there is no time when they are not sinners. If they are sinners all the time, then there is no time when they are not under the death penalty of the broken law, and they will be sent to hell as sinners to pay the death penalty. Christ means nothing to them if they are sinners and cannot be saved from sin.

It is time that you wake up and stop listening to such teachers and begin to read the Bible and obey it. Naturally, a person who does not believe in salvation from sin can never be saved from it. If Christ came to save us from our sins and He cannot do it, then He has failed in His mission.

If the New Testament teaches freedom from sin, then it is either the truth or a lie. If it is a lie, then we might as well throw the whole Bible away and have nothing to do with religion. Christianity, in that case, is a mere sham and a false religion, teaching something that is not true. If salvation through Christ is true, however, then these modern teachers of religion are false, and no one can safely believe them or even listen to them deny the truths of the Bible.

Do not let anyone deceive you into thinking that there is no such thing as living a clean, holy, victorious life in Christ—secure from sin, the flesh, the world, and the devil. If you are not saved from these things, you are not saved from hell, according to the scriptures under Point II above.

There may not be such a life as far as these teachers are concerned—as far as they know. Either they do not know the Bible and they do not have biblical experiences, or they are plain rebels against truth and refuse to get such blessings. In either case, you should not listen to them concerning something as important as your eternal welfare.

Why not listen to Scripture instead? It will not deceive you. It will always tell you the truth, as you can see from the facts stated above.

If you want biblical experiences, if you want faith in God, answers to prayer, prosperity, healing and health, and happiness in God—you will have to conform to God and His Word as you receive it. It is not a question of deciding between what we say and what others say. It is a question of what you can plainly read in your own Bible. That and that alone you will have to give an account for, so do not permit anyone to lead you astray.

Let people brand you as old-fashioned if they want to. If you will follow the Bible, it will pay you great dividends and benefits untold in this life and in the life to come. You are the one who desires such benefits, and you are the one that must see to it that you cooperate with God and His Word to get such blessings.

Man is not going to give you such blessings, but man can hinder you from getting them from God—if you permit him to cause you to fail God and His Word.

---

## V. What the New Birth Is and How It Is Received

The New Birth is a new creation from above—the direct operation of the Word of God and the Spirit of God upon your life, changing you completely when you truly repent and turn to God.

This is brought about in the following manner:

1. **First**, recognize that you are a sinner and lost, without God and without hope.

2. **Second**, admit that Jesus Christ died on the cross to save you from sin by His own precious blood.

3. **Third**, come to God repenting of your sins and turning away from all sin, pleading the merits of the blood of Christ in the name of Jesus Christ—and you shall be born again. That is, the Holy Spirit will then definitely make you a new creature, cleansing you from all sin by the authority of the Word of God and by the blood of Christ that was shed to atone for your sins.

4. **Fourth**, you must believe from your heart when you confess with your mouth that God forgives you of your sins and that He cleanses you from all unrighteousness.

Scriptures for your authority to confess and believe are the following:

> "As many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name" (Jn. 1:12-13).

> "Him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out" (Jn. 6:37).

> "Whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life" (Jn. 3:14-21, 36; 5:24).

> "That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved; For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation . . . For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved" (Rom. 1:16—10:9-13).

> "Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out" (Acts 2:38-39; 3:19; 16:31).

> "For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God: not of works, lest any man should boast" (Eph. 2:1-10).

> "If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness" (1 Jn. 1:9).

The New Birth is not a coming into existence of the body, soul, and spirit. It is simply the cleansing of your body, soul, and spirit from all sin by grace through faith in the precious blood of Jesus Christ (Rom. 3:25), by the Word of God (Jn. 3:5; 15:3; Eph. 5:26; Jas. 1:18; 1 Pet. 1:23), and by the direct power of the Holy Spirit (1 Cor. 6:11; 12:13; Titus 3:5).

It is the new creature experience of a person being renewed in righteousness and true holiness (2 Cor. 5:17-18; Gal. 5:24; Eph. 4:24). It is the blotting out of all sins that have separated you from God (Isa. 59:2; Acts 3:19; 1 Jn. 1:7, 9; 2:29; 3:6-10; 5:1-4, 18). It is genuine conversion from death to life and from the power of Satan to the power of God (Mt. 18:3; Acts 3:19; 26:18; Jas. 5:19-20). It is the adoption of a person from the family of Adam and the family of Satan into the family of God (Rom. 8:14-16; Gal. 4:4-5; Eph. 1:5; 3:14-15; 1 Jn. 3:8-10).

To compare the New Birth with physical conception, begetting, birth, and human existence leads to too many false ideas, for the two births and two lives are not parallel at all:

> 1. One does not exist as a human being before his natural birth; he truly must be in existence before he can be born again.

> 2. One does not choose his natural birth, his parents, or environment; but he must choose the new birth and all the consecrations of Christian living (Jn. 3:16; Rev. 22:17).

> 3. One does not repent in order to become a human being by natural birth; but he must repent and obey the gospel daily to be a child of God (Mk. 1:15; 16:16; Lk 13:1-5; Acts 2:38).

> 4. The first experience is the actual coming into existence of a new body, soul, and spirit—a new person; whereas, the other is merely the reconciliation of such a person to God (2 Cor. 5:17-21).

> 5. The former is a literal, actual conception and birth to become a human being; the latter is simply an adoption of a human being into the family of God (Rom. 8:14-16; Gal. 4:4-5; Eph. 1:5; 3:14-15; 1 Jn. 3:8-10).

> 6. The one is coming into existence as an unclean sinner; the other is the cleansing of the sinner from all sin and uncleanness (1 Cor. 6:11; 1 Jn. 1:7-9; Rev. 1:5; 5:10; 7:14).

> 7. A person has no responsibility regarding his natural birth; in the new birth he has personal responsibility in the initial phase as well as in the continued life of daily righteousness and true holiness, which are required (Jn. 6:37; Lk. 9:23; Rom. 6:14-23; 8:12-13; 12:1-2; 2 Cor. 10:4-10; 2 Pet. 1:4-10).

---

## VI. What the Water of the New Birth Means

The Word of God is the "water" referred to in Jn. 3:5, as proven in Eph. 5:26; Jn. 6:63; 15:3; 17:17; 1 Pet. 1:23; Jas. 1:18.

When you believe the Word of God that you are a sinner, that Christ died to save you from all sin, and that if you confess your sins to God, turn from sin with a whole heart, and believe the gospel—you are conforming to the Word. Then the Holy Spirit will transform your life by the power of the Word of God and the blood of Christ.

The moment you do this, you are saved and born again. You must believe it and walk accordingly. You must begin reading the Bible and praying to God. You must begin to walk and live in the Spirit and conform to the Word of God as you receive the light (1 Jn. 1:7; Jn. 8:31-32).

---

## VII. Prosperity and Money

The old idea among Christians that God is against His children having material wealth—for fear they will lose their souls—is unscriptural. The Bible is full of records of God blessing people with prosperity (Gen. 13:3-9; 26:12, 16; 33:11; 39:2-23; 41:40-44; 1 Ki. 10:7; 2 Chron. 1:12; 9:22–17:5; 32:27; Job 1:3; etc.).

In both Testaments, God has promised riches and blessings untold to those who will serve and obey Him (Lev. 26; Deut. 28; 29:9; Josh. 1:7-9; 1 Ki. 2:3-4; 1 Chron. 20:20; 24:20; 26:5; Ps. 1:3; Prov. 10:22; 1 Cor. 16:1-2; 2 Cor. 8:9; 9:6-11; 1 Tim. 6:17; 3 Jn. 2).

God Himself is the richest person in the universe—so if it would be a sin to be rich, He would be the greatest sinner of all! The very streets of Heaven are paved with gold. Jewels of all kinds garnish the foundations. The gates are solid pearl, and every house is a mansion (Jn. 14:1-3; Rev. 21). If God has promised us these things in the next life, why would it be a sin to have some of these things in this life?

Having wealth is not a sin. The reason God's children are often so poor is that they do not ask and receive as He has promised. Money is alright to have, and it is a great blessing that can be used to help God's work and others in their needs—and this is the purpose of asking God for prosperity.

It is true that people are warned not to trust in riches and permit them to destroy their souls. But saved people can get victory over the wrong use of money just as they can get victory over other sins (Ps. 62:10; Prov. 11:28; Lk. 8:14; 1 Tim. 6:17-19). Such warnings were not given to cause God's people to desire poverty in order to be saved, but rather to regulate the right use of money when you are saved and prospered.

As seen in Point VII of Supplement One, God has provided abundance and has made no limitation to His children in getting all that they want in this life. The laws of prosperity will be fully covered in Lessons Eleven and Twelve, but here we may say: what you need of this world's goods, you should begin to pray for and believe the promises. Even before you get the full laws of prosperity, you can receive many benefits of this kind.

---

## VIII. Power With God

Thousands of Christians are in total ignorance about the Bible doctrine of power for believers. We want you to know the truth about this so you can start from the very first of these lessons to get what you want from God and to get power over the devil.

The Old Testament saints had great power and did remarkable acts that are seldom heard of today among Christians. Abraham had power to heal a whole nation at one time (Gen. 20). Moses and Aaron did great wonders in Egypt (Exod. 7:10–11:10) and in the wilderness (Exod. 15:22-26; Num. 11:2, 29-30; etc.). Moses healed the whole nation of Israel, and there was not a feeble one in all their tribes (Ps. 105:37; 107:20).

Joshua, many of the judges, kings, and prophets did many signs and wonders by the power of God. For example, Elijah did fifteen miracles and Elisha did thirty—such as raising the dead, shutting the heavens against rain, bringing rain, calling fire from heaven, dividing Jordan, healing people, helping people get out of debt, making iron float, and all kinds of miracles (1 Ki. 17:1—2 Ki. 13:21).

Christ came doing miracles and healing all manner of diseases to confirm the Word of God (Isa. 11:1-2; 42:1-7; 61:1-2; Mt. 4:23-24; 11:2-6; 12:18; Lk. 4:16-21; Acts 10:38). The apostles and many others did the same work before Pentecost (Mt. 10:1-20; Mk. 6:7-13; Lk. 9:49-50; 10:1-20).

Christ promised that all believers should have the signs of the gospel following them and that every believer should be able to do the works that He did (Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; 16:15-20; Lk. 24:49; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7; 16:23-26; Acts 1:1-8). The early believers did do the works of Christ (Acts 2:42-47; 3:6; 4:1-16, 29-33; 5:1-16; 6:3-8; 8:5-20; 9:17-18, 32-43; 11:21; 13:6-12; 14:3, 8-20, 27; 15:4; 16:16-34; 19:1-20; Rom. 1:11; 15:18-19, 29; 1 Cor. 1:7, 18-24; 2:1-5; 4:19-21; 5:1-5; 9:18; 12:1–14:40; 16:10; etc.).

We are bringing many truths to you that you seldom get in modern churches. We want you to know that everything God ever promised to Christians is for you—and that God has not changed. If there has been any change, it is on man's part.

If you will accept truth as you can plainly read it in all these scriptures, you can help us give to the world a new type of Christianity that it has not seen since the days of the early Church, except in a measure here and there.

We want you to know that you are receiving lessons that will wake up the Christian world—if Christians who get to know these teachings will rise to their responsibility in the gospel. We want you to know that these lessons and supplements are not just the same old doctrines that you can get in many books. We present old truths and many new ones—new to modern Christians, but not new to the early ones. Many early believers enjoyed these blessings to the full, as we will make clear to you in coming lessons.

During the next two weeks, review these four lessons and do all the things we instructed you to do in the two supplements. You now have been taught the general terms and subjects of the plan of God, that the Bible is the inspired

supplements.title: Supplement 3
supplements.content:
# Supplement Three

## For Lessons Five and Six

In Lesson Five, we explored God's original creations in the dateless past. We've talked about God in the eternal past and seen evidence that He has been creating throughout all eternity. We've discovered that God made a plan for humanity and for all His created beings who have free wills—and that the three divine members of the Godhead are now working out that plan for the highest good of everyone.

We've also traced the outline of God's plan from eternity past into all the eternity to come. God will yet have a universal, perfect, and sinless kingdom in the future—one from which all rebels against His eternal plan will be separated. We've seen that God created this vast universe to be inhabited by eternal free moral agents who willingly give themselves to bringing about the highest public good.

We've also explored the various kinds of beings that inhabit the universe. And we've spent considerable time studying the warfare that Satan, fallen angels, and demons are waging against God and His plan—and against those people who are devoted to cooperating with God.

As believers, you need to become fully acquainted with evil spirits and their tactics in this spiritual war. These forces won't stop at any low or degrading method of warfare. They have no righteous principles, so they'll take advantage of any weakness and ignorance among Christians everywhere.

We've seen that God permits these evil forces to remain active so believers can develop faith and character. As saints, you have a sacred duty and responsibility to know how to wage successful warfare against these unscrupulous spirits.

Sadly, most believers don't seem to care—or at least they don't put forth much effort to defeat these enemies. Many seem quite uninformed about the war between God and Satan. Often they unknowingly cooperate with Satan and his plan, spreading his doctrines and lies more than they cooperate with God and spread His truth.

This misdirection happens because people have changed the Word of God from what is plainly written into human theories and church traditions. These have been handed down by men who have nullified Bible truths by making them mystical, spiritual, and symbolic.

## How to Get Power from God over Sin

According to the Bible, God has all power, and there is no power that isn't ordained by God (1 Chron. 29:11-12; 2 Chron. 20:6; Ps. 62:11; Zech. 4:6; Rom. 13:1-3). If all power comes from God, and if Satan's power was given him by God, then it's clear who holds all power. It's equally clear that if you want power, you should go to God for it—not seek it from demons.

Jesus said, "If any man serve me, him will my father honour" (Jn. 12:26). Every child of God receives power at the new birth to become a son or daughter of God, and this opens up all the possibilities of power from God over all the works of the devil (Jn. 1:12-13).

Children of God are commanded to "Be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might" and to "Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil" (Eph. 6:10-18).

Paul speaks of believers having the "treasure [of God's power] in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us" (2 Cor. 4:7). He also states that "the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds; casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ" (2 Cor. 10:4-6).

Think about that! You have power through God to destroy the works of the devil and to bring every thought and imagination into obedience to Christ! How can anyone have this power and continue in sin and defeat? How can someone teach that we have to sin every day in thought, word, or deed?

Think of it like having access to a master key that opens every door in a building, yet insisting you're locked out. Such teachers haven't experienced the reality of what Christian life can be. They make poor leaders, even of the blind, and as Jesus said, "if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch" (Mt. 15:14).

No one who cares about truth and their eternal welfare should listen to or support anyone who teaches that you can't have a Christian experience that saves you from your sins and keeps you from living in sin.

Peter said, "Who are kept by the power of God through faith unto salvation ready to be revealed in the last time . . . As obedient children, not fashioning yourselves according to the former lusts in your ignorance: But as he which hath called you is holy, so be ye holy in all manner of conversation [manner of life]; Because it is written, be ye holy; for I am holy . . . Seeing ye have purified your souls . . . Being born again . . . having escaped the corruption that is in the world through lust . . . give diligence to make your calling and election sure: for if ye do these things, ye shall never fall" (1 Pet. 1:5, 14-16, 22-23; 2 Pet. 1:3-10).

Everyone who is born again lives free from the sin business (1 Jn. 2:29; 3:5-10; 5:1-4, 18; 2 Cor. 5:17; Eph. 4:24; Gal. 5:24).

This holy living is accomplished by God's power, not our own. It's possible because we're made free from the law of sin and death and because we walk not after the flesh but after the Spirit (Rom. 8:1-13).

Should we lower our view of the Almighty and say He cannot save from sin or keep free from sin those He redeems? If God can do this but chooses not to, then sending Christ to die for this purpose was a failure. Making full provision in the gospel for this to be normal life for every child of God would be a farce.

Scripture is definite that sinners "shall not inherit the kingdom of God." So if Christians are sinners and all other people are sinners, then not one person will be saved (1 Cor. 6:9-11; Gal. 5:16-24; Rom. 1:29-32; 8:1-13).

There's a natural power of godliness that comes with the new birth. Those who have only a form of godliness deny this power (2 Tim. 3:5). God's power gives each person "all things that pertain unto life and godliness, through the knowledge of him that hath called us to glory and virtue" (2 Pet. 1:3-10).

The power from God to live clean and holy is yours for the asking. You can use this power of godliness to show other sinners that the gospel is real—that every person can be saved and kept from all habitual sin (1 Jn. 1:7, 9; 2 Cor. 5:17; Titus 2:11-15).

It takes power from God to live victoriously over sin, the flesh, the world, and the devil. But God our Saviour has all power to sanctify and make us "meet for the Master's use, prepared unto every good work" (2 Tim. 2:18-26).

Not only can you have power to live a life pleasing to God, but you can receive power from God. The way to get this power is to make a full surrender to God, renouncing all sin and bad habits, believing on the Lord Jesus Christ to save from all sin and give sufficient power to overcome.

When you believe from the heart, you are cleansed from all sin (Rom. 10:9-10). When you receive Jesus—not just mentally or formally, but in reality—this cleansing takes place (Jn. 1:12). When you believe that Jesus is the Christ, not only mentally but from your innermost soul (which includes turning to God from sin and Satan), this cleansing happens (Acts 26:18; 1 Jn. 5:1-4).

You might say you've done this, yet old sins and habits remain in your life. There's been no change. It may be that you haven't made the necessary change for God to work and complete the transformation in your life.

The first thing to do is settle the fact that there must be a change—and there will be when you truly get into Christ. The following scriptures prove this: "If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new. And all things are of God, who hath reconciled us unto himself by Jesus Christ" (2 Cor. 5:17); "They that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts" (Gal. 5:24); "The blood of Jesus Christ cleanseth us from all sin" (1 Jn. 1:7); He has "washed us from our sins in his own blood" (Rev. 1:5).

It's simply ridiculous to think anyone could experience this kind of change and still continue in their old sins. Because it's clear there must be a change, do the one thing necessary to bring it about.

All you have to do is obey what the Bible requires, and the rest is up to God. "If we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness" (1 Jn. 1:9).

It's impossible for anyone to truly confess their sins and not be forgiven by God. It's possible for someone to confess only part of their sins and not be heard. You won't be forgiven if you hold back some sin or habit and refuse to give it up, or if you have hatred in your heart and refuse to forgive.

But when you're honest with God—giving up your sins and habits, willing to forgive everyone, and making proper restitution wherever possible—God will not fail to forgive and make you a new creature.

You must receive Christ: "But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name" (Jn. 1:12). This means you must quit receiving sin, the world, fleshly lusts, and the devil into your life. Christ and the devil cannot both be in your life at the same time.

Jesus said, "No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon" (Mt. 6:24).

If Jesus told the truth, then those who preach that everyone must serve sin in the flesh while serving God in spirit are wrong. There cannot be such a double life.

Paul said, "Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness" (Rom. 6:16-23).

## How to Get Power from God over Sicknesses

Power from God to overcome sickness is on the same basis as power from God to overcome sin. Jesus taught that God has power to do both, and that one power is just as easy to receive as another: "For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise and walk?" (Mt. 9:5).

He further taught that conversion and bodily healing go hand in hand: "lest at any time they should . . . understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them" (Mt. 13:15). Paul and others taught the same truth (Acts 28:25-31; Jas. 5:14-16; Ps. 103:1-3; 2 Chron. 7:14; Exod. 15:26).

Every believer can get power from God over diseases. God gives every one of His children power when they receive the true Spirit baptism (Lk. 24:49; Acts 1:8; 2 Tim. 1:7-8; Jn. 14:12). We'll explore this more in Lessons Twenty, Twenty-two, and Thirty.

Apart from receiving this baptism, faith is open to every child of God to get exactly what they want, as we can see from all the promises. Faith is power. The person who has faith has power. To the extent you have faith, you have power. Think of it like a key that starts an engine—faith is the key that sets God's power in motion.

There are degrees or measures of power, and God gives every child of God a measure of faith and power they can use to get what they need in life. Even if you don't have delegated power to exercise at will, you can still have faith in God. This faith sets all of heaven in motion on your behalf so you can get exactly what you have faith for.

As long as we limit God and His promises, we'll be limited in results. If we have unlimited and unqualified faith, we'll have unlimited and unqualified answers to prayer. That means we can have power over sin and sicknesses of all kinds.

The early disciples were given power over all demons and diseases, and they exercised this power before they were permanently endowed with power at Pentecost (Mt. 10:1-8; Mk. 3:15; 6:7-13; Lk. 9:1; 10:1-20). They were given power over all the power of the devil.

God's kingdom is always in power, not in word only (1 Cor. 4:20). Is God's kingdom still in existence? Has it lost its power? If not, then everyone in the kingdom has access to that kingdom's power.

Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God to all who believe (1 Cor. 1:18-24). It's impossible to have Christ in your life without having access to power, because He is the power of God to the believer.

The gospel is the power of God unto salvation to everyone who believes (Rom. 1:16). Salvation includes healing as well as forgiveness. If this is true, then the gospel is the power of God to heal, and no one can truly believe the gospel without believing in power to heal through the gospel.

The way to get power to heal yourself and others is by believing in Christ according to the gospel. By faith in the promises, each child of God can appropriate what they need. You can get healing through prayer and faith as simply and quickly as you got forgiveness of sins or anything else you've ever received from God.

Healing comes on the same basis as any other answer to prayer. So why not accept this truth and ask God in faith for the power of healing in your body? If it's provided in the gospel, you might as well have it. Why be satisfied with less?

Is it possible you don't want to be healed? Would you rather be sick and helpless? Certainly not! Then accept truth, which will make you free.

Being healthy is within your grasp. It's in your power. God wants His saving health to be known among all nations (Ps. 67:2). He has told us exactly how to have health.

If you'll literally practice the instructions of the following passages, you'll have health: "Forget not my laws; but let thine heart [not mind only] keep my commandments: for length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee. Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart: so shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man. Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths. Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the Lord, and depart from evil. It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones. Honour the Lord with thy substance, and with the first fruits of all thine increase: so shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine" (Prov. 3:1-10); "Attend to my words . . . For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh" (Prov. 4:20-27; 16:24).

In Ps. 91 you're told that if you "dwell in the secret place of the most High" and "abide under the shadow of the Almighty," then "There shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling."

In Isa. 58 you're promised that if you practice twenty principles of righteousness, "Then shall thy light break forth as the morning, and thine health shall spring forth speedily . . . Then shalt thou call, and the Lord shall answer."

It's God's highest will that His children be in health (3 Jn. 2).

## How to Get Power from God over Poverty

The Bible declares that God "giveth power to get wealth" (Deut. 8:17-18). When Solomon asked God for wisdom instead of wealth so he could better judge Israel, God gave him both. Solomon became one of the richest men who ever lived, simply because of God's blessing.

God also blessed Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, David, Job, and many others with great wealth. It's in God's power to make rich or to make poor (1 Sam. 2:7; Prov. 10:22; 1 Chron. 29:12).

If riches come to some people because of God's blessing, then having wealth isn't a sin. We're warned in Scripture not to misuse wealth to oppress the poor and not to let riches ensnare us into sin so we forget God and are lost (Prov. 11:28; Mt. 13:22; Lk. 8:14; 1 Tim. 6:17; Jas. 5:1-6).

The Bible lists several blessings better than riches: righteousness (Prov. 16:8; Ps. 37:17-18); a good name (Prov. 22:1); long life (Prov. 3:1-2); and wisdom (Prov. 3:13-16).

There are many assets in life better than money, so you shouldn't set your heart on wealth as your chief objective. People with riches face greater temptations and often do things they wouldn't do without money.

Riches are temporary and should be recognized as such (Prov. 23:5; 27:24). You're told not to set your heart on riches or trust in them, but to trust in the Living God (Ps. 52:7; 62:10; 23:4; 1 Tim. 6:17). Many Scripture passages reveal the sins of the rich—how they oppress the poor, are boastful, proud, mean, and ungodly because they have wealth and influence.

On the other hand, the Bible speaks of many blessings of wealth when used the right way for people's good and God's glory. Wealth provides protection (Prov. 10:15; 18:11); it makes many friends (Prov. 14:20); it gives power in life (Prov. 22:7); and it enables you to be rich in good works.

This in turn—if done in the right spirit (not to be seen by others, but because it's right to do good)—will result in increased rewards in eternity (1 Tim. 6:18).

Money is power in modern times. Every child of God could do infinitely more for God and lost souls with more of this kind of power. Think what could be done with riches in spreading truth and blessing people! There's no end to the good you could do if you had the means.

Looking at wealth from this perspective—even praying to God for it and expecting it from Him so you can do more for God's cause—isn't a sin. The average person doubts whether God cares about His children enjoying prosperity.

Satan and even well-meaning gospel workers constantly warn believers against desiring wealth until it's become a universal belief among Christians that wealth is more or less sinful. Yet if a wealthy person becomes interested in a local church or generous in helping Christ's work, they're greatly honored and respected. This honor wouldn't be given if having wealth or asking God for it were sinful.

Anyone with common sense knows there's nothing wrong with having wealth or getting it rightfully. Every child of God has many needs that could be met with more money. People often long for more wealth to better support their church and God's work. They don't consider this desire sinful.

If wealth suddenly came to them, they'd still believe they could be true Christians if they used it rightly. So we must conclude that the widespread theory that it's wrong to pray for financial help is unscriptural and unreasonable.

Some argue at this point that it's not wrong to have wealth, but there are many more important blessings to ask God for. This is really just an excuse for unbelief.

People simply don't want to let go of their old traditions, theories, and unbelief. They won't definitely pray and ask God for financial help the way they ask for physical or spiritual help.

If they'll learn the truth and ask God in faith, nothing will be impossible financially—any more than physically or spiritually. Let's be sensible and study the Bible from the standpoint of getting financial help.

First, let's ask ourselves questions about this subject like we're starting to ask about other things. Are we children of God? Does God really love us as we love our children? Would we want our children to prosper? Does God want anything less for His children than we want for ours?

Why should He be different from us in desiring the best things of life for His children? What would be sinful about God blessing His children with wealth? Just because a few backslide and lose their souls over prosperity, that's no more excuse for living in unbelief and poverty than rejecting salvation just because some people backslide.

From the standpoint of God's infinite Fatherhood, it's His highest will for His children to be blessed with prosperity.

Think about it from the standpoint of spreading the gospel to the ends of the earth—helping support missionaries, supporting God's work at home, helping the poor become prosperous, happy, and healthy. If every Christian would claim the promises as they should, all the financial problems of every local church and worldwide ministry would be automatically solved.

When Israel obeyed the Lord and brought their tithes and offerings, there were heaps upon heaps of supplies for God's work (2 Chron. 31:1-21). If all Christians were prosperous, there would be no need for modern methods of raising money for churches.

Some churches resort to gambling and un-Christlike activities just to keep going. If everyone were prosperous, ministers would only need to make an appeal or give people an opportunity to share from the abundance God has blessed them with.

Now let's look at Bible promises about prosperity. Notice how simple and clear God has made His Word about this. God promised Israel abundant prosperity if she would conform to His will (Lev. 25:21; 26:4-9; Deut. 7:13-15; 15:4-6; 28:7-8). Prosperity was promised as a natural result of obedience to God, and so it is today.

The Psalm that all Christians quote starts with "The Lord is my shepherd I shall not want." Other promises in the Psalms include: "They that seek the Lord shall not want any good thing" (Ps. 34:10); "Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits" (Ps. 68:19); "Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits: who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases; who redeemeth thy life from destruction; who crowneth thee with loving kindness and tender mercies; who satisfieth thy mouth with good things; so that thy youth is renewed like the eagle's" (Ps. 103:1-5). See Ps. 84:11.

To those who honor God with their tithes and offerings—not because they want blessings, but because it's right—God has promised, "I will open to you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it" (Mal. 3:9-11).

If someone argues this was for Israel only, we answer: it's also for the Church according to Mt. 6:26-33; 10:29; Lk. 6:38; 2 Cor. 9:6-12; Phil. 4:19; 3 Jn. 2. This last passage expresses God's will for Christians: "Beloved, I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth."

## How to Appropriate the Benefits of the Promises

The way to receive the promised benefits is to accept God's promises as being for you and ask God in faith for what you want. If you believe what you ask without wavering, it will be done for you because God loves you and will prosper you in everything you undertake.

If you're hindered in getting an answer, don't lose faith and blame God for not giving it. Remember there's a devil with many demons who will try to keep you from being prosperous.

You should recognize all hindrances against God's promises as coming from this source. Fight and resist Satan, and he will flee from you (Jas. 4:7). Claim the fulfillment of these promises just as you would for forgiveness, healing, or any other benefit you've asked of God.

If you're in the habit of running away every time the battle goes against you, you'll never get what you want. At some point you must take a stand with God against these evil spirits and master them. Then it will be easy to get what you want from God.

Review Lesson Six on how to defeat Satan, then literally practice what you know. Victory will be yours.

Happiness can be yours; freedom from fear and want can be yours; success can be yours; answers to all your prayers can be your experience—if you'll do what you're instructed. If you don't obey exactly what you know to do, how can you expect these blessings?

Satan isn't the type to give up. If he sees you're easily discouraged, he'll be even more aggressive in defeating you. Decide that all double-mindedness and failure must stop.

Have no fear of the outcome. Have faith in God. He will see you through. He's with you. He'll stand by you and back you in your fight for anything He has promised.

But He wants you to learn to fight, not be a defeatist just because the answer doesn't come the moment you ask. Anyone can give up and fail to get what they want. That's what almost all Christians are doing now because they lack proper teaching and coaching on how to get things from God.

We want to help you learn to receive from God those benefits that are rightfully yours as His child. If you don't get proper help from your church, we want to fill that gap.

You cannot fail. You must not fail. So much depends on your getting from God what He promised and what you've dared to ask. Your friends are watching to see whether you get what you asked for. Even sinners are watching your war with demons.

For their sake, listen carefully, learn exactly what to do, and do it without fail. The only cause of your failure is unbelief.

Can it be that you're still questioning God's will concerning the blessings He's abundantly promised? If so, stop all doubting right now!

It's pure nonsense to listen to the devil tell you that God won't do what He promised, or that it's not God's will for you to get what He said He'll give you. If you listen to the devil, no wonder God doesn't hear you.

Think about it—you wouldn't listen to anyone who called you a liar every time you opened your mouth and made a promise. Make sure this foolishness of making God a liar stops once and for all. Just believe what God says and forget what people and demons say!

God says you can have whatever you ask. Anyone who even suggests you can't have what you pray for is your enemy and God's enemy. They're helping Satan rob you of what's rightfully yours.

They can't rob you of these blessings if you decide not to listen to them and instead have faith in God. You know now what you must do, so do it with all your heart and you will receive abundantly.

God gives to all people liberally and doesn't scold them, but He does require faith without wavering. He won't answer on any other terms (Jas. 1:5-9; Heb. 11:6). If you don't get the answer, then you know what's wrong. Correct it, and you will be heard.

It's similar to trying to turn on a light that won't work—you have sense enough to know something is wrong. It would be foolish to keep trying without fixing the problem. When you don't get what you pray for, act sensibly. Find out what's wrong and correct it.

It's not possible that you won't know what's wrong. The steps to getting benefits from God are simple. If you're still having trouble and don't know what to do, review Supplements One and Two and carefully follow the lessons on how to pray.

If you've followed all these steps and still there's no answer, then settle it once and for all that it's unbelief and that you're under direct attack from demons. Then you'll know what to do.

"Resist the devil and he will flee from you" (Jas. 4:7). How to resist the devil is clear: Take a firm stand in your innermost being against thoughts that you're not going to get an answer. Renew your faith in what God has said. Get it firmly settled that what you asked is promised by God. If it's promised, have no further doubt that you're going to receive.

Begin praising God for it and obey Ps. 37:4-5: "Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart. Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass."

Maintain this attitude and ignore any feeling, thought, or circumstance that contradicts the answer. You can say "I believe" just as easily as you can say "it's hard to have faith."

You can make every thought and word a positive act of faith. You can simply refuse any negative thought. Learn to build up faith instead of tearing it down.

State the truth in everything you say. Never think or express one thought contrary to the fact that God has heard you and that you have the answer. Though you can't see the answer, it's still yours—for faith is the substance of things hoped for and the evidence of things not seen (Heb. 11:1; Rom. 4:17-21).

If you have faith, you have the answer.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Three. Do You Know:

- **That the planets reveal truth?** Besides the natural times and seasons, there are certain times and seasons for preordained events in God's plan for humanity. The children of Issachar had understanding of "the times, to know what Israel ought to do" (1 Chron. 12:32). The wise men knew by the star when Christ was born (Mt. 2:1-2, 7, 9-10). Jesus understood the signs of His time and rebuked the Pharisees for not discerning them as well as they could discern natural seasons (Mt. 16:1-4). In Ps. 19:1 we read that the heavens declare God's glory and the firmament shows His handiwork. See also Acts 1:6-7; 1 Thess. 5:1-3. These passages clearly indicate that the planets rule times and seasons and reveal certain truths.

- **That eternity is made up of time and that time shall not cease?** There's a time and season for everything done under the sun (Eccl. 3). God originally made the sun, moon, and stars to regulate times and seasons on the eternal earth. The work of day four was the permanent restoration of the solar system in connection with the restored earth—to divide day from night and to be for signs, seasons, days, and years forever (Gen. 1:14-18). This solar regulation causing endless time on earth is eternal: "They shall fear thee as long as the sun and moon endure, throughout all generations . . . His name shall endure for ever: His name shall be continued as long as the sun" (Ps. 72:5, 17); "I have sworn unto David my servant, Thy seed will I establish for ever . . . his throne as the days of heaven . . . as the sun before me. It shall be established for ever as the moon" (Ps. 89:4, 27-37); "Thus saith the Lord, which giveth the sun for a light by day, and the ordinances of the moon and of the stars for a light by night . . . If those ordinances depart from me, saith the Lord, then the seed of Israel also shall cease being a nation before me for ever" (Jer. 31:35-36; 33:20-22). God promised Noah that day and night, summer and winter, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat would continue forever on earth (Gen. 8:22). The earth is eternal, so time made up of seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, and years is also eternal (Eccl. 1:4; Ps. 104:5). When the Bible says "time no longer" it should be "delay no longer," for it speaks of days and years continuing after this (Rev. 10:6-7; 12:6, 14; 13:5; 20:1-7). When the Bible says "there shall be no night there," it refers to the city, the New Jerusalem, not the earth outside the city (Rev. 21:23-27). We must conclude that time will continue eternally.

- **That you are in eternity now?** As we've seen, eternity will be made up of time as we know it. If time continues eternally as it is now, then in the sense of being in eternal time, we're now in eternity. We don't mean we're in our eternal state—that won't be true until we enter immortality (1

supplements.title: Supplement 4
supplements.content:
# Supplement Four

## For Lessons Seven and Eight

In Lesson Seven, we discovered that Earth was originally ruled by an angel—one who governed perfectly for an unknown period of time until he decided to exalt himself above God. Many other angels also rebelled against God's Moral Government. God ceased to be all-in-all. Because of this rebellion, the Earth was reduced to chaos by a flood, and all life was destroyed, as we see in Gen. 1:2.

Then in Lesson Eight, we saw how God restored the Earth to a second habitable state in six literal days, creating new fish, birds, land animals, and humanity. We also explored *why* God did this. His original purpose in creating the Earth was to fill it with intelligent free moral agents—beings He could reveal Himself to and bless forever with all the good things of life.

God's blessings on His creation were wonderful—beyond what human words can describe. If humanity had never sinned, there would have been no sickness, disease, defeat, harm, pain, suffering, sorrow, misery, failure, or any of the corruption that's now in the world through lust.

Because it was never God's will for humanity to sin or for these conditions to exist, we can safely conclude that this is *still* not His will.

Scripture—and common sense—both affirm that if humanity hadn't fallen, everyone would have been healthy, wealthy, and wise. It's equally clear that all free moral agents were meant to work toward the best public good according to the will of the Moral Governor. Even now, under sin's influence, every person is naturally obligated to consecrate themselves to the best good of all society.

God Himself, as the Supreme Moral Governor, is under the same moral obligation. Therefore, it was—and still is—His highest will that all creation exist in the highest state of perfection so this goal can be reached. It could never be God's will for even one person to be given over to anything that would hinder their part in helping all of society achieve the highest good.

So sin, sickness, poverty, want, unhappiness, and failure are *foreign elements* in God's eternal perfect plan for creation. Because they're foreign, they must be eliminated before God's plan can be fully realized. They are not—and cannot be—any part of God's will for His creation. They are the works of the devil and are enemies of God and all creation.

If they're enemies, then we should treat them as such. We should wage constant, aggressive warfare against them until they're entirely driven out of the personal life of every child of God who cooperates with Him. Think of it like weeds in a garden—you don't negotiate with weeds or welcome them as "blessings in disguise." You pull them out relentlessly until your garden flourishes the way it was designed to.

As long as Christians count these curses as friends, blessings in disguise, or the will of God, they'll be failing God and cooperating with their own enemies to destroy themselves and mar God's perfect plan for humanity.

## How Saints Add to the Glory of Satan in a Threefold Way

### I. Saints Add to Satan's Glory by Giving in to Him

**1. THROUGH UNBELIEF.** "But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him" (Heb. 11:6); "But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering" (Jas. 1:5-8).

It's a well-known fact that where people are taught the truth, they develop faith and receive blessings from God. After all, "faith cometh by hearing; and hearing by the word of God" (Rom. 10:17). No one can have faith in something they know nothing about. No one can trust God until they know about Him.

Likewise, unbelief is the result of false teaching—of accepting lies instead of truth, of cooperating with Satan instead of God.

Some argue that parts of the gospel are only for Jews, other parts only for Gentiles, some parts for this age and others for that period. They claim that miracles, healings, gifts, and supernatural powers were only for the apostles and aren't for us today.

Most believers have become experts at tearing down faith and throwing cold water on anyone who dares hope that God will be a just, merciful, loving, and kind Heavenly Father—one who gives His children at least as much as evil parents give theirs. If someone even suggests that God will give His children what He's promised, they're immediately told not to expect too much from God, that such blessings might not be best for them. They're told to simply be satisfied with whatever the Heavenly Father sees fit to give and that it's sinful to be bold enough to ask for more.

If we're going to believe in the Bible at all, let's believe it in its entirety. If we're going to profess that God is able to do all things, then let Him do at least a few of the things He has abundantly promised. If we're going to be in gospel work, let's work *with* the gospel, not against it.

Let's believe the best of it—the promises—and let's receive its promised benefits. If we're going to be as sensible in religion as we are in other areas of life, then let's not accept the worst ideas of the Bible while rejecting the best. We don't act that foolishly in our everyday affairs, so why be foolish in religious matters when we can do better?

This is just plain common sense. Let's wake up and recognize that when we oppose God and the Bible, we're cooperating with Satan and demons—our own enemies—and we're destroying ourselves while defeating those who are trying to conform to God's whole will according to the Bible.

Yes, it's true: we glorify Satan through unbelief and by giving in to his gospel-defeating program. This can be stopped right here and now—and it must be stopped before we can begin reaping the wonderful benefits of God's promises.

**2. SAINTS GLORIFY SATAN THROUGH FEAR OF SICKNESS, PAIN, TROUBLE, AND DEATH.** Job said, "The thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me" (Job 3:25).

Fear comes through wrong thoughts, and wrong thoughts are the seeds of future actions. Thoughts are blueprints for what we'll eventually do. Every thought projected into the subconscious mind eventually surfaces in the active mind. If we entertain it there, it will pass into the cells and tissues of our bodies.

What we think, to a great extent, becomes materialized. This is especially true with thoughts affecting our physical, material, and spiritual lives. When we persistently hold onto thoughts in these areas, we cause certain events to come to pass—at least within the realm of human possibility.

Think of it like a navigation system in your car—whatever destination you program in is where you'll end up. If you constantly program fear, worry, and defeat into your mind, don't be surprised when you arrive at those destinations.

The devil's chief work is getting people to think wrong thoughts—that they're sick, weak, helpless, unhappy, mistreated by God and others. He wants us to believe we're facing certain calamities financially, physically, mentally, morally, and spiritually—and that we have to accept these conditions as God's will simply because they've occurred.

Satan knows that if he can get people to form a strong mental picture of something, they'll act accordingly to make it a reality. If he can get them thinking and talking about sickness, failure, or whatever they're susceptible to, those conditions will soon materialize. If he can get them moaning, grumbling, and discouraged about any difficulty, it will develop.

Paul said, "God hath not given us a spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind" (2 Tim. 1:7). Job's spirit of fear wasn't from God. This was probably one reason God permitted him to be tested. He was perfect in many ways, but not in being free from fear. If he wasn't free from fear, he didn't have perfect love—and there was plenty of room for refinement in his life.

If you would ignore, minimize, deny, and refuse sickness, poverty, troubles, tears, worries, sin, and suffering—whatever shouldn't be in the life of a child of God—you would soon have nothing to fear or worry about. What you are today is the result of yesterday's thoughts and fears. The sins, sicknesses, failures, and unhappiness you experience today are the results of yesterday's thoughts and plans. If those wrong thoughts had been cast down, no such wrongs would have occurred.

If a man or woman lives in thoughts of illicit love, they'll soon become victims of sin and lust. Movies, novels, magazines, provocative advertisements, dance halls, and immodest people are constant fuel for wrong thoughts—and we must refuse to feed on such ideas. The mind must be kept clean and pure.

Mental pictures of lust set passions in motion that only supernatural force can break. Thoughts control and materialize mental pictures. Constant dreaming and longing for harmful things soon produce sinful acts. This is why superstitions exert such influence on many people.

Don't play with wrong thoughts. They're dangerous. Eventually they will bite like an adder and sting like a scorpion.

Think right thoughts and you'll have no fears—and then you'll have no regrettable actions. We're all mental broadcasting stations, raising or lowering the atmosphere of the community where we live.
