One grouchy, pessimistic, trouble-expecting person controlled by fear and wrong thoughts can bring darkness wherever they go.

If a child begins doing wrong and you constantly talk about their wrongdoing, the child may become confirmed in that behavior. Tell them constantly that they're no good, and soon they'll prove you right. They'll more or less mold their life by their mental environment. You expect them to be wrong, they expect themselves to be wrong, and unconsciously this thinking makes them wrong.

Such a life glorifies Satan as much as anything—maybe even more. If Satan can get you to fear, he'll glory over it because he knows this is the first step toward your downfall. Satan had many months of glory from Job because he convinced him to fear that certain events would happen. And this trial may have been the only way God could bring him out of that constant state of fear and worry.

**3. SAINTS GLORIFY SATAN BY GIVING IN TO HIM IN TIMES OF TEMPTATION AND TRIAL.** If every Christian realized the source of their temptation and understood that God hasn't forsaken them just because they're under attack from demons, they would conduct themselves much more courageously and Christ-like.

Satan gets people to think God has forsaken them when something goes wrong. He convinces them that if God were with them, they wouldn't be going through such a trial. But you're not backslidden just because you're tempted. No sin is committed until lust has conceived and sin is finished (Jas. 1:13-15).

People under attack from demons should do what James said: "Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you" (Jas. 4:7). Peter also said, "Be sober, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour; whom resist steadfast in the faith, knowing that the same afflictions are accomplished in your brethren that are in the world" (1 Pet. 5:8-9).

**4. SAINTS GLORIFY SATAN BY PERMITTING HIM TO KEEP THEM SICK.** No child of God has to be sick or stay sick. It's the right of every believer in Christ to have perfect health and freedom from the works of the devil.

The following passages prove this: "He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty . . . A thousand shall fall at thy side, and ten thousand at thy right hand; but it shall not come nigh thee . . . there shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling" (Ps. 91); "Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses" (Mt. 8:16-17); "And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive" (Mt. 21:21-22); "Ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you" (Jn. 15:7); "If the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you" (Rom. 8:11).

The responsibility for getting healed and remaining healed is up to you. If you want healing, resist the devil and pray in faith, nothing wavering. Believe that you've received the answer—that you're healed right here and now—and reject anything to the contrary.

If God has provided healing through Christ, then it's the right and duty of every person for whom Christ died to receive the benefits He died to provide. If it's the moral obligation of each person to consecrate themselves to the best good of all society, then it becomes your obligation to exercise faith and get well so you can be a better citizen of that society and show others how they can receive such benefits too.

Matthew said, "Let your light so shine before men that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven" (Mt. 5:16).

As long as you remain sick, you're not living up to your duty to society, and you glorify Satan more than God. God can receive glory only in healing and delivering people from the works of the devil—not in permitting the devil to have victory over those whom Christ died to liberate.

If every person would determine to devote their whole life to God and glorify Him completely, they would defeat Satan and refuse to be held in bondage any longer. They would be sustained by God and Christ, and Christ's work on the cross would become effective in the bodies of all.

Let no one say they're glorifying God while sick and defeated in life, when the works of the devil are being manifest in them instead of the works of God. Christ came to destroy the works of the devil and give life more abundantly. Those who don't permit Him to complete this work in them are causing Christ's work to be a failure in them (Jn. 10:10; 1 Jn. 3:8; Acts 10:38).

**5. SAINTS GLORIFY SATAN BY PERMITTING HIM TO ROB THEM OF THE WONDERFUL MATERIAL BENEFITS PROMISED THEM BY GOD.** If God has promised and provided material benefits for all people—particularly His own children who are the special subjects of His providence—then all believers can have these blessings.

If they don't receive them, it's not God's fault. It's the fault of the saints for permitting themselves to be defeated by Satan. God will give them the benefits He has promised and abundantly provided if they will study the Bible and acquire the faith and wisdom necessary to receive them.

The following passages prove that material blessings are promised every child of God:

- "He shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper" (Ps. 1:3)
- "Honour the Lord with thy substance, and with the first fruits of all thine increase: so shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine" (Prov. 3:9-10)
- "Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse, that there may be meat in mine house, and prove me now herewith, saith the Lord of hosts, if I will not open the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it" (Mal. 3:10-11)
- "But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you" (Mt. 6:33)
- "Give, and it shall be given you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again" (Lk. 6:38)
- "He that soweth bountifully shall also reap bountifully" (2 Cor. 9:6-10)
- "Beloved I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth" (3 Jn. 2)

### II. Saints Add to Satan's Glory by Advertising His Works and Magnifying His Power

**1. SAINTS ADD TO SATAN'S GLORY BY BEING LIVING TESTIMONIES OF THE POWER OF SATAN.** It may sound harsh to state the whole truth about this, but regardless of what anyone says, if sickness, sin, defeat, poverty, and failure are the works of the devil, then when these things are manifest in the lives of people who claim to be Christians, they glorify Satan more than God by bearing witness of these conditions in their lives.

If it's not God's will for His children to be in these conditions, then they're out of His will to remain satisfied enduring these curses.

How can anyone glorify God better while sick, sinful, and poverty-stricken than if they were healthy, happy, victorious, prosperous, and able to do the whole will of God according to Scripture?

If these conditions are the work of the devil, then whenever you're sick, sinful, and defeated in life, you're testifying for Satan and magnifying his power. If you had freedom from these curses, you'd be testifying to the goodness of God in delivering you from sin and sickness and in blessing you with all the good things of life.

**2. SAINTS ADD TO THE GLORY OF SATAN BY BEING HIS TOOLS AT TIMES.** The Christian life is so straight and narrow that we must be on guard at all times, lest we submit to satanic influences that will hinder Christ's work.

In Mt. 16:21-25, Christ rebuked Peter for allowing Satan to influence him to try and stop Jesus from going to the cross. Here we see clearly that a Christian can, in ignorance, yield to satanic influences for a moment. It's such an easy thing to do—a person can do it without realizing it. For that reason, we must learn all about our enemy and his tactics to wage successful warfare against him and always use all our time and talents for God's righteous cause.

Every time a believer participates in division, strife, malice, hatred, or wrongdoing of any kind, they're being used as a tool of demons. All fault-finding, fears, unbelief, worry, fretting, bitterness, pride, retaliation, sinful lusts, worldliness, foolishness, insincerity, hypocrisy, dishonesty, deceit, failure, uncleanness, doubts, formality, deadness, lack of spiritual power—whatever is contrary to holy living and sound doctrine—shows that the people involved are yielded to satanic powers to that degree.

Anyone who knowingly yields to these influences commits sin. Those who come to understand that these are manifestations of the wrong spirit in their life and conduct must quit all such acts or they will backslide entirely. These conditions cannot be encouraged in any life, or they'll cause a spiritual and moral breakdown and lead to other states that will damn the soul.

**3. SAINTS ADD TO SATAN'S GLORY WHEN THEY PROPAGATE FALSE DOCTRINES.** Such propagators of falsehood become living epistles of Satan, known and read by all.

Any doctrine that's in the least out of harmony with revealed truth should not be tolerated in the life of a child of God. When so-called "saints" become dishonest in refusing to accept plain truth—whether to further a party, a church creed, or some personal bias—they're adding to Satan's glory. They're rejecting truth and spreading falsehood.

Any dishonesty in accepting certain statements on a subject and twisting them to prove some church or personal theory—while disregarding other scriptures on the same subject—is satanic in origin and practice. Those who carry on such a program are adding to the glory of Satan.

### III. Saints Add to Satan's Glory by Failing God in Life and Work

**1.** Every excuse for unbelief and every argument that casts any reflection on God's infinite Fatherhood, love, and will for the best good of all creation is helping Satan keep people in unbelief and bondage to sin, sickness, poverty, and failure.

All accusations against God alleging that He wills for people to be sick, helpless, powerless, sinful, and unhappy add to Satan's glory. They spread falsehoods about God and His will toward humanity and hide the true will of God from people.

**2.** All failure to trust God in times of temptation, sickness, trial, and suffering—and all insinuations that God is responsible for these things—add to Satan's glory and rob God of the respect and true understanding He deserves.

All failure to fight the fight of faith and wage aggressive warfare on Satan simply adds to his glory and defeats God's plan for humanity. Failure to pray for lost souls, for your own family, your own church, your own business, and for the needs of others—all of this adds up to defeat for God's cause and results in lost souls, thereby glorifying Satan.

Failure to read and study God's Word so you can be a workman rightly dividing the Word of Truth helps in the destruction of eternal souls and therefore adds to satanic success.

**3.** Unconcern for lost souls, failure to live and walk in the Spirit and thus live victoriously over sin, disease, and failure in life, and disobedience to God in not earnestly coveting the best gifts and the endowment of power for service—all of this helps build up Satan's kingdom.

Lack of missionary vision regarding the needs of God's work as a whole destroys the highest efficiency of the child of God in their part in total warfare on evil spirits.

Until believers wake up to see their misconceptions about God and His Word and literally carry out what God really says, Satan will get more glory out of their lives than God will.

As long as Christians think and teach that it's God's will for them to be poor, weak, defeated, sick, sinful, and suffering needlessly, Satan will defeat them and rob God of the glory that would be His because of His blessings in the lives of His people.

If what we've stated is truth, then those who desire to please God should take these thoughts to heart and refuse to ever again give any glory to Satan.

You may well ask, "How can we always glorify God and not Satan?" There's only one answer: by walking in the light as God makes it clear through His Word. The main action you can take is to refuse to ever live in doubt and unbelief—and always carry through to complete victory those blessings that you ask of God.

## Hindrances to Answered Prayer

There will be hindrances to answered prayer, and there's no use trying to avoid this fact. Furthermore, hindrances to answered prayer are so well known that believers even manufacture hindrances of all kinds as excuses for their unbelief.

The hindrances to answered prayer are Satan, demons, fallen angels, doubt, unbelief, and false doctrines among Christians concerning God and answered prayer.

## How to Overcome Hindrances to Prayer

In Lesson Six, Point X, 11, we saw how to overcome Satan and demon powers. Review that material and doggedly carry out the instructions revealed in Scripture on this point.

Let it be settled forever: Satan and demons must be defeated if you want definite answers to prayer. You must strive to bring yourself to the point where you stop all doubting and unbelief. You must lay aside all falsehoods and get to know the truth, and the truth shall make you free (Jn. 8:31-32).

You will get to know truth if you follow the light as you receive it, and you will be made free accordingly. Above all, be careful that you have Scripture to prove every part of your faith and every detail of doctrine—then it will be truth that sets you free.

Correction of doubt, unbelief, and falsehood is entirely up to you.

## Ten Questions For Supplement Four. Do You Know:

- **That Jesus actually descended into Hell while His body was in the tomb?** This is the truth. In Mt. 12:40; Eph. 4:7-11 we read that Christ went "in the heart of the Earth" and "into the lower parts of the Earth" during the time His body was in the tomb. This is what David meant by the Holy Spirit in Ps. 16:10; Acts 2:25-27, "For thou wilt not leave my soul in Hell; neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption" in the grave. During this time in Hell, Christ preached to the spirits in prison (1 Pet. 3:19).

- **That 200 million horsemen will soon appear on the Earth to slay millions of men?** In the future Tribulation during the sixth trumpet judgment, four angels will be loosed that are now bound in the river Euphrates, and 200 million demon or spirit-horsemen will be loosed out of the bottomless pit. Each of these four angels will lead 50 million of these horsemen in a different direction to slay one-third of all men at a certain hour (Rev. 9:13-21). This will literally happen in the first 3½ years of Daniel's Seventieth Week, for the middle of the Week starts with the seventh trumpet (Rev. 11:15-18; 12:6, 14; 13:5). All these events take place after the rapture, as proven in Rev. 4:1, which says all the events of Rev. 4–22 "must be hereafter," that is, after the churches.

- **That the United States is not once definitely mentioned in prophecy?** The very absence of any clear, definite reference to America in prophecy is sufficient to prove this. It's true that some teach Isa. 18 refers to the United States, but this is not so, for it refers to a land "which is beyond the rivers of Ethiopia," as it plainly says. There would have to be a lot of dirt moved for the United States to be relocated to this land "beyond the rivers of Ethiopia." Other students of prophecy find the United States in the beast of Rev. 13:11-18, but this refers to a man and not a country, as is clear from the language of the passage and because he is called the "false prophet" who will be destroyed with the Antichrist at the Second Coming of Christ (Rev. 16:13-16; 19:20; 20:10). The first beast of Rev. 13 is a "man" according to verse 18, and in verse 11 the second beast is called "another," which, in the Greek, means another of the same kind. If the first is a man, the second beast must also be symbolic of a man. The language of the passages concerning the two beasts proves that individuals are referred to in Rev. 13. Still others say the United States is referred to in "the islands" mentioned in Scripture, but this cannot be proven. This is mere human theory, so we conclude that there is no direct reference to the United States in prophecy.

- **That you will know each other in both Heaven and Hell?** "For now we see through a glass darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known" (1 Cor. 13:12). We are told in 1 Cor. 15:25-29; Phil. 3:20-21 that every person will be resurrected with the same kind of body that was sown in the ground and that every person will have "his own body," so naturally if we know each other here, we shall know each other in Heaven or Hell. In Lk. 16:19-31, the rich man while in Hell knew Lazarus, and so will every other person know whomever they see whom they knew on Earth.

- **That one-third of the ships of the sea will soon be destroyed?** This will happen in the future tribulation when the second trumpet blows (Rev. 8:8-9).

- **That the future Tribulation will not be worldwide?** Nearly every plague that will be poured out in the Tribulation states that it will be over a fourth or a third part of the Earth only, thus showing that the trouble for which these plagues are sent will be only on part of the Earth. See Rev. 6:8; 8:7-12; 9:18; 16:10, 12. As we shall see, Antichrist will rule just a certain part of the Earth, and because he is the one causing the Tribulation, it will be only over the part of Earth which he rules.

- **That many people have already gone to Heaven and Hell?** Enoch (Gen. 5:24; Heb. 11:5); Elijah (2 Ki. 2:11-13); Paul (2 Cor. 12:1-7); John (Rev. 4:1); and many bodies of the Old Testament saints (Mt. 27:52-53); and all those that were not resurrected were captured from the devil in the underworld and taken to Heaven when Christ went back to Heaven (Eph. 4:7-11; Acts 1:11). Now when every righteous soul leaves the body at death, it goes to Heaven until the resurrection (2 Cor. 5:8; Phil. 1:21-23; Rev. 6:9-11). All the souls of the wicked that have ever died have gone to Hell and will continue to go there until after the Millennium, when those in Hell will be brought before God in judgment, and then they will be cast into the Lake of Fire (Lk. 16:19-31; Rev. 20:11-15).

- **That the Bible tells you what Satan looks like?** He is a most beautiful angel with an angelic body. All angels have bodies like men, as we saw in Lesson Four. Satan then looks like a human being bodily, but much more beautiful according to Ezek. 28:11-17. (See Lesson Six, Point X).

- **That the Bible tells you where Cain got his wife?** He married his sister, for "Adam called his wife's name Eve; because she was the mother of all living" (Gen. 3:20). In Gen. 5:4, we read that Adam "begat sons and daughters," and in Gen. 6:1-2 we read, "When men began to multiply on the face of the Earth, and daughters were born to them, That the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose." One old tradition says that Cain had a twin sister named Lulawa and that he married his sister. Be that as it may, we know that he had to marry a sister to get the race started, for all humanity came from Adam and Eve.

- **That the Bible tells you where Hell is located?** It is located in the lower parts or in the center of the Earth (Mt. 12:40; Eph. 4:7-11; Isa. 14:9).

supplements.title: Supplement 5
supplements.content:
# Supplement Five

## For Lessons Nine and Ten

In the beginning of PART II: God's Historical Dealings With Man, we studied the Dispensation of Innocence. We saw how God tested man in a state of innocence to discover whether he would remain true to his test of obedience.

We've seen that man failed, lost power over Satan and demons, and became a fallen, helpless creature set upon by the most subtle and unprincipled evil spirits in existence. Man lost control of the Earth and became subject to Satan.

We've also seen that this Fall was brought about by doubting God's Word, by adding to it, by contradicting it, by misinterpreting it, by temptation to transgress it, and by actual transgression of it. These were the steps of man's Fall, and he must take steps that are just the opposite of these in order to be redeemed from the fall.

The steps one must follow to be redeemed are:

### 1. Doubt Concerning God's Word

If you want healing, health, success, happiness, and prosperity from God through the gospel, you must first stop doubting the Word of God in its entirety. If man had never permitted himself to doubt in the first place—if he had warded off all suggestions of Satan that are contrary to God's Word—there wouldn't be the struggle that exists today to believe God without doubting.

The first and last lesson you must learn if you want anything from God is to doubt not. It is reported that Franklin D. Roosevelt said, "It never occurs to me to doubt God." Every person should maintain this attitude toward God at all times.

God has made it very plain that the absolute condition of answered prayer is: "Let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not: and it shall be given him. But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed. For let not that man think that he shall receive anything of the Lord. A double-minded man is unstable in all his ways" (Jas. 1:5-8).

The Moffat translation reads: "Let him ask God who gives to all men without question or reproach, and the gift will be his. Only, let him ask in faith, with never a doubt; for the doubtful man is like the surge of the sea whirled and swayed by the wind; that man need not imagine he will get anything from God, double-minded creature that he is, wavering at every turn."

The Weymouth translation reads: "Let him ask God for it, who gives with open hand to all men, and without upbraiding; and it will be given him. But let him ask in faith and have no doubts; for he who has doubts is like the surge of the sea, driven by the wind and tossed into spray. A person of that sort must not expect to receive anything from the Lord—such a one is a man of two minds, undecided in every step he takes."

What could be clearer than this? If this is God's condition of answered prayer, it will pay for everyone to make up his mind before he prays that he is going to cleave to what he decides to ask of God and fight it through until he gets an answer.

If children of God could learn this one secret, they would stop asking God for blessings until they thoroughly made up their minds that they want them badly enough to see their desires through to a complete answer. Then, following this procedure, their prayers will be answered.

But many times people change their minds when the answer doesn't come all at once. They decide that it isn't the will of God, or that it wouldn't have been best for them to have received an answer. These things should always be settled before prayer is made for something. The fact that an answer isn't forthcoming the same moment we ask is no proof that it isn't the will of God or that it might not have been best for us.

This habit of changing your mind mid-prayer is the most harmful procedure for a child of God to follow, but sad to say, it's one of the most common. A few failures like this will soon form a habit that causes failure in almost every prayer you pray.

Failures are so common in the lives of people concerning answered prayer that it's safe to say not one percent of all prayers are answered. But under the circumstances, even this is a high percentage. Definite requests are made to God by the millions, but few are answered.

This condition doesn't seem to bother the average praying man or woman. In fact, many believe the false idea that it's normal not to get an answer to prayer, except occasionally.

Such people should wake up and realize that if they can get an answer once in a while, they can get an answer every time they pray. If desperation and absolute necessity can force them to have faith enough for God to answer one time, they can force themselves to become earnest and have enough faith for everything they pray for at all times.

Why should there ever be a doubt in any mind if the simple, definite promises of God are found to cover the need? If God's Word does not promise what is asked, it's not necessary to have it, for Peter said: "According as his divine power hath given us all things that pertain to life and godliness . . . Whereby are given unto us exceeding great and precious promises: that by these ye might be partakers of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world through lust" (2 Pet. 1:3-4).

If absolute, unwavering faith is what God demands before He obligates Himself to answer, let everyone who asks see to it that he asks in faith, nothing wavering, and since he has the definite Word of God promising him what he asks, he will receive.

Since it was Satan (through the serpent) that raised the first doubt in man and made it second nature to doubt God, let everyone recognize that all doubts are of the devil and are contrary to the Word of God. Let him put a stop to all doubting by wholeheartedly resisting the devil, and the devil will flee from him (Jas. 4:7). All doubting is definite opposition to God and His Word, and when you're in such opposition to the One you expect an answer from, you cannot hope to receive it.

In order to not doubt God's Word, you must know it beyond question. This is the secret of knowing the truth, and it will make you free, as promised by Jesus (Jn. 8:31-32).

The way to know the Word of God is to take it for what it says and reject the interpretations of demons and men who try to change even one jot or tittle of it. All such changes are satanic in origin and propagation and must be rejected before doubt can be completely dispelled.

All doubts must be rooted out before prayer can be answered, and prayer must be answered before the benefits of the promises can be fully realized. The way to answered prayer then becomes clear, and it's the duty of each individual to see to it that he masters all doubt and demons and cooperates with God's Word with all his heart.

### 2. Addition to and Misquoting God's Word

Until all doubt is cleansed from your life, there will be some addition to and misquoting of God's Word, because this is part of doubting God. As long as doubt is tolerated and indulged in, it will be easy to reason about the Word of God and excuse yourself for doubt and unbelief.

The habit of praying people is to question, wonder, reason, argue, and lay blame upon God when prayer isn't immediately answered. They seldom stop to analyze the truth, realizing that they are to blame for yielding to satanic powers which cause a person to walk in doubt and unbelief.

If immediate answers to all prayers are your goal, you must never add one thing to God's Word or take one thing from it. You must learn to take every statement of God as it is written and practice it to the letter.

If there is any occasion to argue or contend about any part of it, you must take the side of God and maintain faith in it just as it is, making no apology for faith in it. You must never give in to any excuse for unbelief and never doubt one thing that is plainly written.

Your first and last contention should be that it is clear, and that it means what it says and says what it means. You must never excuse yourself or anyone from absolute conformity to it just as it is written, whether it harmonizes with your own theories or those of your church.

Dishonesty and handling the Word of God deceitfully will destroy more faith in one day than can be built up in a lifetime. You must never be guilty of trying to evade truth or what is plainly evident in the Scriptures themselves.

A frank admission of what is plainly written and a confession of faith in it will do more to build up faith than all questioning and doubting programs of the devil and natural reason.

### 3. Contradiction of God's Word

Not only must you never doubt, add to, or take from God's Word, but if you want God to honor you enough to answer prayer, you must never try to make God a liar by contradicting the Word of God.

There is nothing to be gained by maintaining faith in man's interpretations of the Bible at the expense of maintaining faith in God and what He plainly says. If God makes a statement, it should be final, and no argument is necessary—for such arguing is merely trying to evade plain truth.

It's not possible to misunderstand God or His Word if you will put forth the least effort to accept what He says just as He said it. You can afford to contradict theories of men concerning God's Word, but you cannot afford to contradict what is plainly written.

When God makes a statement, it should be accepted at face value. There should never be one word uttered that will in any way throw a reflection upon God or His Word, or make Him seem false in any detail of His statements.

If God promises certain things to men upon certain conditions, then be strong in faith, knowing that God is right and anything to the contrary is wrong. It's only by faithfully taking God at His Word in all that He says that faith will grow and become unwavering.

When you achieve unwavering faith in what God says, you will receive unwavering answers to your prayers. But when a person is double-minded and changes his mind every few minutes about what he has asked of God, how can God decide whether the man really wants it or not? Under the circumstances, God does not give it to him.

When you doubt, you contradict God's Word that promises you what you have prayed for, and such contradiction cuts you off from the answer.

Our motto should be: **"The Bible States It; I Believe It; That Settles It."**

### 4. Misinterpretation of God's Word

The next step in retracing our steps from the fall is to stop misinterpreting God's Word. It's impossible to misinterpret what is written if you will take at face value what is plainly written.

Every person that has intelligence enough to read what God says has intelligence enough to believe what He says, and that's all that is necessary. The very fact that a person can see what it says well enough to accept it or make a change to it as he pleases proves that he can understand what it plainly says.

If what he reads is not clear, he couldn't understand it well enough to change it. Thus, if all can understand what is plainly written well enough to change it as they please, it's clear that all can, if they choose, believe it and leave it as it is.

A solid, unshakable faith will be created if God's Word will be taken just as it is. Faith will never have to change because the Word itself will never change.

### 5. Temptation to Transgress God's Word

Temptation to transgress the Word of God must be rejected with a firm and definite refusal. You must resist the temptation to transgress God's Word concerning open sin, or you become a sinner and incur the death penalty.

You must likewise refuse temptation to doubt the promises of God if you want answers to prayer. You cannot transgress God's Word concerning sin and still be exempt from the death penalty. Neither can you transgress the law of faith and still get from God those things for which you have prayed.

The law of faith says:
- "All things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive" (Mt. 21:21-22)
- "All things are possible to him that believeth" (Mk. 9:23)
- "What things soever ye desire when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them" (Mk. 11:22-24)
- "Ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you" (Jn. 15:7)
- "let him ask in faith, nothing wavering" (Jas. 1:5-8)

There is no possible way to transgress the law of faith and get an answer. Jesus said, "heaven and earth shall pass away [be changed] but my word shall not pass away [be changed]."

Think of it like this: you might as well shoot peas at a battleship hoping to sink it as to expect an answer when the law of faith is willfully transgressed.

When you are tempted to transgress, such temptation is a warning signal that you are being attacked by demon spirits who want you to disobey God. No sin has yet been committed until actual transgression, so a person under such temptation must immediately become aggressive against this temptation and refuse all further parley. It must be no, now and forever! When temptation is resisted, faith grows, and final victory is but a matter of time.

You may be tempted in every conceivable way to break the law of faith or to transgress some part of God's Word. Don't do it. It will not pay.

God is faithful, and He will stand by you and by His promises, so never doubt that He will do what He says. James said: "Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you. Cleanse your hands, ye sinners; and purify your hearts, ye double minded" (Jas. 4:7-8).

Paul advised believers: "Hold fast the profession of our faith without wavering, for he is faithful that promised . . . Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompense of reward. For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise . . . Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him" (Heb. 10:23, 35-39).

This last passage teaches that it's God's pleasure to see His children exercise faith in spite of the devil, demons, and all opposition. He glories in their spunk when they resist the devil, and He will back them up in their fight.

"Without faith it is impossible to please him" (Heb. 11:6). It is written of Enoch that he pleased God by believing that God would translate him (Heb. 11:5).

Who ever heard of a man being translated in Enoch's day? Yet he believed the Word of God that he was going to be translated, and such faith pleased God. God gets great pleasure out of men daring to believe that anything is possible.

Enoch did not argue, doubt, question, and call God a liar when God told him that He was going to translate him. He believed that it would be as God said, and it did happen. This is faith.

The disciples were rebuked on several occasions when they doubted. When they worried about food and clothing, Jesus said: "If God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little faith?" (Mt. 6:25-34).

When Peter dared to walk on water and did so until he doubted, Jesus said: "O ye of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?" (Mt. 14:28-33).

When the disciples questioned Him about having bread to eat, Jesus said: "O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?" (Mt. 16:5-10).

And so the story continues. All wavering, questioning, worrying, fretting, complaining, discouragement, fear, or reasoning about how to get the benefits of life or the fulfillment of God's promises is pure unbelief. Such a program never did produce desired results, so why not cleanse our lives of all unbelief and dare believe God?

If such doubting is displeasing to God, and if this is the reason why prayers aren't heard, then why continue to deliberately and willfully yield to such temptations, thereby committing sin against God and robbing ourselves of our rights in the gospel?

We know we won't get anything by this method, so what have we got to lose in trying another way? It certainly couldn't produce more failures than the method we are currently using.

There were only two times when Christ marveled at men. The first time He marveled because a man had such great faith. The centurion said: "Speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed . . . When Jesus heard it, he marveled, and said unto them that followed, Verily, I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel . . . And Jesus said unto the centurion, Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour" (Mt. 8:5-13).

The second time Jesus marveled was because of the unbelief of His own hometown people (Mk. 6:1-6). God marvels at both things—great faith and unbelief.

Evidently, the greatest mystery to Him is that men do not believe Him after having more than His Word, as the centurion did. We have His promises by the hundreds, and many hundreds of healings, answers to prayer, miracles, and supernatural manifestations in Scripture proving to man that God will do what He has promised, and yet it seems so hard for the average person to believe.

It's no wonder that God refuses to answer when men refuse to ask in faith, nothing wavering. If man does not want the benefits of the promises, then let him go without them. This is happening in the lives of multitudes, but it can be different with every person if he chooses to come out of this one failure in life and believe God.

### 6. Transgression of God's Word

For every sin committed, there is a penalty incurred. This is true of man's laws and it's also true of God's laws. Laws without penalties would be no laws at all. They would merely be advice, and not even good advice at that, for they would not offer any incentive for man to accept them.

Think of it like this: traffic laws without consequences would turn every intersection into chaos—the rules only have meaning because breaking them matters.

When a person yields to temptation to break the law of faith or any other law of God, he incurs the penalty of the broken law. There are some sins that demand the death penalty, such as those in Gal. 5:19-21; Rom. 1:29-32; 1 Cor. 6:9-11; Mk. 7:19-21.

The penalty for breaking the law of faith is failing to receive those things which man would receive if he exercised faith instead of unbelief. No man can break this law and still get results or answers to his prayers.

When prayers are not answered for one who is in Christ, let it become a settled fact with him that he is breaking the law of faith. All the devils in Hell or any power on Earth cannot keep God from honoring faith if it is properly exercised.

It matters not what has been asked of God—He will answer upon the sole condition of faith in His promises. Jesus said: "If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you" (Jn. 15:7).

This is final and irrefutable. It's the all-inclusive condition of answered prayer. Make no mistake about it—this is the cause of unanswered prayer, but it can easily be corrected.

All you have to do is get into Christ and meditate on the Word of God until your heart becomes full of His promises, and then ask in faith, nothing wavering, and it shall be done.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Five. Do You Know:

**1. That three separate and distinct persons of the Godhead are now carrying out God's plan of creation and redemption of all things?**

The Bible is very clear that there are three separate and distinct persons in the Godhead—that they all existed as separate beings from all eternity, that they were all capable of doing the same things, and that they all took separate parts in the creation and redemption of all things. All three had a part in the drafting of God's plan, as we saw in Lesson Five, Point II.

God's plan is revealed in three distinct parts and is now being carried out by three distinct persons.

One person, now called the Father because He had a Son, took the first or headship part and commanded what was to be done. This is revealed in both Testaments. David said: "thou art exalted as the head of all" (1 Chron. 29:11). Paul said of saints: "ye are Christ's; and Christ's is God's" (1 Cor. 3:23). He also said: "I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the head of the woman is the man; and the head of Christ is God" (1 Cor. 11:3). This proves that Christ and God the Father are two distinct persons as much as man and woman are.

Another person, now called the Son because he had a Father, took the second or representative part in the creation and redemption of all things. God the Father created and redeemed everything by Jesus Christ. We are told that: "All things were made by him . . . the world was made by him . . . God created all things by Jesus Christ . . . by him all things were created . . . by whom also he [the Father] made [planned] the worlds" (ages, Jn. 1:3, 10; Eph. 3:9; Col. 1:15-18; Heb. 1:1-3). Then too, it is by Jesus Christ that God redeems the world to Himself (Jn. 3:16; 17:4; 2 Cor. 5:17-21; 1 Pet. 2:24).

Still another person, now called the Holy Spirit to distinguish Him from the Father and from the Son, took the third part of direct operation in the creation and redemption of all things. It was the Holy Spirit that brooded upon the face of the deep and operated at the command of God in restoring the Earth to a habitable state (Gen. 1:21; Job 33:4). It was the Holy Spirit that came upon Mary and by whom she conceived the Son of God (Lk. 1:35). It was the Holy Spirit that has operated in the lives of all men of all ages, bringing about the work of redemption in their lives and giving them power to carry out the will of God (Acts 3:21; 2 Pet. 1:21; Jn. 3:3-5; 14:16-17, 26; 15:26; 16:7-15; Acts 10:38).

We can say that God the Father redeems by the sacrifice of Christ and through the direct power of the Holy Spirit.

**2. That demons and Satan were not created sinful?**

All things were created perfect and sinless in the beginning of their creation. Concerning Lucifer, it is stated in Ezek. 28:11-17: "Thou wast perfect in thy ways from the day that thou wast created, till iniquity was found in thee."

Concerning the angels that fell, it is stated that they "sinned" and "left their first estate" (2 Pet. 2:4; Jude 6-7). Concerning demons, it is also clear that they sinned, for they already know their punishment for what they have done (Mt. 8:29; Lk. 8:31).

Not one Scripture says that God ever created anything sinful or imperfect. In Eccl. 3:11 we read: "He made everything beautiful in his time," and anything contrary to this idea would be out of harmony with God.

**3. That people will be saved after the rapture of the Church?**

This is clearly stated in Acts 2:16-21, where we read of people being saved and receiving the Holy Spirit in their lives during the Tribulation. See also Rev. 7:9-17; 12:17; 15:2-4; 20:4-6, where great multitudes of people will be saved and come out of the great tribulation.

That the Church is raptured before the Tribulation will be proven later.

**4. That there have been only two worldwide dictators since the creation of the earth?**

The first one was Lucifer, as proven in Lessons Six and Seven. The second one was Adam, as proven in Lessons Eight and Nine.

There will not be another worldwide dictator until Christ comes to reign on the Earth forever. This we shall see in Lesson Forty-eight.

**5. That Hell has literal fire and not spiritual fire?**

One reading of the following passages will prove this:
- "I am tormented in this flame" (Lk. 16:19-31)
- "Into the fire that never shall be quenched" (Mt. 25:41, 46; Mk. 9:42-50; Isa. 66:22-24)
- "He shall be tormented with fire and brimstone . . . And the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever" (Rev. 14:9-11; 19:20; 20:10)

The idea of spiritual fire could never be understood in such plain literal passages that speak of fire, smoke, and brimstone. There is no statement in Scripture that even hints of spiritual fire that will torment people, so to even question the reality and literalness of Hell is showing opposition to the Bible.

**6. That the soul and spirit or the inner man is immortal?**

This is definitely stated in 1 Pet. 3:4: "But let it be the hidden man of the heart in that which is not corruptible, even the ornament of a quiet spirit." If the spirit is not corruptible, then it is immortal.

**7. The righteous are in a conscious state of bliss between death and resurrection?**

This was true of Lazarus and all the Old Testament saints who were held captive in the lower parts of the Earth before Christ captured them and led them to Heaven when He ascended on High (Lk. 16:19-31; Mt. 12:40; Eph. 4:7-11; Heb. 2:14-15).

This was true of Moses who appeared with Christ on the mount while his body was in corruption in Moab (Mt. 17:3; Deut. 34:5-6; Jude 9).

This will be true of the future martyrs of the Tribulation (Rev. 6:9-11). And this will be true of all who die in Christ now and go to Heaven until the resurrection (2 Cor. 5:8; Phil. 1:21-23; 1 Thess. 4:16).

**8. That the wicked are in a conscious state of torment between death and resurrection?**

This is proven by the rich man in Hell in torment after death (Lk. 16:19-31). This is also proven by the fact that there are conversations and other manifestations of consciousness in Hell (Ezek. 32:21; Isa. 14:9; 2 Sam. 22:6; Ps. 55:15; 116:3).

**9. That the soul does not sleep in the grave after death?**

This is proven by the Scriptures in the two points mentioned above. All passages used to teach soul-sleep really refer to the body, which alone sees corruption and goes to the grave.

This is clear from an examination of all passages that speak of the dead knowing nothing in the grave. According to Jas. 2:26, it is the body only that dies, and therefore this is the only thing that is put in the grave. The souls go to two different places where they are conscious after death, as proven in Points 7 and 8 above.

**10. That the dead cannot communicate with the living after death?**

This is clear from what was said of the rich man in Hell, who was told that the dead cannot go back on Earth to warn the living (Lk. 16:19-31).

It is true that Moses appeared with Christ on the mount, but this was a divine plan to bear witness to the sonship of Christ and to show witnesses what the coming kingdom was to be like (Mt. 16:28–17:8). There is not one authentic case of the dead communicating with the living to teach that men in general who die can so communicate.

supplements.title: Supplement 6
supplements.content:
# Supplement Six

## For Lessons Eleven and Twelve

In Lesson Eleven, we explored the Dispensation of Conscience — all of God's dealings with humanity for 1,656 years, right up until Noah's time. We looked at how both humans and angels failed during this period.

Here's what we discovered about Satan's strategy: his goal was to prevent the seed of the woman — Christ — from ever entering the world. Why? Because if he could stop Christ from coming, he could avoid his own doom and keep control of the Earth indefinitely.

But here's the wonderful news: Satan was defeated in his plan for that age and every age that followed! The Messiah did come, and He made complete provision for every person to have power over all satanic forces. Because of Christ, you can claim God's benefits for this life and for eternity.

In Lesson Twelve, we tackled some tough questions: Why aren't God's plans for human flourishing fully realized? Why do we see such widespread failure, poverty, sickness, and unhappiness on Earth — and why shouldn't these conditions exist?

Here's what we discovered: It is not — and could never be — God's will for you to suffer misery and want just so you'll stay humble enough to be saved. That doesn't have to be your lot in life! The gospel enables you to overcome every obstacle and live saved, prosperous, and happy in Christ.

We also saw that many doctrines being taught in modern churches are simply false and unscriptural. You are under a moral obligation to have faith in God and never doubt anything you ask of Him.

Think about it this way: You can't secure the best for yourself or your fellow man while living in defeat and failure. You don't endure these things for God's sake or Christ's sake — you endure them because of your own failures and because of false teachings.

It's almost unpardonable to be satisfied with defeat when you have the power to do otherwise! That's why we need to understand the laws of prosperity — the principles through which all people can have guaranteed success if they'll obey them.

## LAWS OF PROSPERITY

### 1. Believe It's God's Will for You to Prosper

**THE FIRST LAW OF PROSPERITY IS TO BELIEVE THAT IT IS GOD'S WILL FOR YOU TO PROSPER AND THAT YOU ARE IN HIS WILL.**

Contrary to what many people think, God wants you to be prosperous! If that's true — and it is — then God will see to it that you prosper if you'll learn these laws and cooperate with Him in all things.

Ask yourself: Why would God want His own children to lack what they need while giving good things to those who rebel against Him? That simply doesn't make sense.

Until Christians wake up, believe the whole Bible, and demand that their leaders teach the whole truth, we won't see the prosperity, happiness, and success that should mark every believer's life.

God has created an abundance of everything people need — there's enough for everyone. He laid down certain laws of life and prosperity, and all He asks is that we obey them. These laws are plainly revealed in Scripture, given for all to follow, and those who follow them will surely reap the promised benefits.

Look back at the promises we studied in Lesson Ten, Point III. You can't read all those promises without being convinced that it's God's highest will to bless His children with prosperity and everything they need and want right here and now.

Let this be firmly settled in your heart: you are in harmony with God's will when you desire to prosper, be healthy, and have victory over sin and failure.

### 2. Make God Your Partner

**THE SECOND LAW TO FOLLOW IS TO MAKE GOD YOUR PARTNER IN LIFE.**

Work with Him and obey His laws of prosperity. Make Him the Senior Partner in your business. Give Him the seat at the head of the table in all your conferences.

Recognize Him in everything you do. Stay in touch with Him about His guidance in every business deal. Be aware of His presence with you always.

Talk to Him about your problems as you would talk to your best friend. Turn all your problems over to Him. Don't make a move without feeling sure it's God's will and for everyone's best interest.

Think of it like this: If you have a brilliant, successful business partner who knows everything and can handle any problem, wouldn't you want their input on every decision? That's what God offers to be for you.

If you give all your hard problems to God; if you simply stop fretting and worrying and trust Him with all your heart to work things out for His glory and your good — He will do it.

But here's the key: After turning things over to God, don't interfere and spoil things! Stay out of them until God works them out. Don't be impatient or unbelieving.

If God is your Senior Partner and He's running your business, trust Him — no one could operate it better. Since you believe God can handle your problems better than you can, let Him do it. He will remove obstacles and work for your good.

Believe His Word: "all things work together for good to them that love the Lord, to them who are called according to his purpose" (Rom. 8:28). Since God cannot fail, you cannot fail if you keep all things committed to Him.

God never meant for His children to become failures. Think about it: a failing, sinful, rebellious child brings disgrace upon his parents. Do you think God is any different? Do you think He'd be glorified by His children's failures or derive any pleasure from them? Would He want the worst for His children instead of the best?

No! Jesus taught us that God loves us more than we love our own children (Mt. 7:7-11). We are the only ones holding back the floodgates of God's abundant blessings.

As long as we believe God is a hard, unloving Father, we can't hope to receive these benefits. The truth is that nothing makes God happier than to see His people blessed, happy, and successful in everything they do.

Remember: His children are not rebels against Him — rebels are children of the devil (Jn. 8:44; Eph. 2:1-3; 1 Jn. 3:8-10). Real children of God won't get into sinful business, so any legitimate, worthwhile business is something God will prosper.

Making God your partner means full surrender of your life to Him, obedience to His Word in all things, and constant dependence on God to know His will and ways. Without God's approval and help, you're already headed for failure. But with Him guiding your every step, you cannot fail.

Make sure your plans, ambitions, and undertakings all harmonize with God's highest will, then go ahead fearing nothing. If you want to overcome what's been holding you back, start right now by taking God into your life and obeying Him. He will lead you into prosperity.

It's only when you stop trying to do everything on your own and quit leaving God out of your affairs that you'll begin to experience Him. That's when His life, wisdom, and power will begin showing up in your life.

Christ came to give life and give it more abundantly (Jn. 10:10). There's no limitation in God unless you limit Him by unbelief and failure to claim the promised benefits.

### 3. Get Into Your Right Lifework

**THE THIRD LAW OF PROSPERITY IS TO GET INTO THE LIFEWORK THAT YOU FEEL GOD WOULD HAVE YOU IN OR FOR WHICH YOU ARE BEST SUITED.**

God has a lifework for you, and you can be extremely happy and successful if you find that work and make God your lifelong partner.

If your lifework isn't clear to you, get this settled once and forever. At minimum, you can know what your present work is at all times. If there comes a time to change, you'll move in God's will as you've been doing. As you go along, God will guide you and open doors of opportunity.

Here's something to remember: You are capable of filling your own place in life and doing your work better than anyone else can. Put your whole life into your work, realizing you labor for God and others.

What matters to God is whether you work honestly and faithfully at your calling. That's all He asks, and He'll reward you accordingly.

Real happiness lies in liking what you're called to do, not in doing something solely because you think you'd like it. You can become very unhappy if you let yourself think you're in the wrong lifework. Thousands are actually in the right work but are unhappy because they yearn for something else.

When plans don't materialize or doors close in one occupation, look to God — He will open doors in another.

Become content with your lot and make a success of what you're currently doing. Then you'll be capable of success in other areas too. But if you never succeed at anything and are always dissatisfied with every kind of work, it's not the work that's wrong — it's you.

You must get hold of yourself and conquer that restless, shiftless spirit. God will help you become stable, contented, successful, and happy in His will in any honest work.

Ask God to guide you into your lifework and His best plan for you. Talk things over with Him and wait for His guidance, which may come in ways you don't at first understand.

If you're in your right work, seek God to open up even greater fields of service. If you're in the wrong work, much prayer will open the right door. Stay constantly in tune with God and praise Him for His help. Thank Him for the work you already have.

Act as if God is guiding you. Be happy and contented in your work, and you will be a success.

### 4. Have Faith in God

**THE FOURTH LAW OF PROSPERITY IS FAITH IN GOD.**

You must believe that God loves you and that He is with you, giving you strength to overcome all hindrances to your happiness and success.

Every person who has made a success in life has had to overcome opposition to their progress. The stories of successful people are full of how they overcame obstacles and finally succeeded. These challenges continue through life, even in success and happiness.

It's in life's struggles that you need faith in God. All your obstacles are small — they shrink to nothingness when compared to God's promises and the faith they create.

When your troubles seem like giants, steal away in secret and pray. Get a good night's rest. Read God's promises, have faith in Him, and watch those troubles become very small.

If problems persist, get your mind off them. Turn them over to God, forget them for the time being, and you'll find He takes care of them better than you ever could.

We're told: "Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass" (Ps. 37:5). This law of success works without fail because God's Word cannot fail. The more you do this and the less you think about hindrances, the more you'll overcome them.

God is your Father if you've surrendered to Him. He can be trusted. He'll keep His promises better than any earthly friend.

Get His promises in you and abide in Him, then "ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you" (Jn. 15:7). What a blessing to know that God is with you and by faith you can do all things and get what you want!

Your enemies cannot triumph. Your problems cannot defeat you. God cannot fail.

The minute you're tempted to worry about some problem, turn it over to God. He sees further than you can and works out things invisible to you.

Throw your worries away! Become reckless in faith and confidence in God. Accept what comes with peace and thankfulness. "This is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith" (1 Jn. 5:1-4).

### 5. Be Unafraid to Launch Out

**THE FIFTH LAW OF PROSPERITY IS TO BE UNAFRAID IN LAUNCHING OUT INTO NEW VENTURES AND TO MAKE THE BEST OF YOUR OPPORTUNITIES THAT COME.**

Many people could have been prosperous if they hadn't been afraid to seize opportunities that came to them.

Have no fear! If you've been praying for things that would bring prosperity, God is sure to open many doors. You must not be afraid to enter them under His guidance.

Have confidence in yourself and in God that He's leading you and you cannot fail.

When going into new ventures, be led by God as to how far to go and how much to invest. Don't go into debt beyond your ability to pay. Don't become reckless or act unwisely.

Be led by God and go as far as you feel He wants you to go — as far as common sense dictates. Don't get your eyes on sudden riches and overstep yourself.

It's similar to climbing a mountain: you can always climb higher, and a steady, healthy ascent with secure footing is better than racing up recklessly and tumbling down.

You can always grow, and steady, healthy growth in business with security is better than blowing a bubble beyond the bursting point. Act sensibly and wisely and you will succeed.

Let the law of increase work for you normally. Stay within your ability to take care of business and build on a solid foundation that will last.

### 6. Follow Biblical Business Principles

**THE SIXTH LAW OF PROSPERITY IS TO FOLLOW CERTAIN BUSINESS PRINCIPLES TAUGHT IN SCRIPTURE.**

Paul laid down some powerful business principles: "Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good. Be kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love; in honour preferring one another; not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord; rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer; distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality; bless them which persecute you; bless, and curse not; rejoice with them that do rejoice, and weep with them that weep . . . Be not wise in your own conceits. Recompense to no man evil for evil. Provide things honest in the sight of all men . . . Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good" (Rom. 12:9-21).

If you obey these principles in business, you cannot help becoming happy, prosperous, and successful in all you undertake.

Paul also taught: "That ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and work with your own hands, as we commanded you: that ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and that ye may have lack of nothing" (1 Thess. 4:11-12).

And Solomon said: "Seest thou a man diligent in business? He shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before mean [obscure, or unknown] men" (Prov. 22:29).

These passages teach that you should constantly work at making a success. Don't let grass grow under your feet! Use every legitimate means and don't neglect one opportunity to reach your goal.

This may mean self-sacrifice at first. It will mean many hours of hard work, much prayer for guidance, being honest with all people, and living to serve others through your business.

Some people don't care to succeed enough to obey these laws. They're naturally shiftless, careless, indifferent, and unconcerned about their responsibilities. They won't put forth the least effort beyond what they feel like doing.

They're like sinners who know they're lost and doomed to Hell but don't care. Or like sick people who know how to get well but won't make the effort to meet the conditions.

If sinners really cared about going to Hell, not one would neglect salvation for a minute. Likewise, some would succeed if they cared enough to put forth their best effort.

If you don't put forth proper effort to prosper, don't expect God to bless you with prosperity.

### 7. Obey the Golden Rule

**THE SEVENTH LAW OF SUCCESS IS TO OBEY THE GOLDEN RULE.**

This means you'll always do unto others as you would have them do unto you.

If you do this, you'll never take advantage of anyone to prosper. You'll always be fair and square in all business dealings, giving customers value for money received.

You'll never skimp in making products or cheat in selling them. You'll never misrepresent any item and never sell a product you wouldn't use yourself.

You'll never be dishonest but will always tell the truth. You'll never be selfish. You'll always be friendly, courteous, helpful, happy, and committed to serving your fellow man and pleasing God.

You'll always let customers know you appreciate their business and show them how you seek to serve them.

You must be a real human being and make people feel they'd like to be around you. You can be that kind of person!

No matter your age, appearance, education, or position — you can be real and attractive in personality. If you let Christ live in and through you, He will free you from dullness and fill your life with charm and influence.

Power and radiance come from a change of heart and from Christ living in you. Regardless of age, you can have charm, attractiveness, and influence. These qualities can't be put on in a beauty parlor — they must come from the inward person.

It's fine to be as attractive as you can be outwardly, and this will help. But let the greatest attraction be "the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, even the ornament of a meek and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price" (1 Pet. 3:1-4).

### 8. Seek First the Kingdom of God

**ONE UNFAILING LAW OF PROSPERITY IS TO "SEEK YE FIRST THE KINGDOM OF GOD, AND HIS RIGHTEOUSNESS."**

If we do this, we're promised: "and all these things [blessings of life] shall be added unto you" (Mt. 6:33).

This means putting the interests of God's kingdom and others before our own. We must recognize that a person's life doesn't consist of the abundance of things possessed (Lk. 12:15).

Once we learn this lesson, we can easily be free from covetousness, which makes us live only for self.

Jesus said: "He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it" (Mt. 10:39).

In other words, the self-centered person who holds onto life's things only to please himself will lose the reality of true living. He'll be banished from eternal society, where everyone — like God — consecrates themselves to what's best for all.

But if we give up selfish living and seek first God's kingdom and righteousness, we'll receive "an hundredfold" in this life and in the life to come, everlasting life. Christ Himself assures us of this in Mt. 19:27-30.

Giving up self-interests for God's kingdom means consecrating yourself to live for the good of all people and the betterment of society. If everyone lived for everyone's best good, we'd have heaven on Earth!

Paul said: "For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself . . . For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's" (Rom. 14:7-11).

### 9. Give to Others

**ONE LAW OF PROSPERITY IS THAT OF GIVING TO OTHERS.**

Generally, the person who always wants to know what they'll get out of everything makes a failure and loses what they already have. They lose friends and become an outcast from society. No one wants to be around them, and they go through life alone.

But if you start with the idea of giving instead of getting, you'll succeed.

Give service to people in your business and you'll prosper. Bless others and seek God and His righteousness, and all the blessings you need will be added to you.

If there's one thing you can succeed at in life, it's giving. You can always give, and if you will, you'll have more to give. This is a law that never fails.

Jesus said: "Give, and it shall be given you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom" (Lk. 6:38).

People may temporarily hinder you from making money, but they can't hinder you from giving. No one wants to stop you from being generous — you become a hero when you give! You're wonderful to everybody as long as you're generous.

Something wonderful happens when you give. Begin by giving yourself to God and to others in service.

Give your good will, your wishes, your time, your talents, your life, your all — and you'll begin receiving from both God and the people you bless.

Occasionally you may find someone who won't give back, but for each one who doesn't, many others will give to you in appreciation. You should give your all to serving God and others not because you expect something in return, but because it's right and because it's your nature. The giving back will happen naturally.

You'll have many friends pulling for you to succeed in anything you undertake. They'll put forth every effort to help you. One will show you how to make more money. Another will open a door of blessing here, another there. Others will give you things that help in times of need.

As you're prospered materially and spiritually, give out to help others, and your supply will never be exhausted. "Give and it shall be given you" is both a divine law and a divine promise.

The Christian will prosper, and as you do, give yourself to hospitality, remembering that "Love worketh no ill to his neighbor: therefore, love is the fulfilling of the law" (Rom. 12:13; 13:10).

Paul said: "He that soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he that soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully . . . God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work: (As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor; his righteousness remaineth forever. Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;) Being enriched in everything to all bountifulness, which causeth through us thanksgiving to God. For the ministration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God" (2 Cor. 9:6-12).

### 10. Honor the Lord with Your Substance

**THE LAST LAW OF PROSPERITY WE WILL MENTION IS TO "HONOUR THE LORD WITH THY SUBSTANCE, AND WITH THE FIRSTFRUITS OF ALL THINE INCREASE"** (Prov. 3:6-12).

If we do this, we have the promise: "So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine."

We also have the promise in Mal. 3:10-11 that if we honor God and pay our tithes, He will open the windows of Heaven, pour out blessings we won't be able to receive, rebuke the devourer, and bless the works of our hands.

In this Malachi passage, we have an accusation by God that He was being robbed; a command to bring all the tithes into the storehouse; and a challenge by God to prove Him and see if He wouldn't cause prosperity.

If you pay your tithes — ten percent of all your income, wages, increase, or profit — to the Lord, and keep accurate records so there are no mistakes, God is under obligation to meet His challenge.

Mr. Kerr of the Kerr-Mason fruit jar fame started tithing when he was in debt. He had a mortgage on his home and was worried about his finances. Within three months of starting to tithe, unexpected blessings came in the form of a fruit jar patent.

As he put it: "God opened my eyes to see His love and faithfulness to His promises." That same year, with $100 and strong faith in God's tithing promises from Mal. 3:10, he organized a corporation that has grown beyond bounds.

I've never seen or heard of anyone who hasn't been blessed when they've faithfully tithed with faith in God's promises. There are no testimonies I know of about rich people starting to tithe, but there are many testimonies of people who began tithing when poor and are now rich.

Here are a few examples:

> Mr. Crowell, founder of the Quaker Oats Company.

> Mr. Colgate, founder of Colgate soaps, perfumes and powders.

> Mr. Proctor of the Ivory Soap Company.

> A. A. Hyde of "Mentholatum" fame.

> Henry Delaney of Resinol Ointment renown.

> Mr. Matthias Baldwin, founder of the Baldwin Locomotive Industry.

> R. G. LeTourneau, maker of large earth-moving equipment.

No one can practice these laws of prosperity without becoming prosperous. You can't truly give yourself to God and others to be a blessing in business without reaping the rich rewards promised in these laws.

If you want to prosper, set your own house in order and faithfully follow these laws. Soon God will lead you into blessings you never thought possible.

Apply yourself strictly to these laws and principles, and watch things happen that will bless you more than you're blessing others.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Six
### Do You Know:

- That the so-called appearance of Samuel from the dead in 1 Sam. 28 was a demonic spirit imitating Samuel to deceive Saul? This is plainly stated in 1 Chron. 10:13-14.

- That the Battle of Armageddon is not to be an ordinary battle between two sets of nations? This battle will be one between the armies of Heaven under Christ and the armies of the Earth under Antichrist as proven in Zech. 14; Joel 3; Rev. 19:11-21.

- That all saints, dead or alive, will soon be taken to Heaven bodily to be forever with the Lord? This is expressly stated in 1 Thess. 4:13-16; 1 Cor. 15:23, 51-58; Phil. 3:20-21; Jn. 14:1-3; Col. 3:4.

- That Russia will not fulfill prophecies Bible students say she will? Bible students in general teach that Russia is the king of the north of Dan. 11 and that Russia will be the head of the nations of Ezek. 38; 39 who will come against Christ at Armageddon. Neither one of these doctrines is true. The king of the north of Dan. 11 is the Antichrist from Syria, and the king of the south of this same chapter is Egypt, two of the four divisions of the Grecian Empire of Dan. 8:20-23, who will fight against each other "at the time of the end." The Antichrist will come from Syria and will overthrow Egypt and many other countries, fulfilling Dan. 11:35-45. In Dan. 11:44, after Antichrist conquers these many countries and gets power over the ten kingdoms that will yet be formed within the old Roman Empire of Dan. 7:23-24; Rev. 17:8-17, we read of tidings out of the north and out of the east that will trouble him, and of his going forth with great fury to "utterly make away many." In other words, when Antichrist gets power over these ten kingdoms, the countries to the north and east of these kingdoms will make war on him and he will become the victor over these new enemies during the last 3½ years of this age. Then he will become the ruler of Russia, Germany, and other countries to the north and east of the Roman Empire territory and will thus fulfill Ezek. 38; 39 by leading all these countries to war against the Jews and Jesus Christ at Armageddon. Thus, instead of Russia being the leading nation and the head of all nations at Armageddon, she is a conquered nation along with others under the Antichrist who will be the leader of all nations against Christ at Armageddon.

- That the Bible plainly shows how the nations of the world will be lined up against each other in the last days and who will be the victor? As seen in the last point above, the Antichrist will come from Syria and will get power over ten kingdoms that will be formed inside the territory of the Roman Empire in the last days. These ten will be lined up against Russia, Germany, Japan, China, and other northern and eastern nations outside the ten kingdoms. The ten kingdoms under Antichrist will be the victor in a war lasting most of the last 3½ years of this age. Then the Antichrist will lead the nations against Jerusalem to destroy the Jews and to stop Christ from setting up His kingdom, but he will be defeated by Christ before all of Jerusalem is conquered (Zech. 14:1-21; Rev. 19:11-21). Thus Antichrist will be defeated before he conquers the whole world.

- That the Bible gives you a simple key to the book of Revelation that will make the whole book simple to all alike? This key is found in Rev. 1:19 and 4:1. The book is in three clearly defined divisions: first, "the things which thou hast seen," that is, the vision of Christ, as in Rev. 1; second, "the things which are," that is, the things concerning the churches, as in Rev. 2-3; third, "the things which shall be hereafter," that is, after the churches, as in Rev. 4–22. To prove that everything of Revelation, from the fourth chapter on, must be after the churches, in Rev. 4:1 after he had written the vision of Christ in chapter one and the things concerning the churches in chapters two and three, John was caught up to Heaven and was told that he was to see the things which must be hereafter, that is, after the churches. If the things of Rev. 4:1 through the rest of the book must be after the churches, then they must be fulfilled after the churches and not during the time of the churches. This is proof that the rapture of the Church must take place before the fulfillment of everything in Rev. 4-22. If we will take literally and consecutively the events of these chapters and believe that they will happen after the rapture of the Church, nothing in the book will be hard to understand.

- That prophecy is as simple as history to understand? This is true because prophecy is simply history written beforehand in the same literal language as history which is a record of things that have happened. If you take all prophecy as a simple record of what is going to happen — the same way you take history as a record of what has happened — it will be impossible to misunderstand prophecy.

- That Christ is literally coming back to the world soon to set up a literal earthly kingdom and to reign over all nations forever? That He is literally coming back is clear from Zech. 14:1-9; Mt. 24:29-31; Acts 1:11; Rev. 19:11-21. That He will rule all nations forever after this is clear from Dan. 2:44-45; 7:13-14, 18; Isa. 9:6-7; Lk. 1:32-33; Rev. 11:15.

- That at the second coming of Christ many sinners will be left to go into the Millennium? That sinners will be here is very clearly stated in Isa. 2:2-4; 65:20; 66:18-19; 1 Cor. 15:24-28; Rev. 20:1-10; Zech. 14:16-21.

- That the rapture of the Church will be before the Tribulation and the coming of the Antichrist? We have already proven in Point 6, above, that the rapture must take place before the fulfillment of Rev. 4–22, which describes the Tribulation period and the Millennium and the New Earth after the Millennium. According to 2 Thess. 2:7-8 it is stated that the hinderer of lawlessness will be taken out of the way "And then shall that wicked be revealed" and since the wicked is here for the last seven years of this age, according to Dan. 9:27, the hinderer must be removed before Antichrist comes and before the Tribulation that he will cause when he comes. That the hinderer refers to the Church will be proven in Lesson Forty-three.

supplements.title: Supplement 7
supplements.content:
# Supplement Seven

## For Lessons Thirteen and Fourteen

## HOW TO GET HEALED AND STAY HEALED

### I. Become Converted and Born-again

The gospel teaches that forgiveness of sins and healing of the body go hand in hand. If ministers would teach the whole gospel, people would have faith for both healing and forgiveness—and they'd receive both at the moment they get saved from sin.

The following Scriptures prove that both benefits were provided through the sacrifice at Calvary: "Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses" (Mt. 8:16-17); "Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose stripes ye were healed" (1 Pet. 2:24).

And these passages show that forgiveness and healing should be received at the same time: "Who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases" (Ps. 103:3); "For whether is easier to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?" (Mt. 9:5); "For this people's heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them" (Mt. 13:15); "Is any sick among you? Let him call for the elders of the church; and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord: And the prayer of faith shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; and if he have committed sins, they shall be forgiven him" (Jas. 5:14-16).

So you can see clearly—healing is just as easy to receive from God as forgiveness of sins. Both can be received by the same simple faith in God, asking in the name of Jesus. Healing is part of your salvation, so don't let yourself be cheated out of it any more than you'd let yourself be cheated out of forgiveness.

### II. Have Faith in God for Healing

If you ask God for healing and believe it's done—the same way you're taught to ask for forgiveness—it will be done. Don't doubt for one moment that God will heal you, just as you wouldn't doubt that He forgives you.

Resist every doubt that you're healed, the same way you'd resist any temptation to doubt your forgiveness. Think of it like this: when you first got saved, you were taught to accept salvation by faith and count it done whether you felt it or not. You believed God forgave you regardless of your feelings—and because you believed, you were saved.
