It's the same with healing. After you've prayed and asked for healing, count it done. Believe it regardless of feelings, symptoms, or anything your body seems to be telling you.

If you'll hold firm that you're healed—just as you know you're forgiven—God will confirm the healing just as He confirms forgiveness. This cannot be overemphasized: healing depends so much on standing firm that your prayer has been heard and answered.

If you want the benefits of the gospel, do what's required to get them, and you won't fail. If you're not saved or haven't followed the instructions in the previous supplements, you'll need to do so if you want results.

We can't guarantee the gospel's benefits until you meet these conditions. Are the benefits worth the effort? If they are, do as you were instructed. If they're not worth the effort to believe God without wavering, then don't be frustrated when you don't get what you want.

You can't expect God to show favoritism and give you these benefits when you fail to do what He requires. God isn't that kind of God—He won't break His laws for anyone. He knows every person can choose to meet the conditions of unwavering faith.

Stop babying yourself or complaining that it's hard to believe. That very attitude is sinful—you should ask God to forgive you for believing lies and Satan instead of truth and God. As long as you cooperate with Satan through unbelief, you can't cooperate with God through true faith. You can't serve two masters or hold faith and unbelief at the same time.

If you've repented of your sins and your unbelief, you have a right—a perfect right according to the gospel—to receive what the gospel promises and what Christ died to give you. So it's all-important to get rid of unbelief and have simple, child-like faith in God for healing, for material prosperity, and for anything you ask of God.

Faith is required according to Mt. 21:21-22; Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; Heb. 11:6; Jas. 1:5-8. (See Lesson Ten, Point III.) Everyone who has faith will be healed without exception.

### III. Wholly Trust God in Sickness and Adversity

The Bible says, "Cursed be the man that trusteth in man, and maketh flesh his arm, and whose heart departeth from the Lord . . . he shall be like the heath in the desert . . . Blessed is the man that trusteth in the Lord, and whose hope the Lord is . . . he shall be as a tree planted by the waters . . . and shall not see when heat cometh, but her leaf shall be green [shall have health] . . . neither shall cease from yielding fruit" (Jer. 17:5-10).

See also Prov. 3:5-8; 2 Chron. 16:12-14; 2 Ki. 1:1-17; Mk. 5:25-34.

### IV. Obey God and His Word

"My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments: for length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee" (Prov. 3:1-8); "Attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings . . . for they are life unto those that find them; and health to all their flesh" (Prov. 2:1-5; 3:13-24; 4:20-23); "If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you" (Jn. 15:7; 1 Jn. 3:2-22; 5:14-15).

See also Exod. 15:26; Deut 4:40; 7:12-15; 28:1-62; 30:1-20; Ps. 105:37; 107:20.

### V. Make Peace with Fellow Men

"When ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses" (Mk. 11:22-26); "Confess your faults one to another, and pray one for another, that ye may be healed" (Jas. 5:14-16).

### VI. Call Church Elders and Believe in Their Prayers

"Is any sick among you? let him call for the elders of the church; and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord: and the prayer of faith shall save the sick" (Jas. 5:14-16); "They shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover" (Mk. 16:17-20).

Thousands of ministers today can testify to miraculous healings in their ministry—in the lives of people who obeyed James and called for prayer. Multiplied thousands are being healed every day by following this divine prescription. In almost every church, you'll find witnesses to the power of God to heal the body by faith in the name of Jesus Christ.

### VII. Control the Tongue

"The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life . . . There is that speaketh like the piercings of a sword; but the tongue of the wise is health . . . He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life; but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction . . . A wholesome tongue is a tree of life . . . pleasant words are as an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones . . . Death and life are in the power of the tongue" (Prov. 10:11; 12:18; 13:3; 15:4; 16:24; 18:21).

"What man is he that desireth life and loveth many days, that he may see good. Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile" (Ps. 34:13-14; 1 Pet. 3:10-11; Jas. 3).

It's interesting to note that nine times out of eleven plagues upon Israel, as recorded in Numbers alone, were caused by the misuse of the tongue.

### VIII. Live in God and under His Protection

"He that dwelleth [remains, settles down, or takes up a homestead] in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow [defense, protection] of the Almighty . . . I will say of the Lord, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust. Surely he shall deliver thee from the snare of the fowler, and from the noisome pestilence. He shall cover thee with his feathers . . . his truth shall be thy shield and buckler. Thou shalt not be afraid for the terror by night; nor for the arrow that flieth by day; nor for the pestilence that walketh in darkness; nor for the destruction that wasteth at noonday. A thousand shall Fall at thy side, and ten thousand at thy right hand, but it shall not come nigh thee . . . There shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling. For he shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee in all thy ways. They shall bear thee up in their hands, lest thou dash thy foot against a stone. Thou shalt tread upon the lion and adder; the young lion and the dragon shalt thou trample under feet . . . He shall call upon me, and I will answer him: I will be with him in trouble; I will deliver him and honour him. With long life will I satisfy him, and shew him my salvation" (Ps. 91).

### IX. Praise God in Faith for Healing

David said, "Bless the Lord, O my soul: and all that is within me . . . forget not all his benefits: who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases" (Ps. 103:1-5).

It's written of Abraham, "He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God; and being fully persuaded that, what he had promised, he was able also to perform. And therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness" (Rom. 4:17-22; Heb. 11:11-12).

Paul said, "Be careful [anxious] for nothing; but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God" (Phil. 4:5-6; Heb. 13:15-16).

### X. Serve God in Holiness as Earnestly as You Sought Him

"As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him: rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving" (Col. 2:6-7); "Sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee . . . Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more" (Jn. 5:14; 8:11); "If our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God. And whatsoever we ask, we receive of him, because we keep his commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in his sight" (1 Jn. 3:21-22; 5:14-15).

### XI. Appropriate the Benefits of Calvary

"Whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord . . . he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning [comprehending the benefits by faith of] the Lord's body. For this cause many are weak and sickly among you and many sleep" (1 Cor. 11:27-30); "What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them" (Mk. 11:24).

### XII. Resist the Devil—Refuse to be Denied

"Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Draw nigh to God and he will draw nigh to you" (Jas. 4:7-8); "The devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour: whom resist steadfast in the faith" (1 Pet. 5:8-10).

See also Eph. 6:10-18; 2 Cor. 10:4-7.

### XIII. Observe the Laws of God and of Nature

Your body is God's house as well as your own. It belongs to God, not to you: "Know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own? For ye are bought with a price: therefore, glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God's" (1 Cor. 6:19-20). Since it's God's house, you're forbidden to mar it, defile it, or abuse it (1 Cor. 3:16-17).

Common sense tells us that God can get more glory from a body that's well and strong—because a healthy person has complete use of all their faculties. A rugged constitution, a keen and alert mind, and unimpaired moral and spiritual faculties are necessary if you're going to fully glorify God in body and spirit.

We glorify God best by bearing much fruit (Jn. 15:8). To bear much good fruit, a tree or vine must be healthy and free from disease and things that cause faulty fruit (Mt. 3:10; 7:16-20). It's similar to how an orchard keeper knows the best fruit comes from the healthiest trees. The same is true for Christians.

If God can get some glory from lives hindered by physical handicaps, think how much more glory He could get from those same people without such handicaps! Naturally, someone with a physical limitation can still do many things—but without that limitation, they could do even more.

All governments and organizations require physical fitness. They set certain standards for their employees and make sure fitness is maintained at peak efficiency. They require that any accident or sickness be reported immediately, and every sore, illness, or cold be taken care of right away to prevent further layoff or spread to others.

In every factory and public place we see signs like "Be careful," "Watch that cold," "Don't expose your body," "Take exercise." Many companies provide their employees places for recreation and exercise. Laws in every city and state require physical fitness for marriage and certain public positions. Free clinics, medicine, and advice are provided for those who can't afford what they need to preserve their health.

Parents will spend everything and sacrifice all they have to make sure their children get the best physical care and protection. They'd heal their own children in a second if they could. Practically every human being would make some sacrifice to see their fellow people healed and enjoying good health. People can fight and hate each other, but when sickness or death comes, those same enemies would help each other get well or have a decent burial. Even most animals take care of their own and do everything possible to protect the life and welfare of their young.

So is God the only Being in the universe who loves to see His own children—His own creation, His own workers, those He professes to love—be sickly, puny, tired, worn out, and physically good-for-nothing? Is He the only one who loves to see pain and disease in His own creation? Is He the only one who hasn't provided for the physical welfare of His own?

Is He the only one who gloats over His children's handicaps and wants them diseased so He can be "glorified" by their sickness? Is He the only one who prefers sickness to health? Who wouldn't heal His children when they get sick? Who chastens His children by causing their lungs to be eaten up by disease, their limbs to rot off, their lives to linger between life and death in the most intense suffering for years and years?

Is He the only one who would make a creation only to let it end in suffering and defeat and die prematurely? Who wills these sin-cursed, disease-destroying forces to take their toll and cut people off before they finish their life's work? Is He the only one who doesn't require a high standard of physical fitness among His workers? Is He the only one whose highest will is shown through the sufferings, sicknesses, and helpless conditions of His people? Is He the only one who gets the best glory from people when they're physically, morally, and spiritually deprived of their normal functions?

If He were that kind of being, why didn't He make humanity sick from the start of creation? Why didn't He keep people sick in both Testaments instead of healing them? If sickness is God's will, why doesn't everyone have the same diseases so all could be equally in His perfect will? Is He a respecter of persons—wanting only some to be in His will through disease while others are well and therefore out of His will?

Why did Jesus die to take away our sicknesses? Why has God provided all the necessary means for healing and health? Why has He cursed those who didn't trust Him for healing?

Such ideas are not in harmony with the Bible or the character of God. It's God's highest will for everyone to "prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth" (3 Jn. 2). The blame for sin and disease cannot be laid at God's door or blamed on His will. The blame falls entirely on humanity and the enemies of both God and people.

Instead of meeting the conditions of the gospel to receive healing and stay healed, people continue to break the laws of God and nature, living in unbelief and rejecting God's way of healing and health in Christ. The sensible thing to do is learn the cause of the trouble, remove it, and keep it removed. Then you can be healed and stay healed.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Seven. Do You Know:

- That the rapture of the Church is not a part of the second coming of Christ? The rapture is a distinct event in itself and takes place at least seven years before the second coming of Christ. The rapture happens before the Tribulation, and the second coming after the Tribulation. The rapture is when Christ comes for the saints (1 Thess. 4:13-17), and the second coming is when He returns to Earth with them (Zech. 14:1-5; Jude 14; Rev. 19:11-21). At the rapture, Christ takes the saints to Heaven (1 Thess. 3:13; 4:16; Col. 3:4), and at the second coming He leaves Heaven with them (Rev. 19:11-21). At the rapture, Christ doesn't come to the Earth (1 Thess. 4:16), but at the second coming He does (Zech. 14:4; Mt. 24:29-31). Since Christ doesn't come to the Earth at the rapture, it can't be called the second coming of Christ.

- That the Bible plainly states qualifications for those who will go in the rapture? You must be "in Christ" (1 Thess. 4:16; 2 Cor. 5:17); be "Christ's" (1 Cor. 15:23; Gal. 5:24); do "good" and be "blessed and holy" (Jn. 5:28-29; Rev. 20:4-6).

- That there have been and will be five raptures in the first resurrection? First, Christ and the saints resurrected after His resurrection going to Heaven (Mt. 27:52; Eph. 4:7-11; Acts 1:11); second, the rapture of those "that are Christ's at his coming" (1 Cor. 15:23; 1 Thess. 4:13-16); third, the rapture of the 144,000 Jews in the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week (Rev. 12:5; 14:1-5; Dan. 12:1; Isa. 66:7-8); fourth, the rapture of the Tribulation saints (Rev. 7:9-17; 15:2-4; 20:4-6); fifth, the rapture of the two witnesses (Rev. 11:3-12).

- That there are two main resurrections from the dead with a thousand years between them? The first resurrection is for the blessed and holy, happening before the thousand years. We read, "The rest of the dead lived not again until the thousand years were finished" (Rev. 20:4-15).

- That we're living in the very last days of this age? This is proven by the fact that certain prophecies were to be fulfilled only in the last days—and since they're now being fulfilled, this proves we're in the last days. Read Mt. 24:37-38; 1 Tim. 4; 2 Tim. 3; 2 Pet. 3:1-9; Ezek. 37; Jer. 30:18-24.

- That no prophecy must yet be fulfilled and no sign must yet take place before the rapture? This is proven because the early Christians were looking for the rapture at any time in their day without any particular prophecy being fulfilled, as shown in Phil. 3:20-21; Titus 2:11-13.

- That all signs of the coming of the Lord point to the second advent and not to the rapture of the Church? This is proven because all signs of the Lord's coming were given in connection with the second advent, not the rapture (Mt. 24-25). Christians look for the rapture to happen at any moment without any particular sign coming to pass, as shown in Point 6 above. You can't look for an event to happen at any moment if certain other events must precede it—so associate all signs of the Lord's coming with His literal coming to Earth, not with the rapture, and everything becomes clear.

- That "two in the field, one taken and another left" does not refer to the rapture? This teaching is connected with Christ's literal coming to Earth "immediately after the Tribulation," as proven by the word "Then" in Mt. 24:29-42. The statement "the flood came and took them all away" means "destroyed them all," proving that those taken away at the second advent will be those killed in the Battle of Armageddon. These will be the carcasses for the eagles to eat, as pictured in Mt. 24:27-28; Rev. 19:17-21; Ezek. 39:17-21. Those left will be those not killed in this battle—they'll be permitted to live on into the Millennium, as in Zech. 14:16-21. This will be made clear by many Scriptures in Lesson Forty-four.

- That the fig tree of Mt. 24:31 does not refer to the Jewish nation? This was never Jesus' intended meaning. He was simply illustrating that just as we know summer is near when leaves appear on trees, so we'll know His coming to Earth is near when we see the signs of Mt. 24:4-26 come to pass. In Lk. 21:29 we read, "Behold the fig tree, and all the trees; when they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand. So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand." If we're going to make the fig tree represent the Jews, then to be consistent we'd have to make all the other trees represent Gentile nations. If Gentiles are going to "bud forth" and be blessed like the Jews, then the old theory of Jews being blessed above Gentiles in the latter days loses its force. This isn't the thought at all, as anyone can see who takes the plain intended truth of these passages. If we want to teach something about the Jews, let's get plain Scriptures to prove it instead of using a few leaves of a fig tree as proof.

- That the Bible teaches a first and second coming of God the Father to the Earth? In Dan. 7:18-22 we see that the Ancient of Days is coming to destroy the Antichrist. The Ancient of Days and the Son of man are two different persons, as proven in Dan. 7:9-14. If the Ancient of Days is not the Son of man, then He must be the Father. If He's the Father, then the Father is coming to Earth when Christ comes. This is also proven in Zech. 14:5; Titus 2:11-13. We call this the first coming of the Father to Earth to fulfill a mission. The second coming of the Father is at the end of the Millennium when the New Jerusalem—the capital city of God—comes from Heaven to Earth to be among people forever, as stated in Rev. 21:1-21. This is the second time the Father is pictured in Scripture as coming to Earth to fulfill a mission. This time He will be forever among humanity, and this planet Earth will be the site of His capital forever.

supplements.title: Supplement 8
supplements.content:
# Supplement Eight

**For Lessons Fifteen and Sixteen**

## The Unfolding of God's Promise

In Lesson Fifteen, we saw how God began revealing more details about His plan during the time of Abraham. He started showing us more clearly who the promised "seed of the woman" would be and why He would come into the world.

God chose Abraham's descendants to be His representatives on Earth. Their job was to demonstrate God's will among all people — to show everyone that God loved them and wanted to bless them with holiness, health, wealth, and happiness. These blessings could never be fully enjoyed apart from God.

God also wanted to show that He had a wonderful future planned for everyone who would follow His ways. During this time — what we call the Dispensation of Promise — God began requiring something specific: absolute, unwavering faith in His plan.

People were expected to believe that God wanted to remove want, sickness, and misery from the Earth. They were to trust that He would restore humanity to the state we enjoyed before the Fall. This approach to dealing with humanity made it possible for any individual who stayed true to God to receive everything He freely promised.

Here's the beautiful part: faith still works the same way today. Even if everyone else in the world refused to believe God and follow His plan, one person who does believe can still receive all the benefits. This makes God perfectly fair to everyone and allows each person to reach what the majority might miss.

## Unlimited Power in Prayer

As God's plan unfolds through these lessons, we can clearly see that the possibilities in prayer are unlimited. Lesson Sixteen showed us there are no limits to what we can receive from God according to His promises. There's no benefit God has promised that we can't obtain.

With this knowledge of God's will and the unlimited power of prayer, there's really no excuse for all the sin, sickness, disease, failure, poverty, and unhappiness in the world. It's honestly hard to understand why God's children settle for so much less than what's available to them when they could have abundance.

Yes, many have been taught wrong or limited ideas about God's grace. But that excuse doesn't work anymore — we're giving you the true light of God's Word in these lessons and supplements. From here on, it's not about ignorance or bad teaching. It will simply be a matter of willfully rejecting plain truth and refusing to put in the effort needed to receive these benefits.

In our previous lessons, we've given you plenty of scriptural proof that there's no limitation for believers in prayer. You can attain everything you want in this life and the life to come. Lesson Ten proved God's plan for meeting our needs. Lesson Twelve showed why that plan often goes unrealized. And Lesson Sixteen demonstrated how to attain the fullness of life simply by asking and receiving according to clear laws God has revealed in Scripture.

With all these wonderful truths plus the many helps throughout the lessons and supplements, you should be receiving God's abundance right here and now.

## Inventory of Your Personal Progress

Let's have a heart-to-heart talk about your progress. After all, it's your personal gain in life that you're interested in. That's why you wanted to study these lessons. That's why you want to continue through the whole series until you have the complete truth that will help you live in God's abundance.

Let me ask you: what benefits have you actually received from these lessons? Take some time to review everything — the lessons and supplements — to see what you've gained in knowledge, wisdom, healing, prosperity, answers to prayer, and whatever you personally want and need in life.

Have you faithfully followed the instructions from the very first lesson until now? Have you practiced the lessons on prayer and consecration to God's will? Have you surrendered your life to God? Are you really born-again? Have you given up the sin-business?

Has your fulfillment of needs and wants been worth giving up sin for? Or have you been trying to attain God's benefits without actually becoming His child — benefits that are promised only to those who obey Him?

Do you have a simple understanding of God's plan so you can follow His will? Have you conformed to the plan as you've learned it? Do you have faith in God, or are you still battling demon powers because of doubt and unbelief?

Only you can answer these questions. No one else can answer them for you except God. It's absolutely necessary that you conscientiously answer these questions before God and check up on yourself to see how you're progressing.

If you're making good progress, it's because you've obeyed the simple instructions to the letter. If you're not progressing as fast as you wish — if your needs for body, soul, and spirit aren't being supplied — it's because you've failed on some point. You're the one who needs to discover where you're failing. You're the one who needs to concentrate on those weak points. Even if you have to start all over again, the rich dividends will make it worth it.

Here's what I suggest: make an outline of the necessary steps you need to follow. Write down the main points from the first lesson and supplement through Supplement Eight and see if you fully understand everything you're supposed to do. Check off the points you've been faithful to practice daily. Also check off the points where you've failed. Then begin to practice all the truth, not just part of it.

If after this you're still not getting the benefits you're entitled to, go to God about it. Quote the promises that cover your needs and wants. Seek Him about the failure. Make sure you haven't been doubting God while practicing these principles.

If you've been wondering or questioning why you haven't made better progress, that very wondering shows you've been doubting. And if you doubt, that doubt itself will defeat you. As long as you question and wonder why your prayers aren't being answered, you'll stay defeated.

Don't mistake this! Don't let anyone deceive you! God's Word is clear on every detail. You can't miss the truth — it's too plainly written to leave anyone in ignorance.

Scripture is direct: "he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because he eateth not in faith: for whatsoever is not of faith is sin" (Rom. 14:23). And James tells us, "let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him. But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed. For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord" (Jas. 1:5-8).

Your duty couldn't be made any clearer when it comes to getting answers to prayer. If you have sin in your life, no wonder you waver. If you don't have sin in your life, you have no business wavering — so quit it here and now.

Make up your mind that you're done with wavering. Simply refuse to make God a liar anymore. It doesn't matter how hard evil spirits press you to doubt God and get discouraged — you don't have to yield to them even slightly. You must stubbornly refuse to doubt what God has said and resist these satanic forces.

Jas. 4:7 says, "Resist the devil, and he will flee from you." You couldn't bring yourself to believe that's a lie, could you? Would you dare say you've resisted and the devil hasn't fled? Would you dare say the Bible isn't true? No! You can't afford to say that if you want things from God.

You might wonder what to do next after you've done all this. The answer is simple: keep doing it until victory comes. And it will come. Never doubt this fact. God is true. The devil is a liar. You can believe God more than you can believe the devil.

If you're having a struggle with unbelief, that very struggle proves you have unbelief — otherwise you wouldn't be having any trouble. As long as unbelief remains, the fight must continue until you firmly and positively refuse to waver again.

Let it be settled once and forever: you will not doubt. Since you know you won't get what you've prayed for as long as you doubt, it should be easy to cast all doubt aside and resist the very unbelief that causes failure.

Think about it this way: you know you won't get the answer as long as you doubt. You also know there's a definite assurance you will get it if you won't doubt. So why be foolish and give in to unbelief? Even if there were the slightest possibility of not getting the answer even with faith, you'd still be better off not doubting — because doubt brings certain defeat. You certainly can't lose any more by having unwavering faith than by having doubt. So why not wholeheartedly cooperate with God and have faith in Him?

Jesus gave absolute assurance that you will receive if you have faith and don't doubt. He said, "If ye have faith, and doubt not, ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree, but also if ye shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; It shall be done. And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive" (Mt. 21:21-22; Mk. 11:22-24; Jn. 15:7, 16).

Why should you be afraid to step out on God's promises? They will not fail. "All the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us" (2 Cor. 1:20). Being afraid to trust God and His Word shouldn't be tolerated in a Christian's life.

Think of it like this: there was once a man crossing a frozen river. He was so afraid the ice might break that he crawled on his hands and knees. When he got out to the middle of the river, he heard whistling behind him. He looked around and saw a boy with a wagon full of corn pulled by a pair of husky mules, coming across the river like it was solid ground. The boy was carefree, with absolute confidence that the ice would hold the heavy load. But the poor man was afraid the ice would break under his own weight, so he crawled across in fear.

God's promises will hold you up. They will not fail. You'll see in due time that God means exactly what He says. One of the greatest surprises of Heaven will be seeing how abundantly God had provided for us in this life, even though we failed to receive those benefits.

Let's make up our minds never to waver or limit God and His abundant promises for our needs in this life. God will not rebuke you for daring to ask anything He has promised. "Let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him" (Jas. 1:4-8).

If you're not progressing as fast as you'd like, get busy — but don't get discouraged. You're the one who's been hindering your own progress. You can progress as fast as you want. Read the promises over and over again until faith is generated in you enough to say, "It shall be done," "I do believe God," "I do believe He has heard my prayer and I have the answer."

God is more anxious for every one of His children to believe than any of them is to receive. God loves each one and deals with each one personally according to their faith. On this basis God deals with everyone. Every single person can get what they want and make the progress in God they desire.

You should be personally satisfied with your progress because you're the one solely responsible for it. There should be no complaining. If you want to develop in the business of asking and receiving, do what everyone has to do to succeed in this kind of business. God is no respecter of persons. He doesn't try to hold one back and push another forward. He doesn't will that any of His children should be defeated in any area of life.

## Partnership With God in Business

We've seen above that there's unlimited power in prayer. A businessman needs God in his business to have guaranteed success. He shouldn't be ashamed to take God as a partner and let that be known. He should honor God in this respect and give Him credit for his success.

He should take God into consideration and pray about every phase of his life and work. He should be just as incurably religious and prayerful about his business as any preacher, and he's bound to succeed.

All men pray, even if some are ashamed of it. Even sinners and infidels find themselves praying to God when calamity strikes or when they're scared. They do it naturally under such circumstances — they don't have to be taught. They simply pray from the heart during those times, without regard to whether it will help or not. They must pray, proving that prayer is natural.

Because prayer is natural, let's all live a natural, normal life and not be ashamed of it. It should be considered the greatest honor to pray to the Supreme Sovereign of the Universe.

The businessman above many others should openly pray to God for help with his many problems. He should learn to let God run his business by following God's will in every move he makes. God is interested in every man's success and will help everyone succeed in life if He's consulted and obeyed.

God can run your business better than you can run it yourself. He knows the details of the business world and all the problems businessmen face. He can supernaturally work and accomplish things where all human efforts fail.

It doesn't matter what business you're in. If it's legitimate, you shouldn't feel it would be wrong to ask God to help you and lead you into unknown ways of making money and helping others through your work.

If you want to succeed in business, you must not only make God your partner, but also be willing to follow the laws of prosperity as discussed in Supplement Six. No one can possibly fail in business by following these suggestions. With God as a partner and by following the laws of prosperity, success in any kind of business is inevitable.

God can help the businessman by changing his life and conduct so he becomes capable of success. God can help by directing him into the proper channels and to the sources of help he needs. God can help by moving on people's hearts at every moment help is needed — to carry through some transaction during a crisis and bring success.

Partnership with God is indispensable to any businessman and any Christian in any walk of life. The person who fails to seek God's help in every endeavor misses many blessings that partnership with God would bring. If you take God into your life and acknowledge Him in all things, you will be a success.

Problems that seem impossible to solve — situations where you can't see any way out — if God is your partner, you can simply turn them over to Him, and He will make a way. Things can work out so easily and quickly when God acts that you'll be amazed how problems get solved.

How anyone can get along without God in their business is hard to understand. Another thing some find hard to understand is how godless people prosper in business without God. But this is easily explained when we consider that following the laws of prosperity will cause both sinners and saints to prosper materially.

If sinners can prosper by these laws without God, how much more will a Christian prosper with God as a personal partner? If Christians could see this, they wouldn't wonder at the prosperity of the wicked. If all businessmen could see that prosperity with God is greater than without Him, many would turn to God for supernatural guidance in business.

## Tapping the Infinite Resources of God

Dwight L. Moody once said, "The world has yet to see what God will do in and for and with and through the man that is wholly yielded to Him." How true this is! The infinite resources of God have never been fully explored.

The average person has such a limited concept of God and His infinite love and provision for all creation that God Himself is limited in manifesting His great love to them. When we limit God, we naturally limit His blessings to ourselves. If people could wake up and see for themselves the abundance of God's provision, they would get so much more from Him.

As long as people argue that it's not God's will to do this or that, or that He cannot or will not do certain things, they'll come far short of God's blessings. It seems to me that just one reading of the Bible would be enough to correct most of the wrong ideas people have about God.

The purpose of these lessons is to help such misled people see for themselves what God's will is and what they're missing of the good things in life. Everyone should read the Bible for themselves and see what it plainly says on all these questions. Anyone who reads for themselves can see where they've been misled. The Bible says one thing, and what men teach contrary to it is easily detected.

You who are taking these lessons have no excuse, for your eyes are being opened to see the infinite blessings God has promised to those who love Him. You have no possible excuse to continue in unbelief and ignorance of what God wants you to have.

God has literally promised material wealth, physical health, and spiritual freedom from sin as well as from evil habits and failure in life. There's not one phase of life that God has ignored in His program. There is no want to those who fear God. There can be no failure for anyone who will tap God's resources. It's the duty of every believer to learn what God has promised and then be bold and aggressive in attaining the benefits provided.

Enter wholeheartedly into fellowship with God. Love and obey Him, and you'll see for yourself that "No good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly" (Ps. 84:11).

You have before you sickness or health, poverty or wealth, failure or success. You have before you the infinite resources of God, and you can have what you want in life that is good. So forget the many false concepts of God and truth you've been taught and lay hold of the abundance of life. You can have anything you want by simply asking and receiving from your Heavenly Father.

## Ten Questions For Supplement Eight. Do You Know:

- **That the bride of Christ is a city and not the Church?** In Rev. 21:2, 9-10, it's stated as simply as possible that the bride of Christ is the heavenly city, the New Jerusalem. The bride then is not the Church or a special part of the Church. Neither is the bride Israel or a special company of Jews such as the 144,000 of Rev. 7 and 14. If the bride is a city, that should be accepted without question. In no place is the Church called the bride, a virgin, a lady, a woman, or referred to by feminine pronouns. On the contrary, the Church is called a "man" because it is the body of Christ, who is a man (Eph. 2:15; 4:13).

- **That all saints of all ages will be a part of the bride of Christ?** If the bride of Christ is a city, then naturally all who will live in this city will be part of the bride. According to Heb. 11:8-16, the Old Testament saints are going to live in that city, so they'll be part of the bride. According to Heb. 13:14, present Church saints are going to live in the same city, so they'll also be part of the bride. The tribulation saints and the 144,000 Jews of Rev. 7 and 14 will also have part in the resurrection and live in this city, so these two companies will be part of the bride as well (Rev. 7:1-17; 14:1-5; 15:2-4; 20:4-6). Therefore, every company of redeemed who will live in the New Jerusalem will be part of the bride of Christ. Just as no single individual or group, or no single building or group of buildings that make up a city could be called that city, so no individual, group, or building in the New Jerusalem could be called the bride of Christ. All the resurrected redeemed saints of all ages and all the other inhabitants and buildings of the New Jerusalem will make the final bride of Christ, as we'll see in Lesson Fifty-two.

- **That there is a difference between the kingdom of Heaven and kingdom of God?** The kingdom of Heaven in this age is the realm of profession as taught by the parables, wherein the tares and wheat and the good and bad are in the kingdom of Heaven. The kingdom of God is the realm of God in the vast universe. The kingdom of Heaven is only a part of the universal kingdom of God, as we'll see in Lesson Thirty-one.

- **That the Church is not the kingdom of Heaven or kingdom of God?** The Church is the wheat-part of the kingdom of Heaven and only a part of the true servants of the universal kingdom of God. In other words, the tares in the kingdom of Heaven and the angels and other subjects of God in the kingdom of God are not part of the Church, as we'll see in future studies.

- **That there will never be a revived Roman Empire, as taught by many Bible students?** The common theory is that the Roman Empire will be revived in the last days and that Antichrist will rule all this empire from Rome. But this isn't taught anywhere in Scripture. According to Dan. 7:23-24, there will never be a revival of the Roman Empire — which would mean one vast empire formed from the twenty-two states now inside the territory of the old Roman Empire. Instead of one empire ruled by one man from Rome, as in the days of the Caesars, there will be ten kingdoms ruled by ten kings from ten separate capitals inside this territory. In other words, ten kingdoms will be formed instead of one empire. There will be ten rulers instead of one. These ten are the same ones symbolized by the ten toes on the image of Dan. 2:31-45 and the ten horns on the beast of Rev. 17:8-17. That they'll exist at the second advent of Christ is clear from Rev. 17:14, where they fight against Christ the day He comes. This same truth is taught in Dan. 2:44-45, which speaks of the God of Heaven setting up His kingdom on Earth during the days of the ten kings.

- **That Antichrist cannot be any man now prominent in world affairs?** This is clear from the fact that the "little horn," the coming Antichrist, cannot come until after the ten kingdoms are formed inside the territory of the Roman Empire. If the "little horn" represents the Antichrist coming after the ten kingdoms are formed and will come from among them, as plainly stated in Dan. 7:7-8, 23-24, then he cannot come until those kingdoms are formed. Therefore, he cannot be any man in world affairs until after they're fully formed. His coming, according to 2 Thess. 2:7-8, is after the rapture of the Church, as we'll see in Lesson Forty-three.

- **That the Antichrist will be a future mortal man and not some supernatural resurrected man or some succession of popes, as taught by many?** That he'll be a man is clear from Rev. 13:18: "it is the number of a man; and his number is six hundred three-score and six." That he'll be mortal is clear from the fact that he'll be "slain" by Christ at His second advent (Dan. 7:11; Isa. 11:4; 2 Thess. 2:7-8). If Christ is going to slay him at the second advent, he must be mortal at that time. This proves he won't be a resurrected immortal man from the dead.

- **That Antichrist cannot be Judas or any man that has ever lived on Earth before?** As we've seen above, he'll be a mere mortal man at the second advent of Christ, so he couldn't be a resurrected immortal man — which he would have to be if he were Judas or some other historical man coming back from death. There's no statement anywhere in Scripture that says he must be some historical man from the dead. This is mere supposition from prophetic sensationalists who try to get more from the Bible than it says. That both Judas and Antichrist are called sons of perdition proves nothing — this term simply means "sons of destruction" because both are destined to destruction. It's appointed unto men "once to die," not two or three times as would have to be the case if Antichrist were some historical man from the dead. Antichrist will die the day Christ comes, so he must be mortal at that time. If he's mortal then, he can't be someone from past history who died and has seen corruption in the grave for hundreds or thousands of years. Therefore, this theory isn't worthy of consideration from Scripture's standpoint.

- **That Antichrist cannot come from Italy, the Vatican, Germany, Russia, or from any prominent country of today, or from Heaven or Hell?** According to Dan. 7:7-8, 23-24, he'll come from within the old Roman Empire and from among the ten kingdoms yet to be formed there. This alone proves he can't come from Heaven, Hell, Germany, Russia, or other countries some say he'll come from. According to Dan. 8:7-9, 20-23, he must come from one of the four divisions of the old Grecian Empire — known today as Greece, Turkey, Syria, and Egypt. When Alexander the Great died, his empire was divided into four parts, as any ancient history publication shows. Dan. 8:9 states, "Out of one of them" — out of one of these four divisions — the "little horn" would come, explained in verse 23 as a king of fierce countenance rising in the latter time of these four kingdoms. The four kingdoms of Dan. 8 will make up four of the ten of Dan. 7. This chapter narrows the Antichrist's origin geographically from ten kingdoms to four, so we can definitively say he won't come from any country outside these four kingdoms. Because he's coming from either Greece, Turkey, Syria, or Egypt, he cannot come from Italy, the Vatican, Germany, Russia, or other places many Bible students claim — none of these countries was part of the old Grecian Empire or its four divisions. If people had read these chapters and believed what's plainly revealed, we never would have had all the foolish speculation about Mussolini, Hitler, or other men. Neither would we have the present foolish speculation that the Pope or some current man will be the Antichrist.

- **That the Bible definitely tells you where Antichrist comes from?** Daniel not only makes clear he'll come from the ten kingdoms of the old Roman Empire (Dan. 7:7-8, 23-24) and from the four divisions of the Grecian Empire (Dan. 8:7-9, 20-23), but also that he'll come from the Syrian division, as plainly taught in Dan. 11. The king of the north in this chapter is Syria; the king of the south is Egypt. Wars between these two divisions of Greece are pictured in Dan. 11:5-34. Verses 35-45 portray war between these same kingdoms "at the time of the end," showing the result of the last war between them. It states that Egypt shall not escape the king of the north in this last war, identifying Syria as the country from which Antichrist must come. If the king of the north were Russia, as many Bible students now teach, how could Dan. 11:44 be fulfilled? What countries are north of Russia that could fight against her, as this verse requires? There are no countries north of Russia, proving the king of the north can't be Russia. When we understand this refers to the northern division of the Grecian Empire — Syria — everything in the passage is clear. The Antichrist definitely comes from Syria and is called the king of the north who will fulfill all of Dan. 8:22-23; 11:35-45; 12:1-7.

supplements.title: Supplement 9
supplements.content:
# Supplement Nine

## For Lessons Seventeen and Eighteen

These last two lessons wrap up "Part II: The Historical Dealings of God With Man." What a journey we've taken together! We've explored so many foundational subjects — the Bible and how to interpret it, the truth about God, His original creations, Satan and the spirit-world, the rebellion and overthrow of the first social system, and the story of re-creation.

We've walked through the Dispensations of Innocence, Conscience, Human Government, Promise, and Law. We've also studied God's plan for meeting human needs, why those needs often go unrealized, the Old Testament Church, divine healing and health, and asking and receiving from God.

By now, you should have a clear understanding of these subjects and how to access the material, physical, and spiritual blessings of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God. All these studies of the historical past will serve as a firm foundation for our future studies on God's present and future dealings with mankind.

Here's the exciting part: the richest studies of your life are yet to come! Lesson after lesson will grow richer and clearer concerning God's plan for you. You'll be able to conform more and more to the total will of God until you become hidden with Christ in God — able to exercise full authority as a son of God with power.

## Satanic Opposition Must Be Understood

As we continue our lessons, we need to do more than just reveal truths that may be new to you. We also need to warn you about old errors that have hindered Christians for centuries.

One of the greatest errors in the Christian world today is accepting any opposition to answered prayer — or any delay in answers — as the will of God. Think of it like this: if you asked your loving father for something he'd already promised you, and someone kept blocking the delivery, would you assume your father had changed his mind? Of course not! You'd realize someone else was causing the problem.

Let me share an example. I recently received a letter from a woman saying she knew it wasn't God's will to heal her insane son because she had believed God and He didn't heal the boy. She said God's answer was simply "No." In other words, because her prayer wasn't granted, she concluded it must not be God's will to heal her son.

There's not one scriptural word to justify this kind of reasoning. This is a plain deception by the devil, making her believe it's not God's will to heal her boy and that she should just accept it. The devil has not only robbed God of the glory He would receive from such a healing, but he's kept his victim insane and deceived a mother into thinking this is how God answers prayer.

The truth is that it *is* God's will to heal this boy and deliver this mother from such slanderous concepts of God. This kind of thinking actually accuses God of being a liar — of not answering prayer as He promised, of being unmerciful and hard-hearted with no sympathy for mother or child, of sending Jesus to die to bring deliverance and then deliberately refusing to provide what Christ died for, and of not meeting His children's faith according to His promises.

You can believe these fallacies if you want, but we warn you — these are some of the very reasons prayer goes unanswered. As long as the devil can deceive people this way, he can hold them in bondage and rob them of the benefits for which Christ died. If we're ever going to receive answers to prayer, we need to learn what to do and intelligently proceed according to that knowledge.

The devil knows the individual disposition of each person. He wages spiritual war against each one according to what he thinks will be the best way to keep that person from getting what they want from God. Satan stops at nothing to achieve his goals. He has no righteous principle about him and no respect for God or any person.

He's out to defeat both God and man, and he has a distinct advantage in some ways. People are born in sin and spiritually dead — sons of the devil by choice and lifestyle. We're born with a natural tendency toward sin, selfishness, doubt, and unbelief.

The Bible declares that God's ways and thoughts are higher than ours as the heavens are higher than the Earth (Isa. 55:9). So naturally, those born in sin contrary to these ways will have some difficulty conforming wholly to them. The carnal mind is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be (Rom 8:7-8).

The new birth is necessary to enable us to conform by nature to God's ways (Jn. 3:3-5; 2 Cor. 5:17). This is why natural people, who are more in harmony with Satan and sin by birth than with God and His ways, experience conflict with spiritual things. It also explains why there's a natural conflict between God and Satan, and between God and the natural person.

We shouldn't expect anyone to learn God's ways by nature or get prayers answered without opposition from Satan. We shouldn't expect there will be no conflict with carnal desires, unbelief, and doubts in the process of getting prayers answered from God.

Paul speaks of being "hindered" many times by Satan in his spiritual work (1 Thess. 2:18). Peter speaks of prayers being "hindered" even by wrong living on the part of those who pray (1 Pet. 3:7). If this is true, then it's even more true that Satan and supernatural evil spirits can and do hinder prayers.

We're instructed to give no "place to the devil" (Eph. 4:27), to "stand against the wiles of the devil" (Eph. 6:10-18), know "his devices" (2 Cor. 2:11), and "resist the devil" (Jas. 4:7; 1 Pet. 5:8). All these facts prove that people who pray to God should expect opposition and all kinds of hindrances to their prayers.

How foolish it is to accept such opposition as God's will or as an indication that God won't grant what's been asked according to His promises!

Here's something important to understand: the reason many followers of false religions seem to get great benefits without opposition is that they're in league with the devil and deceived by him. Why would the devil oppose people he's already deceived, who are already safe in his snare and doomed to Hell with him? That wouldn't be to his advantage.

It's actually wise strategy on his part to help people get some benefits in order to keep them deceived. If he can get anyone to reject the true God and the essentials of salvation through Christ, he'll gladly cooperate by giving that person certain benefits or removing opposition to blessings that come to all by divine providence. He'll even heal or remove sickness from people to deceive them into thinking some false religion is the true one.

It's only when someone becomes aggressive against Satan and turns to God for help that demons wage war against that person. They do everything in their power to defeat anyone trying to receive the benefits of the gospel. They'll work on your feelings, hinder your faith in God, cause doubt and questioning, and obstruct in every conceivable way those who seek God by faith for the benefits promised in His Word.

This must be thoroughly understood. You must make up your mind to go ahead and get God's blessings in spite of satanic opposition.

## Necessary Steps to Follow When Faith Seems Weak and Victory Seems Lost

> 1. Recognize the source of the opposition as being of Satan.

> 2. Be sure that the promises of God cover the thing asked for.

> 3. Be sure that you are not living in open or secret sin.

> 4. Be sure that no doubt or unbelief is permitted in the life concerning the promises.

> 5. Sincerely desire the benefit that you ask of God.

> 6. Ask of God in faith, nothing wavering, believing that what is asked is yours.

> 7. Do not tolerate for one single moment a thought to the contrary.

> 8. Count the thing done that you have asked.

> 9. Give glory to God even before you receive what you have asked.

> 10. Act as if you have received what you have asked.

Remember this: the moment you doubt, or wonder why God hasn't answered, or look around for some reason why He didn't hear your prayer, or begin accepting the delay as God's will for you not to have what you asked — you're defeated. You've failed to hold firm in unwavering faith for the answer.

Never under any circumstance question God's will in the matter of answered prayer. If He has promised you can have whatsoever you ask if you don't doubt, then don't for a moment think it may be God's will to withhold what He promised and what you asked for in faith.

It's always God's will for you to ask and receive what you will. Don't make the mistake multiplied thousands are making — constantly wondering and wavering concerning God's will in anything He has promised. If you faithfully follow the steps listed above, settle on what God's will is according to His Word, and hold to it through all opposition, you cannot fail.

In the final analysis, the answer to your prayer depends on you more than on God. Why? Because God doesn't fail or change — there's no possible failure on His part. The only possible failure is on your part. It's similar to a power outlet: the electricity is always there, always available, always ready. The outlet never fails. If something doesn't work, you check the connection on your end.

We're so used to living by feelings instead of faith and being swayed by circumstances instead of counting things that are not as though they were. We don't always doggedly hold to the necessary steps in answered prayer. Make sure you don't fail, and then there won't be even a faint possibility of failure.

## Some Mistaken Ideas Among Christians

The average Christian not only misses the mark by failing to understand the laws of answered prayer — or knowing them but not obeying them faithfully — but also holds to various fallacies that hinder prayer:

**1. That if a person does the best he can, God will not permit harm to befall him.**

Sometimes the so-called "best one can do" isn't enough to please God or ward off demon attacks. Our performance can't always be adequate because we lack the knowledge to conform intelligently to God's will. People frequently think they're doing their best when they're really putting forth all the effort possible to live as they please and be as much like the world as they can while maintaining a little religion.

In other words, the great effort is holding on to as much religion as possible while living a life of pleasure and selfishness. The best a person can do — if they really want to do their best — is forsake all sin, selfishness, worldliness, and everything displeasing to God, giving themselves wholly to God's total will according to the Scriptures. Until this happens, you haven't done and aren't doing your best. Using "doing your best" as an excuse actually covers up failure to please God according to the light you've received.

Even if you're fully consecrated, you shouldn't become careless by assuming you're eternally safe from all evil and harm. If you truly want to be kept from harm, you must watch and pray and live as taught in the 91st Psalm. You must bring your body under subjection and crucify the lusts of the flesh (1 Cor. 9:27; Rom. 6:1-23; 8:1-13; Col. 3:5-7; Eph. 4:22-24).

It's folly to talk about doing your best until you've done this and continue doing it. In that state, you can claim the full protection of God's promises. But the minute you go back into sin and rebellion, you can't claim that no evil shall befall you. "Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap" applies to the saved as well as the unsaved.

There's no place in God where you're forced to continue in righteousness contrary to your will. God cannot and will not keep one saved who continues in sin. That's one of the most ungodly and unscriptural theories Satan has manufactured. God works according to certain fixed laws, and when they're broken, penalties must be paid. Protection is promised only to those who fear the Lord (Ps. 34:7-10; 91:1-6).

**2. Another fallacy is that God will not allow an honest heart to be deceived.**

This is itself a deception, based on ignorance of the fall of man and a misconception of how God and the devil work. God doesn't work except by spiritual and natural laws that must be understood and obeyed to receive His help.

Eve was as honest as could be, yet she was deceived (1 Tim. 2:14). Other honest people have been deceived through the ages. If God wouldn't permit honest people to be deceived, we wouldn't have so many backslidings, divisions, and differences in doctrines today.

**3. Another false idea is that if a person is wrong in doctrine or manifestation, God won't use or bless them.**

The fact is that God blesses anyone to the extent of their faith and uses anyone to the extent of their yieldedness to Him. God has used sinners to further His plan on Earth, and they were certainly wrong in both doctrine and life.

God used Pharaoh in Egypt (Rom. 9), Cyrus in Persia (Isa. 44:28–45:1), Nebuchadnezzar in Babylon (Dan. 2), and many other sinners. So the theory that God's using someone proves they're saved and in perfect harmony with Him is wrong. God even used a donkey once, and we know it wasn't saved from its sins or correct in doctrine (Num. 22:22-35).

Many good ministers today are used by God in saving souls, yet they're ignorant concerning certain truths. Don't be deceived by this fallacy.

**4. Another fallacy is that God won't permit the devil to afflict a child of God with sickness without giving special permission, and therefore God is the cause of sickness and His children shouldn't rebel against any sickness He sends.**

The argument used to prove this is Job's affliction. However, remember that God was in the process of making His written Word and used Job's experience to teach all people of all ages certain truths — such as the cause of sickness, patience when attacked by Satan, and how God delivers His children from the devil's power.

Scores of truths have been learned from Job's experience through the ages. Naturally God permitted Job to go through this test to purify, humble, and refine him — just as God teaches everyone lessons in times of trouble. But despite this, God cannot be charged with causing this trouble or any other troubles.

Job didn't have the many promises or revelations we have today. He didn't have one book of either Testament showing him God's ways or giving assurance of help in trouble. We have not only Job's book but the entire Bible teaching us that Satan causes sickness and God delivers His people.

The book of Job teaches why people are in trouble. This is clear from Job 33:14-30: "God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not. In a dream, in a vision of the night . . . Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction, that he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man." This passage continues explaining that man is brought low with sickness because of waywardness, and when he finds "an interpreter" to show him that God doesn't desire man to be lost or in pain, then God is gracious and delivers him from Satan.

This fallacy is partly true — God does permit people to be afflicted with bodily pain by Satan to hide pride from them. But this permission doesn't mean God causes the sickness. The foolishness, sin, and pride of man in breaking natural or spiritual laws is the cause. The devil is the direct power executing the law of reaping upon people the ills they've sown.

God permits affliction simply because He doesn't directly break His own law of sowing and reaping. He permits it by not acting to keep Satan from his work in people. It's not God's will for anyone to get into these troubles. If sin had never entered, all would have remained sinless and healthy. If all would submit to God, they could be made free from sin and sickness — this is the very truth God teaches in Job's story and throughout Scripture.

The trouble with true Christians who get sick is failure to understand they're directly attacked by the devil and need faith in God for healing. Almost every one has no consciousness of definite sin committed or rebellion against God's will. Such sickness is plainly a direct attack of Satan, and all that's needed is a vigorous refusal to stay sick and tolerate the devil's work.

If such people would quit blaming God and cooperate with Him against Satan through the gospel, they could experience deliverance. God's permission for them to be sick shouldn't enter their minds. The trouble isn't the idea of His permission — it's their will to be sick.

If you want to be healed, do what God tells you to do for deliverance. Rebuke Satan and take a stand against the devil's work. Yield to God and believe the gospel so God's work can be manifest. Jesus said, "The thief [Satan] cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life and have it more abundantly" (Jn. 10:10).

There's no excuse for defeat for any child of God since Jesus came to give the more abundant life. Review Lessons Ten, Twelve, and Fourteen which prove that God is the deliverer of the sick, sinful, poor, and needy — not their oppressor. With all Scripture's truths written since Job lived, we can all get deliverance from Satan's power and live victoriously over sin, sickness, poverty, and failure.

**5. Other misconceptions are seen everywhere in Christendom.**

The whole Christian world is full of false concepts of God and His truth, as we'll see in future studies. People in error of any kind are hindered in spiritual growth and often become autocratic, proud, and fanatical before others — unable to act rightly until some calamity strikes to disillusion them and dispel their false ideas.

From the beginning of your Christian experience, recognize that demons are real and can deceive honest believers. Understand the methods by which demons work. Know that no place should be given them in life or conduct and that no doctrine should be accepted from them in any form.

## The Source of All Christian Opposition

Demons constantly wage war on saints to instill false concepts about God and make them weak, nervous, discouraged, and ruined spiritually, physically, and materially. They do their utmost to keep people in poverty and failure to bring reproach upon God and His gospel.

They must be resisted and rejected once and forever if complete victory is to be gained (Jas. 4:7; Eph. 4:10-15; 1 Pet. 5:8-9; 2 Cor. 10:4-7).

## Christian Duties in View of Satanic Opposition

In coming lessons, we'll try to make the entire truth of God clear to you on every subject. We want to enable you to rightly divide the Word of Truth, detect demon workings, and confute all error.

The days ahead are rich in blessings according to the knowledge of God. "Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free" (Jn. 8:31-32).

Constantly affirm your faith in God and vigorously deny all fallacy and demon workings — everything contrary to the plain written Word of God. You have a great part in making yourself free. Your part is knowing the truth and cooperating with it in Christ by the power of the Holy Spirit living in you by faith.

Give no place to the devil. If opposition comes when you try to attain the gospel's blessings, don't be confused. Recognize it as the devil's work and as proof you're on the right path to God and His wonderful benefits. Don't fail God, and He won't fail you. Draw near to Him, and He will draw near to you (Jas. 4:6-11).

Intelligently follow truth as you learn it, regardless of opposition, and you'll be richly rewarded — not only here but in all eternity.

Let's all constantly affirm that we believe in God and the gospel of His Son, Jesus Christ; that we know we're saved from sin and bad habits; that we're healed through faith in Jesus Christ; that we have what we've asked of God; that we're being blessed in material things of this life; that our faith is increasing according to the knowledge of God; and that we're getting definite results through obedience to Truth.

If there's any lack of surrender of our lives to God, or any sin in our lives, it must be judged and put under the blood of Jesus Christ. This alone will hinder prayers, as absolute faith cannot be exercised when there's known rebellion in the heart. See to it that this is corrected. Without any question, believe and affirm truth from the heart, and faith will bring results.

## Ten Questions For Supplement Nine. Do You Know:

- **That the Antichrist will never be assassinated?** Many Bible teachers say the head on the beast in Rev. 13:3 being wounded to death and healed symbolizes the assassination of the personal Antichrist. They believe he'll remain dead for three days and that Satan will resurrect him and incarnate himself in him, imitating Christ's death, burial, and resurrection. For some unknown reason without scriptural authority, they make the Antichrist opposite to Christ in almost every detail. This isn't necessary to believe in the coming Antichrist — the word simply means "against Christ." There's no need to manufacture theories. Sufficient prophecy exists concerning him without all this. The head wounded to death simply refers to an empire's destruction and its revival as the eighth and last empire to oppose Jesus Christ at His second advent. The seven heads on the beast are explained as seven kingdoms: five had fallen before John's day; one was in his day; the seventh was to come after his day, made up of ten kingdoms inside the Roman Empire — but that was not the beast itself, which is explained to be "the eighth" of the eight kingdoms symbolized by the beast and its seven heads (Rev. 17:8-17; Dan. 7:23-24). If the heads symbolize kingdoms, not personal kings, then the head wounded to death doesn't refer to the Antichrist's assassination but to the destruction of one kingdom and its revival as the eighth. According to Dan. 7:11; Isa. 11:4; 2 Thess. 2:8-9, the Antichrist will be a mortal man up to the very day Christ comes to Earth, when he'll be killed. This disproves the theory of the Antichrist being killed and resurrected as an immortal man during the last 3½ years before Christ comes, as would be required if he were the head wounded to death and healed.

- **That Antichrist will never reign from Rome or any great capital of the present world?** According to Isa. 13:6, 9-13; 14:1-11; Zech. 5:5-11; Rev. 16:17-21; 18:1-24 and other passages, the city of Babylon will be rebuilt and be the Antichrist's capital during the first 3½ years of Daniel's Seventieth Week. In the middle of that week, he'll break his covenant with the Jews, enter Palestine, and make Jerusalem his capital for most of the last half of the Week (Dan. 9:27; 11:40-45; 2 Thess. 2:3-4; Rev. 13). We've already proven in Supplement Eight, Questions 9 and 10, that the Antichrist will come from Syria. Therefore, his capital will be Syria's capital in the days of these prophecies' fulfillment — which will be Babylon, as proven in the above passages.

- **That Antichrist will never be an incarnation of Satan?** This isn't taught anywhere in Scripture. Satan is a separate person from the Antichrist and merely gives his power to him (2 Thess. 2:7-12; Dan. 11:35-39; Rev. 13:1-4). The dragon (Satan), the beast out of the sea (Antichrist), and the beast out of the Earth (false prophet) are always seen as three separate persons throughout Revelation from when they're mentioned (Rev. 12:1-17; 13:1-18; 19:19-21; 20:1-10). Satan is never incarnated in anyone and doesn't need to be to dominate a person. Teaching that Antichrist is an incarnation of Satan is unscriptural. It would be impossible, for Satan — an angel with a real spirit-body — cannot get inside any man bodily. If he did, he couldn't exist as a separate person outside that body. Because he's always a separate person from any man who has lived or will live, it's best to forget such a theory.

- **That the personal Antichrist will never come from the bottomless pit?** This is clear because he'll be a mortal man up to Christ's second coming, as proven in Point I above. No mortal man could live in the bottomless pit. It isn't even the place for departed human spirits, as is clear wherever it's mentioned in Scripture. The beast that ascends out of the bottomless pit is an angelic being and supernatural spirit working with the human Antichrist, causing him to gain power over the ten kingdoms yet to form inside the Roman Empire (Dan. 7:23-24; Rev. 17:8-17). According to Dan. 10:11-21, there are angelic beings ruling every kingdom of this world. Three things are symbolized in the one symbol — the beast of Revelation: first, the spirit of the Antichrist out of the abyss (Rev. 11:7; 17:8); second, the human Antichrist, a man (Rev. 13:18); third, the kingdom of the human Antichrist and the spirit out of the pit, the eighth kingdom of Rev. 17:8-17. It's the angelic spirit backing the Antichrist's rise to power that comes out of the pit, not the personal, human Antichrist. See Lesson Forty-seven.

- **That Antichrist will not reign over America or be a worldwide dictator?** This is proven by these facts:

> (1) He will reign over only ten kingdoms yet to form inside the old Roman Empire (Dan. 7:23-24). Only these ten kingdoms will give their power and kingdom to him (Rev. 13:1; 17:12-17).

> (2) Certain countries will escape his rule (Dan. 11:40-44).

> (3) Certain countries will make war on him at the very time he's supposed to be ruling the whole world (Dan. 11:40-44).

> (4) Because he'll reign only over ten countries inside Roman Empire territory, because America was never inside that territory and never will be, and because certain countries escape him, we can scripturally conclude he won't rule America or be a worldwide ruler. See Lesson Forty-eight, Point III.

- **That multitudes of people of many nations will never take the mark of the beast and they will never be killed by the Antichrist for not doing so?** It's been proven in the point above that many nations won't be under the Antichrist. Naturally they won't have to take his mark, and they won't be killed because they won't be ruled by him. The Bible speaks of many peoples of all nations left after the Antichrist's reign, people who'll be alive in the Millennium and go up yearly to worship God (Zech. 14:16-21). This further limits the Antichrist's kingdom to part of the world and limits his ability to kill everyone who doesn't take the mark even in his own empire. According to Rev. 14:9-11, no one who has taken the mark will go into the Millennium but will be sent to eternal Hell. That multitudes go into the Millennium proves they haven't taken the mark and haven't been killed, contrary to what many Bible students teach. If Antichrist kills every person on Earth who doesn't take his mark and the Lord sends to eternal Hell everyone who does take it, no one would be left to enter the Millennium. This would contradict Zech. 14:16-21; Mt. 25:31-46; Isa. 2:1-4 and many other Scriptures.

- **That many nations of the Earth will be able to buy and sell and yet not be killed for not taking the mark of the beast?** The old theory that every person in every nation won't be able to buy or sell unless they take the mark is false, as proven by facts and Scriptures in the last two points above. The law that people won't be able to buy or sell applies only to kindreds, tongues, and nations inside the ten kingdoms yet to form inside the old Roman Empire. If the Antichrist reigns only over these countries, then all nations outside his ten kingdoms will be able to buy and sell and won't be affected by any law he makes in his kingdom.

- **That multitudes of people living on the Earth will never even know that Antichrist has been on the Earth until he has come and gone?** It's a known fact that over half the people on Earth today have never heard the name of Jesus Christ or His gospel. It's also well-known that multitudes in interior Africa, South America, Australia, India, Tibet, and many parts of the Earth don't know there have been two world wars in a generation. It will equally be true that these people out of touch with so-called civilization will never know there's been an Antichrist and great tribulation until Christ has come and resurrected saints of all ages extend Christ's kingdom from Jerusalem to the ends of the Earth in the Millennium. It's utter folly to teach that a human being called the Antichrist ruling only ten kingdoms inside Roman Empire territory can take over every part of the Earth and literally kill everybody who doesn't take his mark. This would be teaching that one human being is going to do more in forty-two months or 1,260 literal days in establishing his religion in every person on Earth than God, Jesus Christ, the Holy Spirit, and the whole Church have done in over nineteen centuries to spread the gospel. Are we going to believe the Antichrist as a man is greater than God and all other men who've ever lived? The sensible thing to believe is that Antichrist will be a mere man limited in his rule to only part of the Earth and that he can't make laws affecting anyone outside his kingdom.

- **That the mark of the beast is not 666?** Three brands will be available for people to choose from in the days of the Antichrist inside his kingdom: his name, his mark, and the number of his name (Rev. 13:16-18). The name and mark of this man are not given in any Scripture, so no one will know what they are until he comes and people learn his name and what mark he chooses for his followers. The only one of these three brands given is the number of his name — stated as 666 in Rev. 13:18. So 666 is not the name or the mark of the beast. It is the number of the beast.

- **That at this time no man knows who the Antichrist will be?** This is true because he's coming "after" the ten kingdoms form inside the old Roman Empire territory (Dan. 7:23-24) and "after" the rapture of the Church, or "after" the one who hinders lawlessness has been removed from the Earth (2 Thess. 2:7-8).

supplements.title: Supplement 10
supplements.content:
# Supplement Ten

## For Lessons Nineteen and Twenty

In the first two lessons of PART III, *God's Present Dealings With Man*, we've seen that the fullness of grace and power came to humanity through Jesus Christ. God freely gives both to everyone who believes!

We've worked through many misconceptions—as well as truths—about God's grace and His plan for this present age. We've also explored the New Testament program for the modern church and discovered something exciting: there are no limitations on believers getting and using God's power in this life. We can operate just as Jesus Christ and the apostles did.

In this supplement, I want to show you how you can receive God's power in your life—and what to do once you have it.

## Commands to Get the Power of God

Here's something important: it's a definite command for all believers to receive God's power so they're equipped to witness for Christ.

In Acts 1:4-5, the disciples—not just the apostles—were commanded not to leave Jerusalem until they received the baptism in the Spirit: "And being assembled together with them, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which, saith he, ye have heard of me. For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence."

Being baptized with the Holy Ghost and receiving power are the same thing. When you're baptized in the Spirit in all fullness, you receive power: "But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me . . . unto the uttermost part of the earth" (Acts 1:8).

In Lk. 24:49, Christ commanded them: "Behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem until ye be endued with power from on high."

Now, are all believers throughout this age also commanded to tarry until they're endued with power from on High? Absolutely—and this passage makes it clear: "Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world" (Mt. 28:19-20).

Jesus also taught that we should ask the Father for the Holy Spirit: "If ye, then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him" (Lk. 11:11-13).

## How to Get the Power of God

To receive the Holy Spirit baptism, you must thirst, drink, and believe. Jesus made this clear: "In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me and drink. He that believeth on me as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.
