(But this spake he of the Spirit which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because Jesus was not yet glorified.)"

This passage (Jn. 7:37-39) can only refer to the fullness of the Holy Spirit received on the day of Pentecost. In Acts 2:33, we read about Jesus being glorified and pouring out the Holy Spirit upon the Church in baptismal measure. Up to that point, people had received the Spirit in a measure, as we saw in Lesson Nineteen, I, 3.

You must "hunger and thirst after righteousness" to be filled (Mt. 5:6).

So let's be clear: this enduement of power is for people who are already children of God—not for sinners (Jn. 14:17). If you're a believer, born-again and part of God's family, then you need to tarry, wait, hunger, thirst, drink, believe, and receive the Spirit.

## Men Must Still Tarry for the Spirit Baptism

You might have heard the theory that believers don't need to wait for the Holy Spirit today because He's already come—that all you have to do is receive Him. That's only part of the truth.

Yes, you must receive Him. But until He's given to you personally, you don't have Him. Until He's received and fills your innermost being, you have to wait until it's done.

Think of it like this: you can't just claim a gift that hasn't been opened yet. You can believe it's coming, and that faith is necessary—but to be satisfied without actually having Him would leave you with an empty life. It's impossible to receive such a mighty enduement of power, with rivers of living water flowing out of you, without knowing it!

All the terms we've used—hunger, thirst, tarry, wait—prove there's a time element involved, even if it's just a few moments. If the Spirit were given before you hungered or thirsted, you couldn't have those passions. If He were given before you received, there would be no faith to hope for what we don't yet see—and no expectancy at all.

Consider this: 120 disciples were in the temple continually praising and blessing God for ten days before the Spirit came (Acts 1:3, 12-14; 2:1; Lk. 24:49-53). Now that the Spirit has come into the world to bless people in all fullness, there's no need to wait for Him to arrive in the world. But you still need to go through whatever period of preparation is necessary before He personally takes control of your life.

So approach it this way: the Spirit is already here. He wants personal control of your life. It takes yieldedness, asking God, and hungering and thirsting until you're literally filled with the fullness of God. As soon as you learn to yield to the Spirit and receive Him into your life, that's when the Spirit manifests Himself.

## How to Work the Works of God

Here's something crucial to understand: as a Christian, you always remain a free moral agent. You can help or hinder God's work in various ways.

Certainly, no one would knowingly want to hinder God's work. But sometimes people do things ignorantly, even with the best intentions. Studying the Bible is one of the best ways to help God's work—then you'll know what to do in every circumstance. You won't be doing things wrong when you act.

Whatever measure of the Spirit you receive, use it to the glory of God!

You might ask, "What is the work of God?" Jesus answered that question: "This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent" (Jn. 6:28-29).

To believe on Christ as Scripture describes is the greatest work of God that anyone can do. Jesus said if you do this—and do it right—you'll have rivers of living water flowing from your innermost being. You'll be able to do even greater works than He did (Jn. 7:37-39; 14:12-15).

## What Does it Mean to Fully Believe?

> 1. To believe that all the promises of God are yea and amen to all (2 Cor. 1:20).

> 2. To believe that all things are possible to the believer (Mk. 9:23).

> 3. To believe that all things we ask in prayer we receive (Mt. 7:7-11; 21:22).

> 4. To believe that even all of our desires will be granted (Mk. 11:22-24).

> 5. To believe that we can ask what we will, and it will be given (Jn. 15:7).

> 6. To believe in those things that be not as though they were (Rom. 4:17).

> 7. To believe that God is a rewarder of them that diligently seek Him (Heb. 11:6).

> 8. To believe that God gives liberally and that he does not upbraid or rebuke when we come to him for anything (Jas. 1:5-8).

> 9. To believe without ever a question or a waver that what is asked is done (Jas. 1:5-8).

> 10. To believe that it is the will of God to give what He has promised (1 Jn. 3:22).

> 11. To believe that Christ took all sickness and sin in His own body on the cross and that we do not have to bear them one minute after we accept His vicarious work (Mt. 8:16-17; 1 Pet. 2:24; Isa. 53:4-5).

> 12. To believe that every believer can do the same works of God that Christ did and that God's power in the baptism of the Spirit is the same today as when Christ and the early believers received it (Jn. 14:12-15; Lk. 24:49; Acts 1:8; 2:38-39; 5:32).

To believe all this—and every other part of Bible doctrine and practice—really means something! If someone says believing isn't work, let them try to believe all of this and fully experience it without any effort whatsoever.

The very fact that we're spending so much time writing about these things from every angle, and that we have to answer every unbelieving argument from people who try to deny these truths, is proof enough that believing takes work from all of us.

Here's the simple truth: if you can truly and wholeheartedly believe these things, God will abundantly meet you, and the Holy Spirit will work through you without limitations.

We cannot overemphasize this: you must believe these things before you see the results you want to see. That's why we're going into repeated detail—so this fact can finally dawn on all of us: God means what He says and says what He means about power from God, just as He does about forgiveness of sins (which we've all been taught to believe).

Study these lessons and supplements over and over, especially the Scriptures that naturally build up faith. Never doubt or waver about having all things possible. It doesn't matter what church, pastor, or religious zealot says about any theory to the contrary—if God has said these things, His Word is all-sufficient.

People may argue that these powers aren't for us today, or that we're not seeing them, or that it's impossible to see them. But by now, you know better than to believe such arguments.

We can all get into the New Testament program if we follow the example of the early Church. We know what they did—the record is clear. We know we don't have this program in full operation in our churches today. We also know why: we're not putting into practice the things that made the early Church such a dynamic force.

So why not change our methods? Let's do what the early Church did and see what can be accomplished! If we want what they got, let's go to God to get it. Let's be honest and quit claiming we have what they had until we actually do.

It's similar to claiming you've run a marathon when you've only trained for it—you haven't actually crossed the finish line yet. Let's use plain common sense and realize that anything short of what they received isn't exactly what they received.

If we desire what they got, let's go to God for it in all fullness—the way they received it—and not be satisfied without it. If we don't want what they got, then let's not complain when we don't get it.

When we finally receive what they received, it will produce the same works it did for them—and we'll know it just as surely as they knew it. We'll have the same power and manifestation of God that we read about in the New Testament, and then we'll know the New Testament program is in full operation again.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Ten. Do You Know:

- That the Antichrist will never bring universal peace and prosperity so that the world will worship him and take his mark? All Scriptures teach contrary to this common theory. He will make a seven-year covenant of peace with Israel and by peace destroy many of them (Dan. 8:25; 9:27; Mt. 24:15-26). But among other nations, he goes forth conquering and is at war from his rise to power until Christ defeats him at Armageddon (Dan. 7:8, 24; 8:20-25; 9:27; 11:40-45; Rev. 13:4-7; 17:9-17; 19:11-21; Zech. 14:1-21). He will cause craft (cunning, or deceit) to prosper in his hand, but he cannot bring universal material prosperity—he doesn't rule the world, as we saw in Supplement Nine, Question 8. He personally prospers through war like any other conqueror, but even that is limited to his territory, as in any other war (Dan. 8:24; 11:35-45).

- That multitudes of people will not know Jesus Christ has come to Earth until after His second advent and the resurrected saints begin taking over world governments in the Millennium? This is clear from the facts in Supplement Nine, Question 8, and because Scriptures definitely say many people will not see His glory or person the day He comes to Earth (Isa. 66:19). Only when Christ is reigning and missionaries and rulers go from Jerusalem to tell of Christ reigning in Zion will many nations say, "Let us go up to the mountain of the Lord" (Isa. 2:2-4; 52:7; Zech. 8:23). Only in the immediate vicinity of Jerusalem will every eye see Him at the second advent, which is pictured "as lightning" shining under only one part of Heaven (Mt. 24:27-31; Rev. 1:7).

- That the Bible does not teach a general judgment for both the righteous and the wicked? Scripture teaches that the righteous will be resurrected and reign 1,000 years before the wicked are resurrected and judged (Rev. 20:1-15). The righteous will be judged and rewarded at the Judgment Seat of Christ in Heaven before returning with Christ to reign (2 Cor. 10:9-11; Rom. 14:10-11). Saints will already know their rewards and positions in the eternal kingdom before coming back to Earth with Christ, for when they come, they'll receive those positions (Mt. 16:27). Anyone can see there are 1,000 years between the end of this age when Christ comes (Mt. 16:27; 24:29-31; 25:31-46) and the end of the Millennium (Rev. 20)—so there's at least that much time between the two judgments.

- That saints will be judged for sins committed in this life? "For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad" (2 Cor. 5:10-11; Rom. 14:10-11; Mt. 12:36; 16:27).

- That there will be degrees of punishment in Hell and rewards in Heaven? Degrees of punishment in Hell are clear from Mt. 10:15; 11:22; 12:41; 23:14; Mk. 6:11; 12:40; Lk. 10:14; 11:31-32; 20:47. Degrees of reward in Heaven are clear from Mt. 16:27; 1 Cor. 3:11-15; Rom. 2:6; 14:10; 2 Cor. 5:9-11; 2 Tim. 4:14; Rev. 2:23; 22:12.

- That there are five horsemen of the first four seals of Revelation instead of four as generally taught? There are four horses mentioned—white, red, black, and pale (green)—and then the account says "Hell followed with him," following the green horse. If there are four with Hell following the fourth, there must be five. Both Death and Hell are referred to as separate entities, so they must be symbolized by two separate horsemen (Rev. 6:8).

- That the ten virgins do not represent any class of Christians today? These were ten young ladies who lived in Christ's day—this is clear from the parable itself. Christ used this actual wedding event to illustrate "watchfulness" in the kingdom of Heaven at His second advent after the Tribulation. That's the point He was making, as Mt. 25:13 shows: "Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh." In other words, you people in the kingdom of Heaven or realm of profession—where tares and wheat, good and bad, exist together as taught in Mt. 13—should learn from those ten virgins and not be slumbering and sleeping as they were. Every Christian living today will be either dead or alive at the rapture, and at that time every Christian will go to Heaven before the Tribulation. They won't be on Earth after the Tribulation but will return with Christ to set up an eternal kingdom (Zech. 14:5; Jude 14). The first word in the parable is "Then," proving the connection is to the second coming after the Tribulation, not to the rapture before it—so this parable couldn't refer to any class of Christians today who will go before the Tribulation.

- That the seals, trumpets, and vials of Revelation are yet future? This is clear because all events of Rev. 4-22 must be fulfilled after the rapture of the Church, as proven in Supplement Six, Question 6. If true, then none of the seals, trumpets, vials, or other events of Rev. 4-22 will happen before the rapture. Since the rapture is future and these events must occur afterward, they're also future.

- That the sun-clothed woman of Rev. 12 does not represent the Church? This is very clear, since every event from Rev. 4:1 onward must occur after the churches, as proven in Point 8 above and in Lesson Six, Question 6. Because the woman symbolizes something left on Earth after the rapture, she couldn't symbolize the Church—the Church is gone by then. She symbolizes national Israel, as we'll see in Lesson Forty-five.

- That the manchild of Rev. 12:5 does not symbolize Christ or the Church? The simple reason is that everything from Rev. 4:1 onward must be fulfilled after the rapture of the Church. Rev. 1:1 says the entire book concerns things yet to come to pass from the time John saw them—so bringing the historical ascension of Christ into these prophetic future events is unscriptural. See Lesson Forty-five for further proof.

supplements.title: Supplement 11
supplements.content:
# Supplement Eleven

## For Lessons Twenty-one and Twenty-two

We've devoted most of this supplement to exploring the doctrine of Jesus Christ and the Christian's power of attorney — and for good reason. These truths are absolutely essential.

Without truly grasping who Jesus is and how to use the power He gives every believer, we simply can't experience the full manifestation of Christ's power in our individual lives or in the Church as a whole.

## The Gospel is the Same Today as Ever

Let's be clear about something important: the name of Jesus has not lost any of its power. There's a foolish doctrine floating around that claims the power of Jesus' name was only for the apostolic period or for some future kingdom when Christ reigns.

We need to reject that idea once and for all.

When you read the New Testament passages about the name of Jesus, you won't find a single statement limiting His power to any certain period or particular group. Christ promised to be with us "even to the end of the world" (Mt. 28:19-20), and believers throughout this age were to "observe all things" that Christ commanded the apostles.

## Gospel Benefits are Personal

If certain churches don't want these benefits — if they want to cooperate with the devil and become tools to destroy faith in God and His Word — that's their responsibility. They are the losers, both now and for eternity.

But here's the beautiful truth: even if every other person in the world rejects the gospel, you can still receive every promised benefit. Why? Because every promise is personal, not national.

Each person can and does receive exactly what they have faith for — no more, no less.

## All Men Can Have God's Power Now

The name of Jesus and all the power behind it is for us right now! It's our legal right to use this power today.

That name belongs to everyone who is baptized into Christ (Gal. 3:27-29; Rom. 6:1-8). This works now. All things can be ours for the asking if we have faith and don't doubt.

Think of it like this: every church could automatically become a channel for God's power if they would simply teach what the gospel says about this subject. But sadly, in the average church, men and women are groping in darkness, pleading with God for faith, bemoaning their weakness, and confessing their inability to meet the crisis of the hour.

If they only knew the truth about New Testament power in the name of Jesus, they would immediately become powerful in Christ.

## The Sevenfold Right to Use the Name of Jesus

> 1. **IT IS OUR GOSPEL RIGHT.** The gospel is still the power of God unto salvation to everyone that believes (Rom. 1:16). The word "salvation" here means full deliverance by God's power from sin, sickness, failure, powerlessness, and every known need of human life.

> 2. **IT IS OUR REDEMPTIVE RIGHT.** "Redemption" means God has bought us — we belong to Him and we are not our own (1 Cor. 6:19-20).

> 3. **IT IS OUR NEEDFUL RIGHT.** From the standpoint of need as God's children, we have a right to claim as full a supply as God has promised (Phil. 4:19).

> 4. **IT IS OUR PROMISE RIGHT.** All the promises of God are for all who believe, but not one thing is promised to the unbeliever (2 Cor. 1:20; 2 Pet. 1:3-4).

> 5. **IT IS OUR FAMILY RIGHT.** As sons of God, we naturally have power by virtue of our relationship with God (Jn. 1:12; 2 Tim. 1:7). It's similar to how a child has access to family resources simply because of who their father is.

> 6. **IT IS OUR LEGAL RIGHT.** Legal right to use Christ's name is promised to every believer, as we've seen in Lesson Twenty-two.

> 7. **IT IS OUR COMMISSION RIGHT.** Every child of God has been commissioned to use the name of Jesus (Mt. 28:18-20; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:16; 16:23-26).

## How to Get the Christian's Power of Attorney

> 1. Believe the gospel — that everything it teaches is for you (2 Tim. 3:16-17).

> 2. Repent and turn to God, renouncing all sin and wrongdoing (Acts 26:18).

> 3. Obey the gospel and seek the enduement of power from on High (Lk. 11:13; 24:49; Acts 1:4-15; 2:38-39; 5:32).

> 4. Yield to God and walk and live in the Spirit (Rom. 8:1-16; Gal. 5:16-26).

> 5. Learn to pray in faith, nothing wavering (Jas. 1:5-8; Heb. 11:6).

> 6. Have faith in your mission in life to fully represent God (Acts 1:8).

> 7. Learn to use the name of Jesus according to the Word of God (Acts 3:16; 4:12; Jn. 14:12-15; 16:23-26; Col. 3:17; Jn. 15:7, 16).

## Using the Name of Jesus

When you start using the name of Jesus, you must have a genuine desire to be a true and unselfish representative of God. God's glory and the good of others must be the driving force in all your praying and working for God.

Start using His name in every area of life, and don't accept "no" when there's resistance to the answer. If you let Satan defeat you at the start, the battle will only get harder next time — so don't be defeated on any point.

As victories come, your faith and confidence will grow until you have a normal, steady faith in the name of Jesus where no defeat is possible.

Pray always to God in the name of Jesus. Rebuke demons in His name. Constantly use His name in daily conflict, pleading the merits of the blood of Jesus in His name by the power of the Holy Spirit. Challenge every opposing force in the name of Jesus.

Be fearless and unwavering. It must move. All must yield to the name of Jesus.

Use His name even if you tremble while doing it. It must be done. You don't have to feel any particular power when you do it.

You have faith in His name. You know the power of His name. You know that everything is made subject to His name. You ask — He will do it.

This is the truth of Jn. 14:12-15; 15:16; 16:23-26. This is power and prayer simplified. His power is unlimited, and you are to do unlimited asking and receiving.

Never question God's will in anything you ask according to the promises. It's already His will for you to ask what you will (Jn. 15:7; Mk. 11:22-24).

## Faith Based on God's Word

In all this kind of praying, your faith should be based on the Word of God itself — not on what you hear, feel, or see.

Picture it this way: you're not healed because the pain is gone, saved because you feel well, or delivered because you see deliverance. You're blessed by believing what the Word of God says.

If God says a certain thing is yours when you ask, then count it done on that basis alone.

Many people are living in failure today because they look to feelings and outward evidence for their answer. But the Word of God should be given first place in our lives — not the senses of our body and soul.

Never say "I am healed because I feel no pain" or "my prayer is answered because I feel it is answered." Say it because the Word of God says you have what you ask for.

## Ten Questions For Supplement Eleven. Do You Know:

- That the mark of the beast cannot be given today? This is true because the beast or the Antichrist cannot come until after the kingdoms are formed inside the old Roman Empire (Dan. 7:23-24) and until after the rapture of the Church (2 Thess. 2:7-8). Since both events are still future and since the mark of the beast will only be given during the last 3½ years of this age, it cannot be given today (Rev. 13:5-18). See Supplement Six, Question 10 and Supplement Eight, Question 5.

- That the first war ever fought happened long before Adam's days? It was a battle between God and Satan when Lucifer invaded Heaven to cast God out. Satan was defeated and cast down to Earth, as proven in Isa. 14:12-14; Lk. 10:18. This had to happen before Adam, because Lucifer was already a fallen creature when he came into Adam's Eden, as we saw in Lesson Seven.

- That the greatest wars ever fought have been in Heaven? Not only was the original war between God and Satan fought in Heaven, but all wars fought on Earth have actually been won or lost in the heavenlies between good and evil spirits. This is clear from Dan. 10:11-21; 11:1; 12:1, where good and evil angelic rulers of various earthly kingdoms are pictured waging war in the heavenlies. Wars on Earth result from wars between these spirit rulers of kingdoms.

- That Methuselah was not the oldest man who ever lived? He was the oldest man who ever lived on Earth, but his father Enoch is still alive, residing in Heaven. Enoch will return to Earth and die his appointed death at the hands of the Antichrist at the end of this age. He is over 5,551 years old, whereas Methuselah was only 969 years old when he died.

- That Noah was a woman? The Noah who built the boat was a man, but the Noah mentioned in Num. 27:1 was the daughter of Zelophehad.

- That the Bible records the day and month of Moses' birth and death? This was on the first day of the eleventh month, which was February (Deut. 1:3). This was the day of his birth and death as proven in Deut. 31:2, which says, "I am an hundred and twenty years old this day." In Deut. 32:48-52, God speaks to Moses on "that selfsame day" telling him to go up into the mountain and die. Between Deut. 1:3 and 31:2, we see that all of Deuteronomy was spoken by Moses on that one day, for throughout we read "this day" (Deut. 1:3, 10; 2:18, 22, 25, 30; 3:14; 4:4, 8, 20, 26, 38-40; 5:1, 3, 24; 6:6, 24; 7:11; 8:1, 11, 18, 19; 9:1, 3; 10:8, 13, 15; 11:2, 4, 8, 13, 26-28, 32; 12:8; 13:18; 15:5, 15; 19:9; 20:3; 26:3, 16-18; 27:1, 4, 9-11; 28:1, 13-15; 29:4, 10-15, 18, 28; 30:2, 8, 15, 16, 18, 19; 31:2, 27; 32:46-48).

- That Solomon was the first recorded billionaire? The temple he built for God cost over $87 billion — or twice that much according to 1949 prices, which are more than double pre-war figures (See Lesson Seventeen, Point VII). Before David died, he collected $4,848,500,000 in gold and silver to build the temple, along with brass, iron, and other materials "without weight" (1 Chron. 22:14-16). Solomon inherited all of this plus the billions he accumulated after David's death. At one time Solomon received $17,451,000.00 from Ophir, and the Queen of Sheba and Hiram, King of Tyre, each gave him $3,490,200.00 (1 Ki. 9:14, 28; 10:10). David's personal gift to the temple above what was already mentioned was $100,835,000.00 (1 Chron. 28:2-4). In another year, $19,370,610.00 in gold was brought to Solomon besides gold from year to year and gifts from all surrounding nations (2 Chron. 9:13-28).

- That only eight books of the Bible out of sixty-six have no prophecies in them? They are Song of Solomon, Ecclesiastes, Esther, Ezra, and Ruth in the Old Testament, and 2 John, 3 John, and Philemon in the New Testament.

- That Moses fasted forty days on three different occasions? The first time was when he went into the mount for forty days to get the tables of stone and receive the law (Deut. 9:9). The second time was when he went back for the second tables of stone (Deut. 9:17-18). The third time was when Israel rebelled at Kadesh-barnea (Deut. 9:23-25).

- That there are four men in the Bible who fasted forty days? Moses and Joshua fasted forty days (Exod. 24:12-18; 32:15-19); Elijah went on the strength of the angel's food for forty days and nights (1 Ki. 19:4-8); and Jesus fasted forty days and forty nights (Mt. 4:1-11).

supplements.title: Supplement 12
supplements.content:
# Supplement Twelve

**For Lessons Twenty-three and Twenty-four**

In Lesson Twenty-three, we explored the reality of sin. We saw that God isn't responsible for sin and that it's not His will for people to remain trapped in it.

We've studied the many aspects of what the Bible teaches about sin. In our last lesson, we explained how to get rid of sin and sickness.

The doctrines are clear, and the truths are laid out simply — but here's the thing: knowledge alone isn't enough to bring you the benefits you need and that God has promised. Now we need to put these lessons into personal practice.

It's time to take a personal stand with God, stand firm against these enemies of God and humanity, and step into the experiential reality of redemption.

## How to Identify Ourselves with Christ

### 1. By Making Proper Confession of Sins

This starts with recognizing that Christ died on the cross to atone for your sins — that He actually bore them Himself. When you confess that you're a sinner and that He carried your sins on the cross, you identify yourself with Him in His work of atonement.

The result? Cleansing from all sin follows immediately (1 Jn. 1:9).

### 2. By Crucifying the Old Man

The "old man," as we've seen, is the spirit and nature of the devil working in us before we're saved from sin. Even after salvation, the old man — or the devil — constantly works to regain control over us.

So we must stay on guard and refuse to give the devil any foothold (Eph. 4:27; 5:10-18; Jas. 4:7; 1 Pet. 5:8-9). Just because the devil seeks dominion and you're tempted to yield to sin doesn't mean you still have the old man in you or that you're somehow united with Satan's nature. It's only when you actually yield to sin again that you come back into harmony with the devil.

By "crucifying the old man," we simply mean recognizing that we're freed from him and refusing to obey him in any way. We must consider ourselves dead to him — and him dead to us.

Paul puts it this way:

> "How shall we that are dead to sin, live any longer therein? . . . Knowing this that our old man was crucified with him, that the body of sin might be destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve sin. For he that is dead is freed from sin. Now if we be dead with Christ, we believe that we shall live with him. Knowing that Christ being raised from the dead dieth no more; death hath no more dominion over him. For in that he died, he died unto sin once: but in that he liveth, he liveth unto God. Likewise reckon ye yourselves to be dead unto sin, but alive unto God through Jesus Christ our Lord. Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof. Neither yield yourselves as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but yield yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness unto God. For sin shall not have dominion over you" (Rom. 6:1-14).

You must reckon — or count — yourself dead to sin and alive to God. That means you have nothing more to do with sin and everything to do with God. You're to consider sin a thing of the past, nonexistent as far as you're concerned. God is all-in-all, the only thing that truly matters in your life.

Think of it like this: sometimes parents will cut off a child completely — removing them from the home and any inheritance. As far as those parents are concerned, that child is dead to them. In the same way, you must completely give up sin and count yourself dead to it. This happens by faith in Christ and by giving yourself wholly over to God and a life of holiness.

The old man and the devil's entire operation in your life must be ignored and rejected once and for all. That old life — which is nothing more than the devil working in you — must be renounced forever. We reckon ourselves new creatures in Christ, the old life dead, no longer living in the sins and lusts that destroy the soul.

The old life is past; the new life is here. The devil has no more part in you, so you count him dead (1 Jn. 5:18). The old sins are gone, counted as dead. You're alive to God now and recognize Him as your master.

Yes, the old life, old sins, old man, and the past still exist — but as far as you're concerned, they don't, because you are dead to them. When you served sin, you were dead to God — as far as you were concerned, He didn't exist. Now that you've turned to God and become alive to Him, you count yourself dead to sin and Satan.

You can't be alive to both. You can't serve two masters or live two different lives. When you're alive to sin, you're dead to God; when you're alive to God, you're dead to sin.

### 3. By Faith in Christ

This includes faith in His name (Acts 3:16; 4:12), faith in His blood (Rom. 3:24-25), faith in His Word (Jn. 15:7; 2 Cor. 1:20), and faith in His death, burial, and resurrection (Rom. 6:1-8; 1 Cor. 15:1-19).

You must believe that what Christ died for becomes yours the moment you accept it. We identify ourselves with Him by faith in all His work, and then we receive the benefits for which He died.

### 4. By Walking in the Light

> "But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin" (Jn. 1:7-9).

This means that as you come to know truth, you accept it and obey it — and it sets you free (Jn. 8:31-32). It's similar to walking on a path that gradually becomes brighter — the more light you receive, the clearer your way becomes.

This is growing in grace and in the knowledge of God (2 Pet. 1:4-10; 3:18).

### 5. By Taking Up Our Cross Daily to Follow Christ

This means forsaking everything the gospel requires you to give up and loving the Lord with all your heart and soul (Lk. 14:25-27, 33; Mt. 19:27-30). As you learn truth, you must conform to it daily.

Whatever cross you're called to bear, you must carry it in conformity to God's will. All selfishness in obeying truth must be denied, and the gospel must be followed regardless of personal interests (Mk. 8:34-38; 10:23-31).

### 6. By Walking and Living in the Spirit

This means seeking to know God's will through the Holy Spirit and the Word of God. It means always following the Spirit's leading and rejecting anything in your life that contradicts God's Word and your best spiritual interests.

You must put to death all the works of the flesh and cultivate the fruit of the Spirit (Rom. 8:1-16; Gal. 5:16-26; Col. 3:5-17; Jude 20-24; 1 Pet. 1:3-9).

### 7. By Constant Prayer and Study of the Word of God

In Lesson Sixteen, we covered the need for prayer and how to get answers to your prayers. Along with prayer, there should be constant meditation in God's Word and cheerful obedience to it.

Through meditation and study, you learn the finer points of truth and God's spiritual leadings. We're required to "study" (2 Tim. 2:15), "search" (Jn. 5:39), "continue in," and "know" the truth (Jn. 8:31-36), "obey it" (Jn. 14:23-24; Acts 5:32; Rom. 1:5), and "meditate in it day and night" (Ps. 1; Josh. 1:7-8).

It's through prayer, study, and obedience to the Word that faith increases (Rom. 10:17). We're promised that if the Word abides in us and we abide in Christ, we can ask what we will and it shall be done (Jn. 15:7). This is how we learn the ways to identify ourselves with Christ — and how to actually do it.

### 8. By Faithful Work for God and Consecration to Help Others

We should realize our responsibility — we're saved in order to serve. Find the most spiritual church in your community. Not necessarily the most popular one, because the popular church isn't always the most spiritual.

Choose a church that upholds a genuine standard of Christianity and clean, holy living according to the gospel. Go to a church that expects its members to live free from sin and the bad habits that will destroy the soul. It will pay you great dividends — both now and in eternity — to choose wisely a church that develops your faith and encourages you in following the whole Bible.

Regardless of which church you attend, cooperate with your pastor and the church's program for winning lost souls. Attend every meeting you possibly can. The Bible says:

> "Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching" (Heb. 10:25).

Back your pastor in prayer and be the kind of member he can depend on. Get busy for God and use every opportunity to be a witness for Jesus — publicly or privately. Never turn down a chance to lead prayer meetings, visit the sick, help the needy, or do religious and social work.

---

If you study the Bible, believe its promises, and follow the simple instructions we've given you, it will be impossible to fail God or miss the wonderful benefits He's promised. Believe in the reality of sin, sickness, Satan, demons, and God.

Learn that the true source of help in life comes from God. Learn how to pray and what to pray for. Don't settle for living like many so-called Christians around you.

Step ahead of the crowd. Be an example to others and never stumble over anyone. Here's the thing: if you stumble over a hypocrite, that person is actually ahead of you — otherwise you wouldn't be stumbling over them!

Practice the constant presence of God. Overcome sin and bad habits. Believe that you can have healing, health, and everything God has promised. Seek God daily to experience these benefits, and your life will be blessed beyond anything you can imagine right now.

---

## Nine Questions For Supplement Twelve. Do You Know:

- That the Bible tells us the exact day and month Abraham left Ur of the Chaldees? This was on the 15th of April, exactly 430 years to the day before the children of Israel left Egypt: "Now the sojourning of the children of Israel, who dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred and thirty years. And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the hosts of the Lord went out from the land of Egypt" (Exod. 12:40-41). Abraham started the sojourn 430 years to the day before Israel left Egypt, according to this passage. (See also Gen. 12:1-3.)

- That men traveled through the air many centuries before the Wright brothers did? Ezekiel and Philip were both taken through the air by the Holy Spirit (Ezek. 8:3; 11:3; Acts 8:39). John and Paul were taken to Heaven and came back (2 Cor. 12:1-7; Rev. 4:1). Enoch and Elijah were taken to Heaven bodily and will come back and die at the hands of the Antichrist (Gen. 5:24; 2 Ki. 2; Heb. 11:5; Rev. 11:1-11; Zech. 4:11-14). Elijah came back in the days of Christ so he had at least two trips to Heaven, for he is there now (Mt. 17:3-4). Christ and all the Old Testament saints also were taken to Heaven as well as every person who has died in Christ (Acts 1:11; Eph. 4:7-11; 2 Cor. 5:8; Phil. 1:21-23).

- That Saul was not the first king of Israel and that they had a king over 500 years before Samuel made Saul king? Moses was the first king in Israel, according to Deut. 33:4-5.

- That the name of David's mother is given in the Bible? According to 2 Sam. 17:25 her name was Nahash, for there we find that Abigail and Zeruiah were daughters of Nahash. In 1 Chron. 2:13-16 it says that these women were the sisters of David. If they were David's sisters and if they were the daughters of Nahash, then David's mother's name must have been Nahash. It is argued that Nahash is a man's name. Be that as it may, Noah was a man's name but it was also a woman's name, according to Num. 27. The same could be true of the name of David's mother. Jesse was the father of David and these women mentioned, so if they were also the children of Nahash, then this must be the name of David's mother. That Nahash was a man, the stepfather of David, is unfounded and a mere theory of man.

- That God did not create sin and He is not responsible for it? Sin is transgression of the law. God created creatures capable of laws and He made laws, but He did not make His creatures break the law. The ones who broke the law are the responsible ones and God holds them responsible. This has been made clear in Lesson Twenty-three, Point III, 9 and 10.

- That the hinderer of lawlessness in 2 Thess. 2:7-8 refers to the Church? There are only three things in the world today that hinder lawlessness. They are the Church, the Holy Spirit and human governments. The hinderer that will be taken out of the world must refer to one of these three things. It could not refer to the Holy Spirit and governments for neither will be taken out of the world. It is clear that governments will not be taken for Antichrist will reign over the ten kingdoms during the Tribulation (Dan. 7:23-24; Rev. 17:8-17). The Holy Spirit will be here during the Tribulation, so He could not be the hinderer taken (Acts 2:16-21; Jn. 14:16; Rev. 7:14; 12:17; 19:10; Zech. 12:10). The Church will be taken out of the world (1 Thess. 4:16; Eph. 5:26-27; 1 Cor. 15:51-58), so this must be the hinderer referred to. If you cannot understand how the Church could be referred to as "he," see Eph. 2:15; 4:13 where the Church is called a "man." (See also Supplement Six, Questions 3 and 10; Supplement Seven, Questions 1-8; and Supplement Eight, Questions 1-3.)

- That the mark upon Cain was not a physical mark or change of color? The Hebrew word for "mark" in Gen. 4:15 means "a pledge." The pledge was stated in the same verse, "Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken sevenfold." There is no statement that he was marked physically by a change of color or some other mark. (See Lesson Eleven, Point VII, 2).

- That one man was healed of leprosy three different times? Moses was made a sign to Israel and to Pharaoh three times when his hand was made leprous and was healed (Exod. 4:6-7, 21, 30).

- That Maher-shalal-hash-baz is not the longest name in the Bible, as is stated in all Bible statistics of men? There is another name equally as long found in the title of the 56th Psalm. (See Isa. 8:3 for the first eighteen-letter word.) Isaiah's son was called "Maher-shalal-hash-baz," meaning "haste ye, haste ye to the spoil," because Judah was soon to be spoiled by Babylon and the Jews taken into captivity.

supplements.title: Supplement 13
supplements.content:
# Supplement Thirteen

*For Lessons Twenty-five and Twenty-six*

I want to be upfront with you about something. I make no apology for teaching what the Bible clearly says about the gifts of the Holy Spirit and other doctrines that many modern churches have decided to ignore. Trust me — I'm not taking this stand to win popularity contests!

Years ago, when I was just a boy, I made a commitment to God. I consecrated my life to studying and teaching *everything* in the Bible — whether or not it lined up with my church or any other church. To me, this is the only honest and intelligent path to take.

If others choose differently, that's on them. They'll answer to God for it in the end.

My hope is that you'll continue approaching Scripture with a clear, rational mind. After all, isn't truth what you're really after? Don't you want to know what God Himself teaches?

If that's your heart, then studying these lessons and reading the Scriptures we provide can't possibly lead you astray. Every doctrine we cover is backed up by God's own Word.

## The Right Basis of Understanding Truth

Let's acknowledge a few things together. The Bible is written in human language. There *must* be a basis of truth we can all agree on if we're willing.

God is certainly intelligent enough to make Himself clear on every doctrine He teaches. And it's simply impossible that there are dozens of "correct" interpretations of the same plain statements in Scripture.

Here's what's really happening: there's a supernatural power at work, trying to confuse people and make them think the Bible can mean whatever anyone wants it to mean. This is too obvious to ignore!

So let's take a logical approach and ask ourselves some honest questions.

Isn't the Bible God's inspired revelation? Isn't it written in plain human language so we can all understand it the same way — or is it really so complicated that we have to argue endlessly about what it means?

Why do preachers disagree about what the Bible teaches if it's simple enough for everyone to understand? What good does that division accomplish?

Wouldn't it be better to simply believe together what's plainly written? Why let the devil divide God's people?

We've already addressed these questions thoroughly in Lessons One through Three. We've shown how demon powers work to confuse people's understanding of Scripture. We've proven that the Bible is God's Word, that it's actually the simplest book to understand, and that there's no excuse for misunderstanding it — if we just take it literally, the same way we read any other book.

Once you grasp this foundation, everything else falls into place naturally.

## The Abundant Supply House

Throughout these lessons, we've demonstrated something remarkable: there is no known need or want in the human experience that the Bible doesn't promise to supply — when we meet certain conditions.

Think of it like an enormous warehouse stocked with everything you could ever need, and God has given you the key. The supplies are there. The access is available. You just need to follow the instructions.

If you ever find yourself wavering on this point or questioning any detail, go back and review Lessons One through Three, Ten, Twelve, Fourteen, Sixteen, Twenty, and Twenty-two. Refresh your memory with God's abundant promises and His plan for getting these benefits into your life.

## No Failure is Possible

Here's a truth that should transform how you approach God: no failure is possible when you want to receive from Him — *if* you simply follow the instructions laid out in His Word.

It would be easier for heaven and earth to disappear than for even one of God's promises to fail, as long as we faithfully and literally obey the conditions He's made clear.

Jesus Himself said, "Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away" (Lk. 21:33). And the psalmist declared, "For ever O Lord, thy word is settled in heaven" (Ps. 119:89).

It is literally impossible for God to fail anyone. No one has ever experienced that, and no one ever will. On the other hand, everyone who knows God — or who has heard about others who know God — will testify that He has never failed in a single thing He promised.

## Where the Failure Really Is

If you're not getting what you want from God, the problem isn't far away. An honest self-examination will reveal it quickly. In fact, it's usually so obvious that no real searching should be necessary.

The failure is in *you*. And if you don't think so, that very attitude proves the point.

Those who truly love God will give Him the benefit of the doubt. They'll check themselves to find out where the real trouble lies. And they'll discover that everyone who fails to receive what God has promised is, at the root, doubting Him.

If there's no other reason for failure, doubt is reason enough.

How can you tell if you're doubting? It's actually easy. The very fact that you recognize a failure, that you're questioning why you haven't been answered, that you're wondering about it at all — *that's* proof of doubt.

Someone who genuinely believes God will settle the matter once and for all. They won't question the how or why of any answer — or the lack of one. It's similar to how a child trusts a parent's promise completely, without constantly asking "but are you *sure*?" That child just knows it's going to happen.

If you truly believe, it becomes a settled fact. No questioning. No wondering. No reasoning it out. No discussing it with people or entertaining the devil's suggestions. No relaxing until the answer comes.

Such a person would rather die in faith than live in unbelief, constantly worrying and questioning. But here's the beautiful truth: if you refuse to waver, everything will work out. Unbelief, however, ties God's hands and keeps you from receiving what you want.

## God is No Respecter of Persons

God has plainly stated the grounds on which He will — and will not — answer prayer. He must be faithful to His Word.

He will not deviate one inch from the conditions He's told us we must meet. He won't change His Word for any individual. If He did, He'd be showing favoritism like everyone else.

God is absolutely and eternally true to Himself, His Word, and His entire creation. He will not violate His own principles. If you want answers to prayer, do what God requires, and you will receive the right answers.

## The Word of God is Plain

We've made this clear repeatedly: God's will and Word are plain, and we've stated the promises and conditions of answered prayer many times. There's no excuse for not knowing what to do, and there can be no excuse for not doing what you know you should.

These steps must be followed before prayer is answered, so settle this once and for all.

If there's any rebellion in your heart against certain Bible truths... if you've fought against accepting the whole truth as it reads... if you're still questioning whether what you're being taught might not be true — you must stop this right now.

I understand that many of these truths are new to you and that the average church doesn't teach this way. But does that prove the Bible is wrong?

You can see for yourself that what we're saying is right there in plain English in your own Bible. You don't have to wonder about truth any longer — if you genuinely want it. It's there in your Bible, and it's not a question of whether everyone accepts it or whether it's popular.

The only question is: Do these statements appear in the Bible in plain human language? If they do, you're responsible to God for accepting or rejecting them.

## Don't Sell God Short by Unbelief

You may even have to leave your church in order to believe the whole truth. I hope that's not necessary, but if it is — if that's what it takes to accept truth and receive its benefits — it will richly pay you to do so. Any church or preacher who won't accept the whole truth of the Bible is not worthy of your continued respect and support.

Let me be clear: I'm not advocating that Christians leave their churches. What I *am* advocating is that all churches repent and return to the Bible.

But if it comes down to a choice, individuals in churches should not continue in fellowship with those who reject Scripture. It would be better to leave and join churches in your community that actually follow the Bible. This is a perfectly right position to take, because we must obey God's Word rather than human opinions.

Keep this in mind: when churches become worldly, ungodly, and rebellious against the gospel, they're no longer truly Christian churches. They've become societies of sin and rebellion against God. Anyone who supports such churches will face God's judgment.

## Your Final Court of Appeals is the Bible

Go to it yourself. See if it says what we claim it does. We give you the references to read, and you can verify whether these things are there or not.

Don't take the attitude that these teachings are just "our interpretation" and that another interpretation might be better. That's just an excuse, because you can still see for yourself whether the Bible says what we say it does.

This isn't about our interpretation versus someone else's. It's about what the Bible *actually says*. You need to wake up to this fact before it's too late.

Yes, my friend, we're being faithful to tell you what the Bible says — but it's up to you to accept it and receive the promised benefits. Don't blame God for your failure to get what you want. Blame yourself, correct what's wrong, and what you want will be yours.

I'm asking you to reaffirm your faith in God and His Word. Consecrate yourself fresh to full and complete obedience to truth as you come to know it.

Repeat the affirmations we've given you constantly. Grow in the kind of faith that dares to believe God for what you want.

In our next two lessons, we'll deal thoroughly with the subject of faith. Until then, I join with you in prayer that you might comprehend with all the saints the fullness of God and experience everything that is freely promised to you.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Thirteen. Do You Know:

- **That sinners can commit the unpardonable sin?** This is clear from the fact that the Jews of Mt. 12:22-37 were sinners and Christ warned them of committing that sin. In Heb. 10:26-29 we have the fact that a person who has been sanctified by the blood of Christ can go back into sin and commit this sin. Blaspheming the Spirit and attributing to the devil the works of the Spirit, or speaking a word against the Spirit knowingly and willfully, is the unpardonable sin, and this any person can do whether he is a sinner or not.

- **That one man in the Bible fasted longer than did Christ?** Moses fasted at least three times forty days each (Deut. 9:9, 11, 17-18, 23-25). In fact, it seems from Deut. 9:11-18 that Moses fasted eighty days without eating between the two forty-day fasts.

- **That one man captured a whole army of multiplied thousands single-handed?** Elisha did this when he captured the whole army of Syria and led them captive to Samaria (2 Ki. 6:18-23).

- **That Cain was not the first one to build a city on Earth?** He was the first to build a city on Earth since Adam (Gen. 4:17), but there were cities built on Earth by the pre-Adamites, as proven in Lesson Seven, Point VI.

- **That resurrected men and angels can go back and forth from Earth to Heaven in one day?** Jesus after the resurrection went to Heaven and back in one day. This statement is proven by the fact that He forbad Mary to touch Him for He had not yet ascended to God but was going to God, and on the same day He returned to the Earth and appeared to the disciples (Jn. 20:17-23). If resurrected men can go to Heaven and back in a day, angels can do likewise. Other men have also gone to Heaven but how long it took them is not stated (See Supplement One, Question 9.)

- **That people who go to Heaven will eat food forever?** In Lk. 22:14-18, 29-30; Rev. 2:7, 17; 19:1-10; 22:1-2 it is clear that saints will eat forever.

- **That Egypt will never become great again?** The Bible is very clear that Egypt will never be a great power again as it used to be in the early days of Israel. The prophet Ezekiel said that Egypt would be "a base kingdom. It shall be the basest of the kingdoms; neither shall it exalt itself any more above the nations" (Ezek. 29:14-15). Egypt will be defeated by the future Antichrist from Syria (Dan 11:40-45).

- **That the Arabs will give Israel protection from the Antichrist during the last 3½ years of this age?** Dan. 11:41 states that Arabia or ancient Edom and Moab will escape Antichrist. In Isa. 16:1-5; Ezek. 20:33-35; Mt. 24:15-21; Rev. 12:5-17 we read of Israel fleeing for protection into the wilderness of Edom and Moab, and since they escape the Antichrist and flee into this country for protection, then it is clear that the Arabs protect them during this time.

- **That the Bible names the very city in Edom and Moab that Israel will flee to for protection during the Tribulation?** It is Sela or Petra, the ancient capital of Edom (Isa. 16:1-5; 26:20-21; 63:1-8).

- **That God has a basis of dealing with the heathen who have never heard of the Gospel?** He will deal with them on the basis of the light they have received, and on the basis of obeying their own conscience (Rom. 2:12-16).

supplements.title: Supplement 14
supplements.content:
# Supplement Fourteen

## For Lessons Twenty-seven and Twenty-eight

### I. The Law of Faith

Based on everything we've studied so far, one truth stands out crystal clear: all of God's blessings come through faith. If you want to receive what you need in this life—and in the life to come—you must have faith and put it to work.

Nothing in Scripture is more obvious than this: faith is absolutely essential to receive the benefits of the gospel. And here's the flip side—you receive according to how much faith you have.

Think of it like a faucet: the wider you open it, the more water flows. The law of faith works the same way: "nothing doubting . . . according to your faith, so be it done unto you . . . If thou canst believe, all things are possible" (Mt. 9:28-29; Mk. 1:15; 9:23; 11:22-24; 16:17; Jas. 1:5-8; Heb. 11:6; Rom. 12:6).

If you receive little, it's because you have little faith. If you receive much, you have much faith. If you waver, you get nothing.

If you believe for one thing but not another, you only get the one thing. You can always tell how much faith you have by what you receive.

If a person gives up in the battle and quits believing for what they've asked, it's not God's fault they don't receive. It's their own.

### II. God Never Rebukes Faith

People have been rebuked for lack of faith, but never for having faith or for asking great things from God (Mt. 6:30; 14:31; 16:8; Lk. 24:25; Jas. 1:5-8).

There should never be any doubt about receiving from God if you'll simply see to it that you have faith without doubting or questioning. The answering part is God's job—so why should you, a small human being, worry so much about that?

Leave all that worry to God. He's responsible for the answer, and He will answer if you exercise true faith.

Your part is to ask, simply believe, and refuse to doubt after you've prayed. If you would take care of your part of the program, God would take care of His—and there would be no failure.

When you start meddling with God's business—the answering part—that's when God lets you sweat. He lets you alone until you take care of your own part, and then the answer comes.

If the law of faith says that no answer is granted until all doubting, questioning, unbelief, and worrying cease, then let's settle this once and for all: God is not going to break His own law to please someone who spends more time criticizing God for not answering than praising Him for the answer.

If you want answers, stop worrying about how and when God is going to carry out His part. Let God run His own business in His own way and time.

Be patient and faithful until He answers, and prayer will become a simple, natural part of your life.

### III. How to Get Faith

There are several ways to increase your faith. If you truly want to believe God, take these steps:

#### 1. Get into Christ

This is the first step of the Christian experience. When you're in Christ, you're a new creature—old things, including unbelief, have passed away, and all things have become new and are of God (2 Cor. 5:17).

Anyone who has had a real, personal experience with God will remember that the beginning of the new life was marked by utter simplicity and childlikeness. In the matter of faith, the newly born-again believer was so confident and simple that they would believe God for anything.

There weren't all those arguments of unbelief or endless questioning about God's will. It was taken for granted that anything God promised as good was His will for every believer. Getting answers to prayer was easy—believing God for anything you wanted was natural.

It was only when questioning and reasoning about truth crept in that prayer started failing. That's when you began hearing things like: "God doesn't answer every prayer," "It's not His will to give everything He's promised," "He knows best what to give and what not to give," and "You shouldn't expect all prayers to be answered."

As long as you stayed simple and childlike—believing God was good enough to give His children anything and everything good—prayers were answered in such supernatural ways that you truly felt like a child of God.

A simple faith is naturally born into every new child of God by the Spirit. Faith takes root immediately, and God is taken at His Word. There's no doubt at first that God will answer prayer.

If this confidence in God were nourished and developed normally as we grew in grace and knowledge, we would never have so many failures in prayer. But by listening to older believers who sometimes live in doubt and unbelief, new believers begin following their example—wavering and questioning God about everything.

Christ is the author and finisher of faith in everyone, and a life of faith naturally begins when Christ is received (Heb. 12:1-2). As you receive Christ, you're to walk in Him (Col. 2:6-7). If you continued as you started, faith and zeal would never be dampened, and you'd never revert to a life of failure and constant questioning about God's will regarding His promises.

#### 2. Know the Word of God

The second step to getting faith is to become thoroughly familiar with the Word of God. "Faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God" (Rom. 10:17; Acts 10:44; 15:7; 1 Cor. 1:18-21; Gal. 3:11; 2 Thess. 2:13).

After getting into Christ, the next thing to do is start reading God's Word and meditating on it day and night. God promises that if you do this, you'll be like a tree planted by rivers of water, bringing forth fruit in its season—and whatever you do shall prosper (Ps. 1; Josh. 1).

So many people pray without ever thinking about what God says concerning what they're asking. If you asked the average person what promise of God they were depending on as the basis of their faith, they wouldn't have any particular Scripture in mind.

No wonder such people are seldom heard by God! Jesus said, "If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you" (Jn. 15:7).

The Word of God and prayer must go together if you expect definite results. There is no ground for answered prayer outside of God's Word.

The Bible reveals God's will in all things and promises certain benefits through Christ and faith. No one can normally get prayers answered who doesn't know the truth.

#### 3. Do Not Pray for Faith in God and His Word

Here's something that might surprise you: praying for faith is actually a prayer of unbelief. Consequently, there can't be an answer to it.

Praying for faith is simply an attempt to evade trusting God's Word and get things from God contrary to the way He's set things up. Only the Word can give you the basis for answered prayer.

It's similar to having a bank account but praying for the bank to give you money instead of simply writing a check. The provision is already there—you just need to use it!

Simple faith in God's Word may not seem sufficient or even sensible to the natural person who lives by their senses and always wants to see and feel everything. But this kind of confidence is necessary if you want what God's promises offer.

We're not told to pray for faith—we're told to "have faith in God" (Mk. 11:22-24).

#### 4. Have Faith in God

If people would learn to put confidence in God the way they do in other people—if they would have unwavering faith in His promises the way they trust human words—answered prayer would be a great success.

Prayers would be answered without so much effort. Prayer would be a simple, everyday transaction between God and man.

It's sad to say, but people will trust other humans and put their whole confidence in them more readily than they trust God and His Word. At the same time, they claim they believe God more than any man!

They would never boldly declare that they don't have confidence in God or that they don't believe He's faithful to His Word—but their actions prove otherwise.

If somebody tells them, "Maybe it's not God's will to answer," or "If He doesn't answer, He knows best," or some other excuse for unbelief, it's generally accepted as true. Seldom do such people go to the Bible to see exactly what it says and intelligently follow it.

Even if they find that they're definitely promised the answer, the average person questions God's will with some unreasonable argument of unbelief.

Such people would never be turned aside so easily if another human had made those promises! They would go to court or fight to their last breath to hold someone to their obligations.

But they're so full of unbelief that they don't want to "bother" God with the same problems. They won't put forth the least fight against demonic powers and unbelief to get what God has promised or to hold God to His obligations.

Is it any wonder God ignores such unbelieving, disinterested requests for things He's promised? We would do the same!

In fact, we wouldn't be as tolerant of people who reject us and our word and constantly fail to believe us as God is toward failing, unbelieving humanity. We would be so disgusted with such half-heartedness that we'd withdraw all offers of help.

But despite our failures, God goes on year after year, seeking to prove that He means what He says and says what He means. He's constantly trying to show us that He's faithful to His Word and won't go contrary to it for anyone.

He will meet His own obligations—but on the grounds of His own revealed terms: faith, nothing wavering. We are to blame for all failure; so the quicker we realize the trouble and correct it, the sooner we'll get what we want from God.

#### 5. Listen to Good Preaching

When we constantly hear stories of the triumphs of faith in the Bible—when we read of miracles like God dividing the waters, raining manna from Heaven, sending quails, giving water from rocks, multiplying food, quenching fire, raising the dead, healing the sick, defeating His enemies, and causing hundreds of other miraculous events written for our instruction and to build up our faith—our faith runs high!

We fully realize that what God has done, He can and will do again in meeting His obligations to give us what He's promised. Our faith naturally becomes powerful and expectant.

Personal experiences of believers in all ages are also a great source for building faith. Accept and believe any record of God's dealing with anyone if it's in harmony with His Word.

#### 6. Reject All Bad Preaching

When we go to churches where ministers question these miracles and make fun of them as mere fairy tales—trying to explain away such facts with high-sounding, clever, pseudo-scientific generalizations—our faith is hindered.

We must be consecrated to renounce all such foolish theories as from the devil and cleave to the plain Word of God in all things. No one can expect to build up faith and get to the place where benefits can be received while spending time in places where God's Word is criticized.

If you want simple, unwavering faith, you cannot listen to this kind of preaching and come out without questioning some things.

Yes, all faith-killing, Spirit-quenching, truth-destroying, man-made traditions, sects, and creeds—however clever they may be in doing away with simple gospel truths—must be rejected and renounced once and for all.

Don't tolerate any person or church that slanders God or His Word, which is the source of your faith and eternal life.

#### 7. Obey the Word of God

Obey God's Word to the letter, regardless of how foolish it may appear. God's ways are not our ways, and His thoughts are not our thoughts. His ways are as high above ours as Heaven is above Earth.

Accept His Word as the right thing on every question and obey it to the letter—and it will be proven right. It will be proven true in every respect.

The test of this age is "the obedience to the faith among all nations," as we've seen in Lessons Nineteen, Point IV. Those who fail in obedience to the faith will be rejected by God in the end and consigned to eternal Hell for their failure.

Thus it's critically important that we conform to the Word and obey to the letter what is plainly written.

#### 8. Exercise Your Faith

By exercising your faith, you'll cause it to grow (Rom. 1:17). One of the best secrets to increasing faith is simply to begin using what faith you have.

Picture it this way: faith is like a muscle—what you don't use, you lose. It's a divine law: "Whoever hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have" (Lk. 8:18).

Just as any muscle that remains unused becomes weak and lifeless, so it is with faith. We must use what we have if we want it to become strong and dynamic.

It's not only a natural law that faith increases when used in all life's problems, but God will supernaturally see to it that it's increased. He will impart more faith—the divine kind—to those who properly exercise human faith.

Our faith must work by love; that is, we must exercise our faith for the good of all people, whether friend or foe (Gal. 5:6). Unless we have fervent love for all, our faith can never reach its highest peak of efficiency. Where there is love, there is faith, for both are fruit of the Spirit (Gal. 5:22-23).

Where love, unity, peace, and true Christian fellowship prevail in a church, there will be little sickness and defeat, and few failures in prayer. Where malice, jealousy, gossip, and constant division and strife exist, there will be much more sickness and failure in prayer. Experience, observation, and Scripture all establish this fact.

The Fall would not have been possible without doubt and unbelief in God and His Word. God demands that we learn He is faithful to His Word and means exactly what He says—before He redeems us and before He answers prayer.

God must demand faith and freedom from doubt in order to redeem us from the Fall. (See Lesson Nine for the steps leading to the fall of man.) These steps must be retraced in our redemption and complete reconciliation to God.

We must learn the lesson of faith and absolute confidence in God sooner or later if we expect to live with God forever. There's no better time than while we're on probation to learn this lesson.

This is why God requires faith in all that He says before He obligates Himself to answer us definitely. At first, there's naturally a struggle to trust God whom we cannot see. But after we learn to do this, the hard part of faith is over, and we enter into a place with God where we get things easily—by simply asking and receiving through faith in God and His Word.

#### 9. Yield to the Holy Spirit

Faith is not only a natural ability or exercise of a created faculty—it's also a fruit of the Spirit and a gift from God (Gal. 5:22-23; 1 Cor. 12:4-11).

When we use our own powers of faith in God and His Word properly, we make contact with God in a supernatural way and become partakers of the divine nature (2 Pet. 1:3-4).

We're then free to walk and live in the Spirit, and the fruit of the Spirit will naturally be manifest in our lives (Gal. 5:16-26). We should constantly add to our faith the virtues of God by yielding to Him in daily life (2 Pet. 1:4-10).

This kind of faith becomes a natural fruit of the Spirit and of holy living. It's not an effort to have this faith—it grows in the life of one who yields to God and loves God enough to draw near to Him in daily conflict.

It is a fruit, not a work. It is a gift, not something we earn (Rom. 12:3; 2:4-5; 12:4-11; Gal. 5:22-23).

Human faith in union with the divine will make all things possible to the believer. Let us all have faith in God, in Christ, in the Holy Spirit, in the Word of God, in the atonement, and in all the gospel truths.

Have faith in God and look to Him for needed daily grace for body, soul, and spirit. The more we love God and conform to His Word, the more faith will grow.

This faith will grow until it rests solely in the Infinite. In this place with God, we can reach up and pull down the unlimited blessings of God for ourselves and others.

Remember this: the secret of faith is faith in God.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Fourteen. Do You Know:

- That Christ was not the only one that multiplied food for men? Elisha multiplied food and fed one hundred men miraculously (2 Ki. 4:42-44). Elijah also multiplied the widow's meal daily for "many days" (1 Ki. 17:8-16).

- That Russia will not be the head of the nations at Armageddon? Instead, Russia will be conquered by the Antichrist and will be under him at Armageddon. Antichrist will come from Syria and not Russia, as we have already seen in Questions 9 and 10, Supplement Eight. According to Dan. 11:44 he conquers all the northern and eastern nations outside of the Roman Empire Territory, and he will then be ruler of these countries. Ezekiel 38-39 pictures his coming down from the north at Armageddon after having conquered these countries. Zechariah 14; Rev. 19:11-21 predicts his defeat at the second coming of Christ. So Syria, not Russia, will be the head of the nations at Armageddon.

- That Russia will be defeated three times between our day and the Millennium? This is clear from the fact that ten kingdoms will be formed inside the Roman Empire before Antichrist comes (Dan. 7:23-24; Supplement Eight, Question 5). Because Russia dominates Romania, Bulgaria, Hungary, Austria, Albania, and other Roman Empire states, they must be liberated from Russia before the ten kingdoms can be formed. This will require one defeat of Russia. Another defeat will occur when Antichrist heads these ten kingdoms and conquers Russia during the last 3½ years of this age, as seen in the question above. The third defeat of Russia will be by Christ at Armageddon (Ezek. 38; 39; Zech. 14:1-5; Rev. 19:11-21).

- That men will not spend eternity in Heaven? According to Rev. 21-22 the New Jerusalem, the home of the saints, will be moved from Heaven to the Earth after the Millennium. This proves men will spend eternity on Earth instead of Heaven.

- That God eats and wears clothes as we do? This is clear from Gen. 18; Exod. 24:11; Dan. 7:9-14; Isa. 6; Rev. 4-5.

- That angels also eat food and wear clothes? This is clear from Gen. 18-19; Ps. 78:25; Dan. 10:5-21.

- That Babylon as a literal city will be rebuilt before the second coming of Christ? Babylon will be destroyed as a city under the seventh vial (Rev. 16:17-21; 18:1-24) and in the day of the Lord (Isa. 13:6, 9; 14:3-4); so it must be a city in that day (See Chapter Thirty-seven of Revelation Expounded).

- That there will be sin and death on Earth during the Millennium? This is plainly stated in Isa. 65:20-25; 1 Cor. 15:24-28.

- That God has already sent some of His sons to Hell? Angels are called sons of God in Job 38:7. Many of them are in Hell now (2 Pet. 2:4; Jude 6-7).

- That the devil never enters bodily into anyone? The word "in" means "in union with" and not "bodily entrance into," as we have seen in Lesson Four, Point 1, 4. This is the only sense in which he enters into anyone because he has a body of his own and he cannot get bodily into any other body (1 Chron. 21:1; Job 1:6-12; 2:1-7; Rev. 12:7-12; Zech. 3:1-3; Mt. 4:1-11; Ezek. 28:11-17; Rev. 20:1-10).

supplements.title: Supplement 15
supplements.content:
# Supplement Fifteen

**For Lessons Twenty-nine and Thirty**

We've covered a lot of ground together so far — prayer and how God answers it, the New Testament program for the modern Church, your Christian power of attorney, the truth about the Holy Spirit, spiritual gifts, faith for the real needs of life, the New Testament Church, and the Bible's teaching on power for believers. Throughout all of this, we've seen one thing over and over: everything God gave to the early Church, He wants to give to us today.

## All Spiritual Opposition is Satanic

Here's something worth thinking about: if God is the author of all the Scriptures we've studied proving these blessings are for us today, then who do you think is behind the criticism of these doctrines? It has to be His opponent — the devil.

Anyone who condemns a spiritual experience that's plainly stated in Scripture is being used as a tool of the enemy. The devil's goal is simple: rob people of the purposes and benefits God intends for them.

## Christians Should Unite to Fight Satan

Instead of fighting each other, Christians should be joining forces against our real enemies — sin, sickness, and the devil. Think of it like this: if an army spent all its energy fighting within its own ranks, the actual enemy would have free rein to do whatever he wanted.

That's exactly what Satan accomplishes when he gets believers squabbling over church creeds, rituals, minor theological theories, and even spiritual experiences that should never cause division. His long-range strategy is to keep Christians from gaining power with God against him and his works.

As long as he can prevent aggressive spiritual warfare and keep believers from walking in victory, he's free to continue his work of bringing sin, sickness, defeat, and poverty into people's lives.

## The Goal of Every Christian

Your goal as a believer should be to attain all the fullness of God until the full evidences of Spirit-baptism (as we discussed in Lesson Thirty) are manifested in your life. Don't be satisfied with the measure of the Spirit you currently have.

Sadly, the least little blessing from God seems to satisfy most people. But that's not how it should be.

## Do Not Ridicule Experiences of Others

Most of us have heard people make slanderous remarks about those who claim divine healing power, who say they've been healed by God, who testify to the baptism in the Spirit, or who share some other biblical experience. Some folks have ridiculed supernatural manifestations so thoroughly that hundreds of thousands of people in our biggest churches know nothing of the real truth.

Church members get taught to avoid any mention of these biblical doctrines and to write off all spiritual manifestations as either demonic or fanatical. Meanwhile, those same members smoke, chew tobacco, play cards, drink, and commit all kinds of sins — and their teachers never rebuke them or warn them away from these evils. What a sad condition!

## Spiritual Gifts are of God

This situation should never be tolerated by any church. When it is, that church becomes spiritually dead and stops being a true Christian church — it's just a center for entertainment and social activity.

We've looked at plenty of Scripture proving there's a real baptism in the Spirit, real power from God for believers, and real gifts of the Spirit that should be operating in every church and every member. Instead of shunning these experiences and the people who teach them, church members should welcome any good news about these blessings. They should be eager to learn everything they can.

Ask yourself: Would God give us hundreds of Scriptures making these things clear if He didn't intend for us to have them?

## No Excuse for Ignorance or Controversy

There's no reason to be ignorant of these spiritual blessings if you'll just read your Bible and see for yourself what God says. And there's no excuse for controversy about spiritual experiences if people will be even half honest and open to the truths plainly stated in Scripture.

A popular excuse among those who fight these doctrines is pointing to failures in Christian living — people who don't demonstrate the power such experiences are supposed to bring. But these critics seem to forget that some people in *all* churches fail to live the Christian life. Should we call all those churches "of the devil" because some members fail? Should we condemn their doctrines because a few members don't live right?

Regardless of human failure, we must accept plain truths of Scripture as being from God.

## All Spiritual Manifestations are Strange

Paul told us that the things of God's Spirit would seem like foolishness to the natural man (1 Cor. 2:14) and that God would use the foolish things of the world to confound the wise (1 Cor. 1:26-31). It's similar to how someone who's never seen a smartphone might think the whole concept is absurd — until they actually use one and discover what it can do.

Naturally, any manifestation of the nine gifts mentioned in 1 Cor. 12 would seem foolish and strange to someone who's never seen them in operation. But why reject or criticize something you know nothing about — either from Scripture or from experience?

The natural man isn't used to spiritual experiences, and every one of them will seem like foolishness until he decides to be open enough to investigate what's written about them. When something is clearly written about any spiritual blessing, that should settle it as biblical and from God. We should then bring our natural thinking under control and refuse to reject the truth.

## Christians Cannot Get a Wrong Spirit

Many people are afraid that God might let them get a wrong spirit if they seek spiritual experiences. But this fear has no foundation, as Lk. 11:7-13 proves. God will not permit any honest person to be deceived and receive a wrong spirit if they walk uprightly according to His Word and ask only for things He has promised.

Only dishonest people who try to evade truth and live unclean lives need to fear, because they've opened themselves to demonic deception. As a true child of God, you should walk in simple faith and obedience to all known truth, praying daily, staying under the blood of Christ — and you won't need to give a single thought to getting a wrong spirit.

## The One Great Need of the Modern Church

The greatest need in any church is to forget petty differences and seek God for the New Testament enduement of power for service — the kind of power that does the works of Christ in delivering people from sin, sickness, failure, and poverty.

The Church as a whole may not be willing to do this. But as individuals, we must pursue this if we want to experience the full benefits of the gospel personally.

The world today is dying for apostolic power and genuine Christian living. One of the last letters of the New Testament commands us to contend for the faith once delivered to the saints (Jude 3). Instead of doing this, the modern Church as a whole is making war on the few who are earnestly contending for this faith.

No preacher can lead his people into a revival of faith and power in God if he's fighting against the very experiences that would bring these benefits. Every preacher should encourage anyone in his congregation who hungers and thirsts for righteousness, anyone who has even the least desire for deeper experiences in God than what's typical in the average church today. This is the minimum he can do to bring about a New Testament revival.
