But when Christian leaders begin to oppose and resist the Holy Spirit like Israel of old — when even the smallest manifestation of God's Spirit is fought and rejected as demonic — it becomes very hard for others to attain these experiences. God doesn't require anyone to accept these experiences if they don't want them. But every person should at least encourage others to pursue them, even if they don't want them personally.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Fifteen. Did You Know:

- **That Melchisedec was an ordinary man?** He is called a "man" in Heb. 7:4. He had a "descent" or genealogy but it was not counted (Heb. 7:6). He was an ordinary man who lived in the days of Abraham, as plainly taught in Gen. 14:17-24; Heb. 7:1-11. He was without recorded father or mother, beginning of days and end of life, so that he could be a type of Christ, who really had no beginning or end of days and who was to be a priest after his order forever (Heb. 6:20).

- **That people have appeared to men on Earth after death?** Although this is not true of men in general, as proven in Lk. 16:19-31, it has been true of special cases when God willed it, as is clear in the cases of Moses (Mt. 17:3) and of the saints resurrected after Christ was resurrected (Mt. 27:52). Moses could not have been resurrected because Christ had not yet become the first-fruits of all that slept (1 Cor. 15:23). He appeared in his soulish form, as explained in Question 8 below.

- **That there will be male and female in Heaven?** This is clear from the fact that every person will receive his own body in the resurrection (1 Cor. 15:35-58). The Scripture in Gal. 3:28 simply means that male and female have the same privileges in the gospel and in Christ. No Scripture teaches that any person will be de-sexed by the resurrection.

- **That there may be baby bodies in Heaven?** The fact that every person will receive his own body in the resurrection proves this (1 Cor. 15:35-58). Paul illustrates the resurrection by small and large grains reproducing their own size and kind, so also is the resurrection of the dead. Nothing in nature continues to grow after death. This is one reason we shall know as we are known (1 Cor. 13:12). On the other hand, all may be resurrected fully mature like any normal seed when it is reproduced and fully grown.

- **That the Bible plainly says that the Earth is round?** See Isa. 40:22. When "four corners" are mentioned it means four quarters or directions (Isa. 11:11-12; Mt. 24:31; Rev. 7:1-3).

- **That the devil still has access to Heaven?** He will have access to Heaven until the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week or the last seven years of this age, as proven in Rev. 12:7-12. Then he will be cast out to the Earth and his place will be found no more in Heaven as the accuser of the brethren. (See Supplement Six, Question 6, for proof that all of Rev. 4–22 must be fulfilled after the rapture and during the last seven years of this age.)

- **That Jesus Christ Himself went to Hell?** See Ps. 16:10; Acts 2:27-31; Mt. 12:40; Eph. 4:7-11, which proves that He did go to Hell.

- **That souls of men after death wear clothes?** Moses wore clothes when he appeared with Christ (Mt. 17:3) and the souls under the altar were given clothes to put on (Rev. 6:9-11). See also the last paragraph of Lesson Four, Point 7.

- **That Judas was one time a saved man?** He had the Holy Spirit and power like the other apostles and was a successful preacher and healer like them (Mt. 10:1-20; Mk. 6:7-13). He had his name written in Heaven, as proven by Acts 1:20 with Ps. 69:25-29. He fell from the apostleship by transgression (Acts 1:25). These and other facts that will be given in detail in Lesson Thirty-five prove he was one time a saved man.

- **That Judas was not a devil from the beginning?** There is no such statement in the Bible. He was "a devil" or an adversary against Christ toward the latter part of His ministry and this is all the Bible does say about the question (Jn. 6:70). He was not always an adversary for he was once a saved man, as proven above.

supplements.title: Supplement 16
supplements.content:
# Supplement Sixteen

**For Lessons Thirty-one and Thirty-two**

## The Plan of God is Simple

Throughout our lessons and supplements, we've been showing you that God's plan for humanity is remarkably simple to understand. By now, you can see just how crystal clear the Bible really is on every subject it addresses.

You've probably marveled at how straightforward God's Word becomes when you read it literally, the same way you'd read any other book. For too long, this Book has been held up as some great mystery—supposedly so spiritual, so heavenly, and so supernatural that only a select few "specially anointed" teachers could possibly unlock its secrets.

But despite all the ways men have reinterpreted and changed it over the centuries, it remains a very simple, sensible, logical, and understandable book. Our eyes are opening now to the deceptions—both human and demonic—that have worked so hard to mystify and confuse sincere believers about what God plainly says regarding life now and in eternity.

## The Will of God Made Plain

We've seen clearly that God's will for His children is that they receive from Him—and His abundant supply—everything they need in this life and the one to come. We've pointed out that God loves His own children infinitely more than we could ever love ours.

He gladly gives His children all the good things of life. His promises cover every known need—body, soul, and spirit—forever. His work in providing these benefits is already finished.

The only thing standing between any person and full enjoyment of these blessings is their own failure to take the revealed steps necessary to receive them.

## No Excuse for Ignorance Possible

At this point, there's simply no excuse for not knowing. You now understand God's will and plan for your spiritual, physical, and material needs.

We've provided plenty of Scripture to prove and clarify what you need to know—if you genuinely desire to understand. You've been shown what God's will is and what your responsibility is in obtaining what you need for life.

## No Excuse for Failure to Get What You Want

Likewise, there's no excuse for failing to receive what you want in life. If you understand God's simple plan for you and believe it, where is there room for failure?

Here are some honest questions to consider: Have you earnestly applied yourself? Are you truly convinced that God is faithful to His Word and won't fail to do what He promised?

Have you given your heart fully to God? Are you free from sin and destructive habits that create separation between you and Him?

Have you intelligently taken the steps we've clearly outlined for meeting your needs? Do you accept God's promises as true just because they're in the Bible, or do you believe them from the heart as personal words from God to you?

Have you set your face like flint, determined to receive these benefits regardless of opposition from people or demons? Have you fully resolved that you won't be denied—that nothing will hinder you from seeing these promises fulfilled?

This is your family right and gospel privilege as a child of God. So be bold to obtain grace and find help in your time of need (Heb. 4:14-16; 10:19-23).

## You Have a Part to Do

Remember—you have a part to play in receiving the benefits Christ died to provide. The plan is simple. God's will has been revealed and made plain. You're no longer ignorant about what to do.

Mentally, you should recognize there's no excuse for failure if you'll obey the truth. But that's only the starting point. There must be definite action on your part.

You can't just give mental agreement to truth—you must act on it with all your heart, soul, and strength. You must take the necessary steps before you'll experience what you're seeking.

## Every Problem of Your Life Can Be Solved

Let us state this again clearly: Every financial problem can be solved. Every burden on your heart can be rolled away. Every physical sickness, disease, and plague you're currently facing can be rebuked—and you can be made completely whole.

Every mental struggle and worry can be cleared up. Every spiritual lack can be supplied. Every degree of moral decay can be repaired. Every sin can be forgiven. Every destructive habit can be broken. Every sorrow can be healed.

Every need and want in your life can be met through faithful obedience to God's Word. No problem can remain with you for long if you'll fully seek God and turn your situation over to Him.

## Nothing Impossible With God

You probably already believe this—but you need to become fully conscious of it in your heart. Think of it this way: God made the sun, moon, stars, planets, and everything in the vast universe.

God also created all the moral beings—angels and humans—throughout the universe. He planned for them to conform to His will, look to Him, and depend on Him for the grace and power needed for daily life. He's made an abundance available for everyone.

We proved this thoroughly in Lesson Ten. We also saw in Lesson Twelve why people's needs often go unmet, so review those two lessons.

Check up on your personal situation. Where are you weak, failing, or sinful? Correct those conditions immediately, and you'll be amazed at what happens.

You must confess and magnify God's greatness. Really settle the fact that He is capable of meeting every need in your life. Once this is firmly established, take the next step—ask, receive, and all things will be yours.

## All Things Are Possible to Him That Believeth

This is a direct quotation from God's eternal, unchanging Word (Mk. 9:23). When you fully realize that God is capable and that all things are possible with Him, you should equally settle that all things are possible for you as you cooperate with Him.

Consider the man with the demon-possessed boy in Mk. 9. He had undoubtedly tried every remedy available. He had watched his son suffer unbearable agony countless times—and no parent can witness that without becoming desperate for help from any source.

He had heard about Jesus and brought his son to Him. First he sought help from Jesus' disciples, but they couldn't deliver the boy. When Jesus returned from the mountain, the father brought his son forward and described the desperate situation, including the disciples' failure.

Jesus said, "O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him to me." The man replied, "If thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us." Jesus answered, "If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth." The man cried out with tears, "Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief."

This kind of desperation and faith will always be met by God. Jesus healed the boy and presented him to his father.

We have the same Jesus today—He is alive forevermore and holds the keys of Hell and death (Rev. 1:18; Heb. 13:8). If you want benefits from God, you must have faith—simple, unwavering faith that refuses to be denied.

If you don't want something enough to ensure you don't doubt God, if you're willing to accept "no" for an answer, then honestly—you're not worthy of a favorable response. This is all God requires of anyone, but He does require it of everyone equally without exception before He answers (Heb. 11:6; Jas. 1:5-8).

Since this is God's absolute requirement—and He won't bend from it in the slightest—let it be settled in your heart that unwavering faith must be exercised. Get down to business. Make certain you don't waver, and the answer will be yours.

## Don't Lay the Blame on God for Any Failure

Some people constantly insist they have all the faith in the world and never waver—yet their prayers go unanswered. It's sometimes difficult to get such people to stop blaming God and seriously consider that they themselves might be wavering.

The first step in any such case is for the person to clear God of all blame and take responsibility themselves. This shows both honesty and intelligence by first identifying where the failure actually lies.

If God has promised that He will hear without exception when unwavering faith is exercised, then the blame simply cannot rest on Him.

## The New Testament Terms of Blessings Are Clear

People can argue all they want that God's promises are unconditional—that there's nothing anyone must do to enter grace or receive any blessing. Despite such claims, there are definite conditions people must meet to receive God's promised benefits.

Not a single statement in Scripture says that God's grace and promised blessings on any point are unconditional. If benefits were truly unconditional, everyone would be blessed equally—regardless of whether they're saints or sinners, regardless of whether they pursue holiness and truth or not.

But obviously, not everyone is blessed equally in all things. This proves that certain things must be done to receive what God has promised.

We've shown you lesson after lesson what steps are necessary and what benefits you can receive from God. The best thing for you to do is forget the theories of men that can't be supported by clear Scripture.

You can see for yourself, by reading the promises, what you must do. Don't deceive yourself by casually dismissing these conditions.

It might be something as minor as beginning to question God's will, His goodness, or whether an answer would really be best for you. There are countless arguments of unbelief we've all heard over the years, as we saw in Lesson Twelve.

We must throw aside all these questionings, unsound reasonings, and unbelieving theories. We must stubbornly persist in simple, unwavering faith until the answer comes.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Sixteen. Do You Know:

- **That we have plain revelations in Scripture of eternal conditions on Earth?** Many passages speak of eternal conditions on Earth. These will be fully explained in Lessons Forty-nine through Fifty-two, so we'll simply list a few references here. There will be an eternal kingdom (Dan. 2:44-45; 7:13, 14, 18; Isa. 9:6-7; Lk. 1:32, 33; Rev. 11:15); eternal generations of natural people (See Supplement Two, Question 2); and eternal natural conditions as we know them now—minus the curse and its effects. In other words, the natural conditions that would have continued if humanity had never fallen will continue in the New Earth forever (Gen. 8:22; Isa. 65:17; 66:22-24; 2 Pet. 3:13; Rev. 21-22).

- **That ordinary angels do not have wings?** They've appeared to humans many times without wings—otherwise they would have been immediately recognized as angels (Heb. 13:2; Gen. 18; 19; Judg. 13:6-20; Mt. 28:2-5; Acts 1:11). Only Seraphim, Cherubim, and special classes of angels have wings, as explained in Lesson Six.

- **That the theory of evolution cannot be reconciled with the biblical story of creation?** The "missing link" between humans and beasts has never been found—and never will be—because there is no link missing between them. God created humanity as a fully-grown, intelligent person in one act. In one day He made both humans and land animals as separate creatures and gave them the law of reproduction: everything after its own kind. That's how it has been ever since (Gen. 1:26-31; 2:7-10; 7:14). Throughout Scripture, humanity has always been treated as lord over all animals—the one responsible for any curse upon other creatures. There's simply no way to believe both the Bible and the nonsense of evolution at the same time. Evolution is not a proven fact in any realm—it's really "devilution," a deception designed to discount God and His wonderful works. If anyone can conceive of the vast universe evolving from nothing into the most intelligent human being, they have a seriously warped understanding. The world's greatest scholars have never believed in evolution, declaring it isn't even science—just a theory. So let's forget it once and for all and remain faithful to God's inspired revelation, the Bible. The following ode to a fly by Rev. Thomas H. Nelson, L. L. B., illustrates how foolish such a theory is: "Don't be discouraged, poor little fly, You'll be a chipmunk by and by. Ages later I can see, You'll be a full-grown chimpanzee. Next I see with a prophet's ken, You'll take a place in the ranks of men. And then in the great sweet by and by, We'll be angels—you and I. Why should I swat you, dear little fly? Prospective chum of my home on high. This is what Darwin says—not I."

- **That the ten tribes of Israel were never lost any more than the two tribes?** All the tribes of Israel were scattered among the nations during the time that ten of them were supposedly "lost" (Lk. 21:24; Rom. 11). Paul, James, and others recognized this fact (Acts 26:7; Jas. 1:1; Rev. 7:1-8; Ezek. 37). All of them were "lost" spiritually in the same sense that Gentiles are lost (Mt. 10:5-6; 15:24). This theory will be fully discussed in Lesson Thirty-six.

- **That Christ was crucified on Wednesday instead of Friday?** This is clear from the fact that He was fully three days and three nights in Hell while His body lay in the tomb (Mt. 12:40; Eph. 4:7-11; Ps. 16:10), and that He rose early the first day of the week—after sunset Saturday (Mt. 28:1; Mk. 16:1-2; Lk. 24:1-6; Jn. 20:1-10). If He had been buried on Friday before sunset, He would have been in the grave only one night and one day—making Jesus Himself a liar, since He said He would be there three days and three nights. This proves He was crucified on Wednesday and placed in the tomb before sunset that day. He remained dead Wednesday night, Thursday, Thursday night, Friday, Friday night, and Saturday. He was resurrected soon after sunset Saturday, for He had already risen before Sunday morning, as the passages above explain. Therefore, we should observe a "Good Wednesday" instead of a "Good Friday" festival.

- **That capital punishment is scriptural?** Anyone can see this by reading Gen. 9:6; Exodus 20:13; 21:12, 23; Rom. 13:1-7. (See Lesson Thirteen.)

- **That God the Father is coming visibly to the Earth at the second coming of Christ?** This is plainly stated in Dan. 7:9-14; Zech. 14:5; Titus 2:13. (See Supplement Seven, Question 10.)

- **That there will be planting and harvesting on Earth forever?** This is clearly stated in Gen. 8:22 as continuing as long as the Earth exists—and that is forever (Eccl. 1:4; Ps. 104:5).

- **That Syria and Israel will soon make a military alliance?** This is clear from the fact that Antichrist will come from Syria (See Supplement Eight, Question 10) and will make a seven-year covenant with Israel (Dan. 9:27). We'll explore this fully in Lesson Forty.

- **That the Bible tells why the 144,000 Jews and the great multitude of Rev. 7 will not go up in the rapture of the Church before the future tribulation?** The reason is that not one person in these two groups will be saved at the time of the rapture—only the saved will go at that time (1 Thess. 4:16). See Supplement Six, Questions 6 and 10, and Supplement Seven, Question 2.

supplements.title: Supplement 17
supplements.content:
# Supplement Seventeen

**For Lessons Thirty-three and Thirty-four**

In our last two lessons, we've taken a deep dive into the doctrine of salvation and all its different phases. We've discovered that salvation is the all-encompassing word of the gospel — it addresses every human need, both in this life and the life to come.

It's essential that you understand salvation correctly and learn how to obtain full deliverance from sin, sickness, poverty, want, and failure through the means of grace God has provided.

## 1. Salvation From Sin

Scripture makes it abundantly clear that God wants every Christian to be free from sin. Let's walk through some key passages together.

"Thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins" (Mt. 1:21). Jesus also told people to "sin no more, lest a worse thing come upon thee" (Jn. 5:14; 8:11).

He explained, "Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin. And the servant abideth not in the house forever" (Jn. 8:34-36).

Paul addressed this powerfully in Romans: "Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound? God forbid. How shall we that are dead to sin, live any longer therein? . . . Knowing this, that our old man was crucified with him, that the body of sin might be destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve sin . . . Likewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead unto sin . . . Let not sin reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof. Neither yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but yield yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness unto God. For sin shall not have dominion over you . . . Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness? . . . Being then made free from sin, ye became servants of righteousness . . . When ye were the servants of sin ye were free from righteousness . . . But now being made free from sin, and become servants of righteousness, and become servants to God, ye have your fruit unto holiness, and the end everlasting life" (Rom. 6:1-23).

"For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death" (Rom. 8:1-3). Paul also wrote, "And they that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts" (Gal. 5:24).

John assures us, "The blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin" (1 Jn. 1:7, 9).

## 2. Salvation From Fleshly Lusts and Bad Habits

Many teach that the body sins every day while the soul remains free from sin. However, according to the passages above, the body must be holy just as much as the soul and spirit.

Look at these Scriptures that specifically mention the body being free from sin:

"That ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service. And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind" (Rom. 12:1-2).

"Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body" (Rom. 6:11-23).

"Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh. For if ye live after the flesh ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live" (Rom. 8:12-13).

"Know ye not that ye are the temple of God, and that the Spirit of God dwelleth in you? If any man defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are" (1 Cor. 3:16-17; 6:19-20).

"Let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God" (2 Cor. 7:1).

"Putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ" (Col. 2:11-12; 3:5-17).

"That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel [body] in sanctification and honour" (1 Thess. 4:3-7; 2 Tim. 2:19-22).

## 3. Salvation From Sickness and Disease

Healing of the body and perfect health is part of salvation. You can see this clearly in Lesson Thirty-three, Point 1, 42, and Lesson Fourteen.

Jesus died on the cross so that all people might be set free from sin and disease. There's no reason why anyone should go without these benefits (Mt. 8:16-17; 1 Pet. 2:24; Jas. 1:5-8).

## 4. Salvation From Poverty and Want

Within salvation, we find doctrines like grace, faith, prayer, providence, and power that guarantee freedom from poverty and want. Review Lesson Ten and note in Points III, 1, 2, 3, and 4 how God has provided every benefit for your body, soul, and spirit.

Whatever you need from God that is good for you has been promised. If you believe these promises and ask God in faith without doubting, it will be yours.

God does not and cannot lie. When He makes clear and abundant promises that "all things ye shall ask in prayer believing ye shall receive," He means exactly that.

Think of it like this: if you doubt Him, that's your sin and your loss. He won't grant you the answer when you doubt, so you'll have to go without the things you need and want if you refuse to believe from the heart that your prayers are answered.

Wouldn't it be better to wait and not pray until you firmly believe that God is true, that He means what He says, and that you will receive what He has promised? Then when you pray, settle it in your heart that you already have by faith what you've asked for.

If you maintain this kind of faith and don't doubt in your heart, it shall be done according to Mt. 21:21-22; Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; Jn. 15:7; Jas. 1:5-8; Heb. 11:6.

## 5. Salvation From Failure in Life

There is no possible failure in life concerning answers to prayer and receiving what God has promised — that is, if you make up your mind that you're not going to fail.

This means you're willing to follow the laws of faith, healing, and prosperity to the letter without any doubt about the outcome. It means getting your eyes off the benefits and onto God in Christ, simply having unwavering faith.

It means that regardless of what happens to make you think God hasn't heard your prayers, you still believe and faithfully obey the laws of these benefits. It's similar to an athlete training for a marathon — you doggedly take every step necessary to reach the finish line you're seeking.

You can have salvation from failure just as surely as deliverance from sin and sickness. Review all the supplements and concentrate on eliminating any sins or deficiencies that may hinder your progress in getting what you want from God.

Many churches understand salvation from sin fairly well. Salvation from sickness is understood less, and salvation from poverty and failure even less so. That's why we constantly emphasize that God has provided for every known need of the human race, both now and for all eternity.

In Lesson Twelve, we showed why people's needs aren't fully realized. If you want all the benefits that others fail to receive from God, learn from their failures and don't make the same mistakes.

In Supplement Two, we gave you the key that unlocks God's promises. But you must obtain this key and use it correctly. After all, certain benefits from God can only be received by those who are truly born-again and obedient to the gospel.

## 6. Hindrances to Personal Salvation

As we saw in Lesson Six, the hindrances to your personal attainments in God are the devil, his demons, and fallen angels. These powers must be recognized and dealt with intelligently — but not as mere "errors of the mortal mind."

Picture it this way: as God's child, you must overcome these powers and become a master of yourself through Jesus Christ, the Holy Spirit, the Word of God, and the precious blood of Christ. These powers are real and must be treated as such.

Not only should you recognize the devil and his demons as real spirit-beings, but you must fully understand their power, purpose, and tactics of warfare before you can wage successful conflict against them.

These demon powers will wage unceasing war against believers to defeat them in every way possible. They must be cast out to gain victory, and they must be resisted daily to keep that victory (Rom. 6:11-12; Eph. 4:27; 6:10-18; Jas. 4:7; 1 Pet. 5:8-9).

See Supplements Three, Four, Six, Seven, and Nine for the secrets of how to gain victory over sin, sickness, and poverty.

## 7. Take All Problems to God

You must learn to take all your problems to God, regardless of their nature. He doesn't just save from sin, deliver from sickness, and help with a few problems — He's interested in every problem you face.

Take each one to God in simple faith, believing that He hears you, and you'll be amazed at how He helps.

The only requirements are:

1. Believe God is a personal being, not some abstract, impersonal force.
2. Believe in Jesus Christ as the Son of God, accept Him as your Saviour, and become born-again by the Holy Spirit.
3. Take the promises of God that cover your needs, go to Him in prayer, and ask in the name of Jesus Christ for your answer based on the authority of what He has promised.
4. Ask in faith, nothing wavering (Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26; Jas. 1:5-8; Heb. 11:6).

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Seventeen. Do You Know:

- **That the oil of Mt. 25:1-13 does not symbolize the Holy Spirit?** As we learned in Lesson Thirty-one under "The Kingdom of Heaven Parables," a parable is an illustration, and the details of Bible illustrations shouldn't be given separate meanings from the plain literal statements. No doctrine should be based upon the details of parables. Details are necessary to make a story illustrate a point, but once the truth is made clear, the details and the whole story have served their purpose. The oil in the parable of the ten virgins simply means literal oil in lamps for giving light. There are plenty of Scriptures about the Holy Spirit that we should use as the basis for doctrine, so we don't need to take literal lamp oil as proof of anything regarding this question. (The ten virgins will be dealt with fully in Lesson Forty-four.)

- **That there will be day and night in the New Earth forever?** The Bible plainly says that day and night will continue as long as the Earth remains (Gen. 8:22), and that is forever (Eccl. 1:4; Ps. 104:5). When the Bible says there shall be no night there, it refers to the Holy City, which has no need of the sun or moon to shine in it (Rev. 21:23-26). The sun, moon, and stars will shine forever on the New Earth outside the city (Ps. 72:5, 17-19; 89:4, 29-37; Gen. 1:14-18; 8:22).

- **That the serpent in the Garden of Eden was not the personal devil?** The serpent was one of the beasts of the field which the Lord God had made and was merely a tool of the devil to cause the fall of man (Gen. 3:1). The serpent was cursed to go upon its belly all the days of its life, but Satan does not do this. The ordinary snake does go on its belly now and will continue to do so even in the Millennium (Gen. 3:14; Isa. 65:25). There is no statement in Scripture that says the serpent was the personal devil. (See Lesson Nine, Point VIII, 1.)

- **That the present Earth is eternal?** This is plainly stated in Eccl. 1:4; Ps. 104:5. When Peter said the Earth shall be burned up, he referred to its renovation by fire at the end of the Millennium, as we'll see in Lesson Fifty.

- **That every man does not have a set time to die?** Many statements in Scripture disprove this theory. Paul said many Corinthians had died because they hadn't rightly discerned the Lord's Supper (1 Cor. 11:27-31). Children are promised long lives if they keep the Fifth Commandment (Exod. 20:12; Eph. 6:2). People are promised long lives if they obey God (Ps. 91). God made it a law that those who break the law should die for it — not because that was God's set time or way for them to die, but because of disobedience (Gen. 9:6; Exod. 21:12-36; Rom. 13:1-8). People who commit suicide take their own lives before they would die naturally. Many have died in plagues when they could have lived if they had obeyed God (1 Cor. 10:1-18). People die in many other ways that aren't according to God's plan and before the time they should, as we see every day.

- **That the New Testament does not command men to keep any particular sabbath?** On the contrary, it teaches that you can choose which day, if any, you want to keep unto the Lord (Rom. 14:5-6; Gal. 4:9-10; Col. 2:14-17). (See Lesson Thirty-two.)

- **That there will be the greatest spiritual revival in the last days that this Earth has ever seen?** This will occur during the Tribulation after the rapture, as proven in Acts 2:16-21; Rev. 7:1-14; 12:17; 15:2-4. This will be discussed in Lesson Forty.

- **That the soul of no man will ever be resurrected from the dead or out of the grave?** This is because souls don't go into the graves at death. Only bodies go into the graves at physical death, and they are the only things that will be resurrected out of the graves at the resurrection (Jn. 5:28-29; 1 Cor. 15:35-58). Only bodies die at physical death (Jas. 2:26), so they are the only parts of people that will be resurrected. Souls are immortal and cannot be resurrected from physical death (1 Pet. 3:4). However, they can be resurrected from death in trespasses and sins (Eph. 2:1-9). This will be discussed fully in Lesson Thirty-seven.

- **That there are animals in Heaven just like we have on Earth?** This is clear from the fact that Paul said the invisible things are clearly seen by the visible (Rom. 1:20) and from many references stating that there are horses in Heaven (2 Ki. 2:11-12; 6:13-17; Zech. 1:8-11; 6:1-8; Rev. 19:11-21). If some animals are definitely mentioned as being in Heaven and if other invisible things are clearly seen by visible things on Earth, then there must be other kinds of animals in Heaven. We'll list many of the heavenly things in Lesson Forty-two.

- **That there will not be one sick person on Earth after the second coming of Christ?** Everyone will be healed in the Millennium as proven in Isa. 30:26; 32:1-5; 33:24; 35:1-10.

supplements.title: Supplement 18
supplements.content:
# Supplement Eighteen

**For Lessons Thirty-five and Thirty-six**

In the last four lessons, we've covered the great doctrines of salvation and the true security of the believer. We've also studied God's contracts with humanity and seen that there are certain conditions we must meet to receive His many blessings.

Now, some people object to what we're teaching about these blessings. They wonder why God would want to give us these blessings today more than He has in the past. Others argue that since God knows what we need, if it's His will to bless us, He'll do so without us putting forth any effort. Still others reason that if God wanted to give them these blessings, He would have already—so since He hasn't, it must not be His will.

These arguments are really just excuses rooted in unbelief. They prove nothing about what God actually wants for us.

## God Promised to Pour Out His Spirit in the Last Days

God always keeps His Word, and this is one of the greatest reasons for the present revival of faith and power among Christians. On the day of Pentecost, Peter declared:

> "But this is that which was spoken by the prophet Joel; and it shall come to pass in the last days, saith God, I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams: and on my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out in those days of my Spirit; and they shall prophesy. And I will show wonders in heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke: the sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before that great and notable day of the Lord come: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved" (Acts 2:14-21).

## God and Satan in a Final Contest

God didn't just inspire Peter to predict revival in the last days. He also inspired others to warn that satanic powers would become increasingly active in this time (Mt. 24:24; 1 Tim. 4; 2 Tim. 3; 2 Thess. 2:1-12; Rev. 13:1-18; 16:13-16).

But here's the encouraging news: as these dark powers increase, God's power will also increase to counteract them and prove to humanity that He is the true God (Dan. 12:3; Jas. 5:7; Rev. 11:3-7; 12:11; Acts 2:14-21). The gifts of the Holy Spirit will be exercised in all their fullness, and God will once again demonstrate He is more powerful than Satan (1 Cor 12:1-11).

## God Knows What We Have Need Of

Nobody's arguing this point—but using this fact as an excuse for unbelief is inexcusable. Just because God knows what you need doesn't mean He'll automatically meet those needs without your participation.

Think of it like a scholarship fund. The money might be there, set aside for students, but you still have to apply for it. There must be faith and conformity to God's conditions before you can receive these benefits.

The requirement is clear: "But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you" (Mt. 6:33).

If you want something badly enough, you'll put forth the necessary effort to get it. And honestly, the person who refuses to obey God and do what He requires isn't worthy of answered prayer.

If you want salvation from sin and bad habits, you'll repent and refuse to live in sin. You'll turn to God and serve Him with your whole heart. Then—and only then—will God deliver you.

If you want healing, you'll believe that healing is in the atonement, accept Christ as your healer, and have faith that you are healed when you pray.

If you need prosperity or help with material concerns or business problems, you'll take your need to God in prayer, commit it all to Him, and believe He will bring you through.

Whatever you want from God that's promised in the Bible—if you want it badly enough—you'll get down to business and conform to God's laws that enable you to receive it.

## Don't Play With Religion

Too many people play around with religion. We'll use good sense in every other area of life to succeed, but when it comes to spiritual matters? We throw common sense out the window.

Here's a sobering thought: if most people made a living the same way they handle their spiritual lives, they'd starve to death.

Some folks will faithfully carry out the most intricate details in their careers or hobbies to succeed. But those same people won't take time to learn how to succeed spiritually, and they won't obey the simplest laws of answered prayer. Then they wonder why their prayers aren't answered!

They seem to think any hit-or-miss approach is fine when it comes to religion. They're quick to accept unanswered prayer—in fact, they're resigned to failure before they even pray.

If they're seeking deliverance from sin or sickness, they're satisfied with the outcome whether they get what they asked for or not. They take the same attitude with business problems—ignoring divine laws and trying every means of success except God.

Failure in answered prayer is expected from the start. It's taken for granted that things can't be any different. Let someone suggest it might not be God's will for them to get what they desire, and immediately they accept this as settled, unalterable fact. The smallest hindrance causes them to surrender any hope of getting an answer.

This is simply playing with religion. The devil himself stands by laughing at this helplessness, highly pleased at how easily he keeps people from putting forth enough effort to get what they want from God.

He sidetracks people so easily—he knows exactly what to do and how long it takes to turn them from their purpose. He even gets people to blame God for their failures, or convinces them to abandon their purpose if the answer doesn't come the instant they pray.

Some are foolish enough to listen to him. They cooperate with satanic powers and are more satisfied living in defeat than being bold and determined to get what is rightfully theirs in Christ.

## God is Not Responsible for Suffering and Failure

As we've seen before, Satan, demons, and sin cause the troubles in this fallen world. We would have had no failure, no sickness, and no suffering if humanity hadn't sinned and given dominion over to the devil.

This alone proves God isn't responsible for such conditions in the human race. Yet the average Christian will blame God for their troubles.

Preachers write books and deliver sermons on how God wills and causes suffering. On my desk as I write is a book by a preacher titled "Why Do Christians Suffer?" The author spends all his time trying to show that God authors suffering and sends it upon people.

This is only part of the truth. Yes, God made laws, and when people break those laws, they pay the penalties. God is responsible for making the laws and penalties, but He's not responsible for people breaking them, and He's not the one executing judgment. Satan, fallen angels, and demons execute the penalties for sin in the human race, as we saw in Lesson Fourteen, Point VI.

Here's the truth: if God's will were obeyed by everyone, there would be no reaping for wrong sowing. There would be no sin, sickness, or death—no suffering of any kind.

This will be Earth's condition when God rules here (Rev. 21:1-7). This would be the condition now if God ruled here. Clearly, the rule of others on Earth who rebel against God has caused the sin and suffering we see everywhere.

The preacher I mentioned gives many Scriptures to prove God sends suffering, but he doesn't make clear in what sense God sends it. It's because people sin and disobey that certain sufferings are permitted to come upon them.

They have to reap what they sow. Therefore, God isn't directly causing this suffering—people and demons are. All suffering that doesn't result from personal sin is either the work of the devil (Acts 10:38; Lk. 13:14-16; Jn. 10:10) or the work of wicked people and demons who make war on Christians.

Neither of these sources is from God. Just because God tolerates them due to the present curse doesn't prove He wills or sends such sufferings.

Just because Paul said he was appointed to suffer for the gospel doesn't prove God sends or wills suffering. God knew Paul would face persecution for the gospel because it was new and contrary to existing beliefs. Naturally, this new faith would face conflicts, and those who preached it would suffer persecution.

Today, in Christian lands, Paul wouldn't face the same sufferings because Christianity is the prevailing religion. So no Christian today should use Paul's experience to excuse unbelief or give in to the devil's attacks.

Even Paul's suffering mentioned in 1 Thess. 3:3 wasn't caused by physical disease or sin—things Christ bore on the cross to remove. There's no comparison between Paul's situation and modern people suffering needlessly with pain and sickness because they refuse to accept the benefits Jesus died to provide.

When Peter said we were called to suffer (1 Pet. 2:21), he couldn't mean we're called to bear the sins and sicknesses Christ bore on the cross (1 Pet. 2:24). The suffering he means is what Christ endured for righteousness' sake—revilings and persecutions for doing well (1 Pet. 2:20-23).

Christians aren't called to live in sin, suffer sicknesses, and fail in life. Freedom from these things is exactly what Christ died to give us, as we've seen before.

Yes, tribulations, persecutions, trials, and hardships may come to Christians during certain periods due to wars against Christianity, and all believers are called to endure during such times. This caused the sufferings in the early Church.

Local persecutions for doing good may happen anywhere. Temptations and spiritual warfare with demons occur wherever saved people are, and these are things believers go through.

But to use these kinds of suffering as an excuse to tolerate sin, sickness, bad habits, financial failure, and poverty in America and other Christian nations? That's one of the biggest blunders in Christianity today.

It would only be if atheism gained the upper hand and Christians faced persecution again that believers would experience the tribulation mentioned in Jn. 16:33; Acts 14:22; 1 Thess. 3:4; Phil. 1:29.

Scriptures like Isa. 54:7-8; Num. 13:33–14:5; Jer. 29:10; Deut. 28:1-64; 33:39 and other passages about Israel's sufferings can't be applied to Christians who obey God. These sufferings wouldn't have happened even to Israel if they had obeyed God.

God permitted Israel's sufferings because of sin. He predicted they would suffer if they disobeyed, because then He couldn't protect them from the devil.

For Christians to use such passages to prove God wants them defeated, sick, sinful, poor, and plagued with calamities shows either ignorance or rebellion against God's Word. There's really no excuse for such slander against God or such ignorance of truth—if only people would be sensible and study the facts.

It wasn't God's will for Israel to suffer those defeats. He showed them the way out if they would have listened. The same is true today—it's not God's will for Christians to suffer such defeats if they'll take His plan and cooperate with Him.

May God save us from this modern fallacy that God gets glory from seeing His children in trouble. He doesn't like seeing them suffer defeat, live in sin, die from diseases, be full of despair and unbelief, or be given over to fallen angels and demons.

The Scriptures mentioned above specifically state why God permitted people to suffer defeats and plagues. They can't be used to prove that God directly wills for people to suffer things Christ died to deliver them from.

God always tells people it's because of sin that they're punished, and this will always be the case (Ezra 9:13; Ps. 103:10; Deut. 28; Lev. 26; Gal. 6:7-8; Heb. 10:26-29; 2 Pet. 2:20-22).

If you'll obey the Lord and follow what we're teaching, there will be no failure to get from God what He has promised. You can get anything you want in life. We've proven this in Lesson Ten and other lessons on prayer and faith.

Don't let modern theories of unbelief rob you of these wonderful blessings in Christ. In future supplements, we'll go more fully into proof along these lines. Until then, we'll continue praying for you and with you for the fullness of Christ's gospel in your experience and in the lives of your fellow students.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Eighteen: Do You Know...

1. **That it is possible for every Christian on Earth today to experience the power of God to do the works of Christ?** We've abundantly proven this in Lessons Twenty, Twenty-two, Twenty-six, Twenty-eight, and Thirty. You can't believe the many plain Scriptures proving this and still hold the unbelieving theories of men. These two positions can't be reconciled. To be honest, we have to stand with the Bible: all things Christ did by the anointing of the Spirit, believers can also do, as stated in Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26; Mt. 28:20; Mk. 16:15-20; 1 Cor. 12:1-11; etc.

2. **That the great American desert will soon be like the Garden of Eden?** Many Scriptures teach that the desert shall blossom like a rose and waters shall spring forth in the desert during the Millennium (Isa. 35:1-8; 43:19). After that, the whole Earth will be made new, like a beautiful garden (Isa. 65:17; 66:22-24; 2 Pet. 3:13; Rev. 21-22).

3. **That more men will be called to the ministry during the last seven years of this age than in any seven years in human history?** During the future tribulation, there will be the greatest revival ever witnessed. When the Church is raptured, people will realize they're in the final years of this age, and multitudes will turn to God and start preaching the gospel. This is predicted in Acts 2:14-21; Rev. 6:9-11; 7:1-17; 12:11, 17; 15:2-4; 20:4-6. There will be 144,000 Jews who get saved and become servants of God (Rev. 7:1-8; 14:1-5). It's doubtful this many Jews have become preachers in all of this age so far. Then a multitude of Gentiles from all nations will be destroyed because of their preaching (Rev. 7:9-17; 15:2-4; 20:4-6). None of these Jews or Gentiles will be saved at the rapture, or they would have been caught up to meet the Lord (1 Thess. 4:16). So they not only become witnesses after the rapture—they become saved after it.

4. **That all future saints will not be changed from mortality to immortality by being resurrected?** All future saints will never be resurrected because God's plan permits earthly saints in the Millennium and New Earth to continue eternally as natural earthly people—just as Adam and the whole race would have lived if man hadn't fallen. After the resurrection is past, people will continue being born forever, as we proved in Supplement Two, Question Two. These people will never participate in the resurrection because they'll never die. They'll eat from the Tree of Life and live forever, just as Adam could have done (Rev. 22:1-5).

5. **That no man can be saved and not know it?** Paul said if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creature—old things pass away and all things become new (2 Cor. 5:17). He also said everyone belonging to Christ has crucified the flesh with its affections and lusts (Gal. 5:24). Can this happen to someone without their knowledge? Such a transformation couldn't possibly occur without the person being aware of it. If this is true, those who don't know they've become new creatures aren't in Christ—regardless of what they profess.

6. **That the devil never enters bodily into anyone?** He's an angel with a spirit-body, as proven in Lesson Six, Point X, so it would be impossible for him to fit his body inside a human body. If he left his body, he would be dead (Jas. 2:26), and the Bible doesn't teach that about angels. The only sense in which Satan enters anyone is by being in union with them, as explained in Lesson Four, Point I, 4.

7. **That it is not God's direct will for any man to be a sinner or die of disease?** If God's perfect will were done on Earth as it is in Heaven, there would have been no sin or sickness at all. When God's will is finally done on Earth as in Heaven, there will again be no death or sin (Rev. 21:1-7). Since this is true, it cannot be God's highest will for people to suffer sickness and die in sin today (1 Pet. 2:24; 3 Jn. 2).

8. **That Noah did not preach 120 years while building the ark?** The fact is, he didn't take that long to build the ark, as proven in Supplement One, Question 4, so he certainly couldn't have preached only during that time. Scripture doesn't state how long he preached. No doubt he preached for several hundred years, since he was righteous in his generation—600 years before the flood and 350 years after, with the exception of getting drunk one time.

9. **That the devil, the beast, and the false prophet are three separate and distinct persons?** The idea that the beast is the devil incarnate and there are only two persons referred to by these terms is unscriptural. This is clear because the dragon (a symbol of Satan) is always seen as separate from the beast and the false prophet (Rev. 12:7-17; 13:1-18; 16:13-16; 19:19-21; 20:10). If all three are seen as separate persons in these passages, then Satan is never incarnate in the beast, and there must be three distinct persons.

10. **That angels will be judged by human beings?** Paul plainly states this in 1 Cor. 6:3. This no doubt refers to the judgment of angels (2 Pet. 2:4; Jude 6-7) and to the position of glorified saints in the eternal kingdom as heirs of God and joint-heirs with Christ over the vast creations (Rom. 8:17-18; Rev. 5:9-10; 21:7). This doesn't mean saints will take positions held by faithful angels from ages past, but that humans will rule over fallen angels. Saints may even hold higher positions than good angels—that remains to be seen.

supplements.title: Supplement 19
supplements.content:
# Supplement Nineteen

**For Lessons Thirty-seven and Thirty-eight**

In these last two lessons, we've seen that you are an immortal soul and spirit, and that your body will be resurrected to immortality. You are the greatest of all God's earthly creations—the only creature made in His image and likeness.

This fact alone is enough to prove that God naturally loves you and makes abundant provision for you in this life as well as the next. He provides for the lesser creations, as you can see on every hand—so how much more will He provide for you?

## Man and Evil Spirits Are Responsible

Of all earthly creatures, we humans are the only ones responsible for our present state of sin and bondage, poverty and want, pain and disease. We have voluntarily submitted our domain to the devil and evil spirit forces, and we—along with them—are responsible for these conditions.

God is not responsible for them in any sense. These conditions would not curse the human race today if God could get every person to obey Him. They should not be in the life of any child of God, and they won't be, when believers come to know the truth that sets them free.

Why would God will such conditions in the lives of His own children whom He has so dearly purchased by the death of His own Son?

## Sin and Demons Hinder Man's Blessings

The Bible is very clear that sin and demon powers are what hinder you from receiving God's blessings: "The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly" (Jn. 10:10).

Faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the Word of God (Rom. 10:17). But faith is often hindered because the devil takes the word of faith out of your heart and mind before it can germinate and bring forth fruit (Mt. 13:19; 2 Cor. 4:3-4).

The Bible also makes clear that we ourselves permit the devil to do this—otherwise it couldn't be done (Mt. 13:15; Jas. 4:7; 1 Pet. 5:8-9; 2 Cor. 4:3-4).

## God's Abundant Promises and Provision

There is no reasonable excuse for the unbelief in Christendom concerning this truth. Paul said, "He that spared not His own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?" (Rom. 8:32).

The Psalmist said, "They that seek the Lord shall not want any good thing" and "No good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly" (Ps. 34:10; 84:11). Jesus said, "If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him" (Mt. 7:7-11).

Many other promises plainly state that you can ask what you will and it shall be done (Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26).

## God is Always True to His Word

You would think every Christian would have enough faith in God because of His promises to tackle their own problems, resist the devil, and receive the many benefits they need so much in life.

But many times, instead of believing God, people believe the devil. Instead of holding to the fact that God's promises are true, they argue against them and seek every excuse under the sun to disbelieve them. Instead of fighting the demons who seek to rob them of God's blessings, they actually cooperate with those demons in destroying faith in God.

Meanwhile, they claim they love God with their whole heart and that the devil has nothing to do with their failures. They generally lay any blame on God when they fail to get what is promised in the gospel.

They're quick to accept the idea that it's not God's will to bless them if there's the least delay or hindrance. It never seems to dawn on them that such opposition could be from the devil and not from God. Even if they mentally acknowledge this opposition as satanic, they don't put forth the least effort to defeat the powers of darkness.

They seem satisfied to do without these things—they'd rather go without than fight a little for them. On the other hand, they'll fight a good fight of unbelief and maintain a consistent faith that such blessings may not be God's will, and thus hinder the answer to their prayers.

Even when they hear many plain, simple, scriptural statements from the Word of God saying they can have the benefits of the promises, many are still hard to convince that these things are true.

## One Thing God Requires of Everyone

Every person who professes to believe in God will agree that everyone should lay aside all arguments of unbelief and have faith that God will do what He has promised and answer prayer. Yet in spite of mentally acknowledging this fact, it seems so hard to get many of the same people to put forth the necessary effort to believe what God has promised.

It is a fact that God will always do what He has promised. It is also a fact that He requires every person to have faith that He will do what they ask of Him.

God demands the same act of unwavering faith from everyone alike. He is no respecter of persons, and He has made it very clear what each one must do to get what they desire. The one thing God requires is faith—unwavering faith (Heb. 11:5; Jas. 1:4-8).

## No Efficacy in Human Works or Good Deeds

There's a prevailing idea among people in general—even among the best of Christians—that you must become good enough for God to hear you, or that long service to God earns you an answer to prayer.

Many pride themselves in the fact that they've been Christians for many years and have numerous Christian accomplishments to their credit. They seem to think that God owes them answers to prayer because of this.

Many times an older Christian is puzzled because God answers the cry of sinners and heals them or otherwise answers their prayers when they come to God, while their own prayers go unanswered.

The reason is simple. The sinner knows he has no human merits or goodness to offer as a basis for claiming the benefits of the gospel. He comes to God as a sinner—unworthy, undone, and helpless. In simple, child-like faith, he surrenders himself to God and throws himself upon God's mercy, asking for those things Jesus died to give him.

He is immediately heard by God and goes away with what he asked for.

On the other hand, some believer who has served God for years comes to God asking and pleading for some answer to prayer—and goes away unanswered. This seems unfair to him and to others who know how well such people have lived.

But in such cases, remember: God does not and will not answer because of human merit, good works, clean living, a perfect character, or a wonderful reputation. God answers solely when a person realizes their human nothingness and unworthiness, and expresses simple, child-like faith in God through Jesus Christ.

## Man Must Learn His Lesson

Sooner or later, you must learn the lesson that you are unworthy of God's blessings, that you cannot possibly merit any favor with God in yourself, and that only by repentance and absolute dependence upon the merits of Christ's work are you blessed.

Think of it like this: a child who comes to a loving parent with empty hands and a humble heart receives far more than one who comes with a list of chores they've done, expecting payment. The parent wants to give freely—not because of what the child has earned, but because of love.

This is the sum total of the Christian faith as far as getting things from God is concerned. Christian men can pray and beg and ask and seek God for something on any other basis, and they will never be heard. Don't miss this truth—it's the cornerstone of prayer and its answer.

When the Pharisee prayed and bragged on himself, he was not heard by God. But when the publican smote upon his breast and said, "God be merciful to me a sinner," he went down to his house justified (Lk. 18:9-14).

Long Christian service, winning souls, godly living, a good character, a perfect reputation, or the most wonderful stage of human living will never be the basis of answered prayer. Learn this lesson once and forever—it will save many heartaches and anxieties in Christian life.

No one will ever get good enough for God to answer any prayer, and no one will ever get bad enough for God to turn them down if they come in faith and repentance through the gospel.

## It is Simple to Believe God

So many say that it's hard to believe God, or that it's hard to have enough faith to get an answer to prayer. But this is a lie and a slander against God and His character.

Why should it be hard to believe a God who has always been faithful to every person who has ever lived? Why would it be hard to trust in a God who has never failed anyone? Why should anyone tolerate such theories and express them in words and acts when there is no foundation for such lies?

We take each other's word without proof. Children believe their parents. Most people will believe the word of those over them in any realm—except in religion. Many won't believe the preacher who declares that God will answer prayer, that God is true, that He will never fail anyone, and that He is under obligation to meet every need according to His promises.

Children won't doubt their parents, and people will seldom doubt those over them—but when it comes to believing the preacher concerning the gospel, that's another question.

## It is Always God's Will to Help Men

How could God be hindered except by unbelief? How could He fail to do as He has promised and still keep the respect of His own creation? How could He fail those for whom Christ died?

How could He will to see them suffer defeat, poverty, want, pain, sickness, sin, and unhappiness? No normal earthly father would get pleasure out of seeing his own children suffer these things if he could help them.

How much more will God help His own children whom He sent Jesus to die for? How much more is it God's will for His children to be happy in life, victorious over sin and evil, free from sickness and pain, and from poverty and want?

God can help His children out of such conditions—He is not helpless like human parents. God has promised to help His children, but only upon the one condition of faith. So it is up to each person to get from God those things that belong to them by virtue of being partakers of gospel redemption through Jesus Christ.

## There is No Excuse for Children of God to be Defeated

This is as true as can be. God is stronger than the devil, fallen angels, men, and all demon forces. The devil is already a defeated foe.

Demons have no power over Christians if they will use the means of grace God has provided for them. Speaking of saved people, the Bible says, "Greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world" (1 Jn. 4:4). If Christians would believe and use the power of God that comes through the Holy Spirit, there could not possibly be any defeat.

God's promises are so abundant and all-inclusive that every believer can get what they want in life—not only what they need. So there cannot possibly be an excuse for anyone to be sick, sinful, helpless, and defeated in life if they will cooperate with God and fight against the devil (Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24).

## The Proper Action to Take

If you could only realize that these facts are true, you would become stirred up to attain to the benefits for which Christ died. But as long as you remain in ignorance and unbelief, you won't become concerned enough to get rid of sins and sicknesses through Christ.

The thing to do is quit complaining if there is any defeat in your life. If you don't sincerely desire these benefits promised by God, then quit laying the blame on God for such defeats. Lay the blame on the devil and the enemies of God who seek to hinder you from getting what God wants you to have.

If you want the devil to get glory and have right of way in your life, then that's what will happen. But if you want God to receive glory from your life and have the right of way in you, then accept the truth that will make you free.

You not only don't need to be sick and sinful, but you don't need to be in want all your days, for God has made full provision for you to get what you want in material things and all other good blessings of life. This we have seen in Lesson Ten and other lessons.

All you need to do to get what you want and need in life is follow the instructions we have repeatedly given in past supplements and in the lessons themselves. Don't fail to follow instructions to the letter.

Above all, never doubt that you are on the right track and that you are going to receive what God has promised. It doesn't matter what your needs are or how impossible it seems to you. God is true to you and will fulfill His will in you if you will fulfill His will by having faith and humility.

## God is Not Responsible

God is responsible for providing all good things for you. He is responsible for making so many unlimited promises concerning every known need of the human race, here and now. He will meet His part of the responsibility in giving you all the benefits of the promises.

But He is not responsible for your attitude toward these blessings. He is not responsible for your reception of these wonderful truths. You are the only one robbing yourself of these fortunes of grace. You are the one holding back the floodgates of blessing in your life.

God has done His part, and you must do your part if you want the good things which you are freely promised. God cannot remain just and righteous to you if He broke His laws to give you these good things contrary to the conditions He has given in connection with the promises.

The conditions are clear. God's Word has gone out of His mouth—He cannot and will not lie. He will do for every person what He has promised all believers. He cannot fail to do His part, so if there is any failure in getting what is promised, the responsibility is on the individual and not on God.

Let the people who want what God has promised do as He requires: have faith in God, nothing wavering, as is plainly stated in Mt. 21:21-22; Mk. 11:22-24; Heb. 11:6; Jas. 1:5-8.

If unbelief is the hindrance, then let it be corrected by a definite refusal to question the will of God, the promises of God, the love of God, and the faithfulness of God. Let there be a dogged faith maintained.

It's similar to how you might stubbornly hold onto something you deeply care about in other areas of life—use that same stubborn determination in connection with faith. Simply reject unbelief and doubts, believe God, and the answer will materialize.

## Ten Questions For Supplement Nineteen. Do You Know:

- That one-third of God's own angels rebelled against Him when Lucifer rebelled? This is clearly taught in Rev. 12:1-12. In this passage the great red dragon (the devil, as explained in verse 9) is seen drawing one-third of the stars of the heavens down to the Earth. These stars are called "his angels" in the same passage. Since God originally created them, they were His angels. So if they are now the angels of the devil, then they rebelled with him in the original rebellion, as we have seen in Lesson Seven.

- That there are other kingdoms on other planets? It is plainly stated in Col. 1:15-18 that God through Christ created thrones, dominions, principalities, and powers, both visible and invisible, that are in Heaven and in Earth. These dominions and principalities are not all located on Earth, for it is stated that some are in Heaven. The word "Heaven" should be understood as the whole of the heavens, which are inhabited as proven in Supplement One, Question 7. There must be life on all the habitable worlds, and if this is true, then they are organized into dominions and kingdoms as we understand it on this planet.

- That a day never means a year and a year never means a day in prophecy? This theory is purely of human origin based upon the fact that God punished men forty years according to the number of days the spies searched Canaan (Num. 13:25; 14:34) and according to the number of days Ezekiel lay upon his side (Ezek. 4:1-13). People have used this as a basis for changing a day to a year or a year to a day in Scripture as their fancy led them. This has led to innumerable fallacies concerning Bible dates and prophetic truths. There is no basis for such a theory, for in the above cases a day always means a day and a year always means a year. And so it is in all Scripture. Forty days will always be forty days and not forty years, and forty years will always be forty years and not forty days. To deviate from these facts is to follow foolish reasoning concerning Bible interpretation.

- That this age cannot end any day as some teach? There are many prophecies yet to be fulfilled and a number of years to come to pass before this age ends. There will be at least seven years that Antichrist will be here before the age ends (Dan. 9:27; Rev. 13:5). How many more years there will be before he comes is not clear, so it is impossible for this age to end any day, as taught by many Bible teachers. Many Scriptures will be given to prove this in the next two lessons.

- That the Church does not have to go through the Tribulation to be purified? There is no such statement in Scripture. If there was, then it would be necessary for all the dead saints to be resurrected and live in the Tribulation in order to go through the same process of cleansing—and this no one teaches. It is the blood that cleanses from sin and not a period of tribulation, and this will always be the case (Eph. 1:7; 1 Jn. 1:7; Rev. 1:5). The Church is ready and always has been ready, for all the true believers in Christ are new creatures in Christ, and as long as they stay in Christ they are ready. Those out of Christ are never ready, and there is no statement in Scripture that Christ cannot come back for those that are ready until certain ones who are not saved get ready. On the contrary, all in Christ are ready at all times and will be raptured together at the proper time (1 Thess. 4:13-17; 1 Cor. 15:23; Eph. 5:27).

- That a literal earthly kingdom will continue on this planet forever? This is proven from the following Scriptures (Dan. 2:44-45; 7:9-14, 18; Isa. 9:6-7; Lk. 1:32-33; Rev. 1:5; 5:10; 11:15; 22:5-6; Zech. 14; etc.).

- That the Earth will not be desolate during the Millennium? There is not one statement in Scripture to support such a theory. On the contrary, Rev. 20 speaks of the saints reigning on the Earth over natural men, and at the end of the Millennium many people will rebel against the government and will be destroyed by fire. At the second coming of Christ, before the Millennium, the kingdom will be set up and it will continue forever (Mt. 13:42-43; 25:31-46; Rev. 19:11-21; 20:1-15; Zech. 14; Dan. 2:44-45; 7:9-14, 18; Heb. 12:25-28). People will go up to Jerusalem to worship year by year in the Millennium (Isa. 2:1-4; Zech. 14:16-21), so there will be plenty of people on Earth during that time.

- That the next form of government on Earth will be a theocracy? This means government by God through Jesus Christ and the saints as taught in Dan. 2:44-45; 7:13-14, 18; Zech. 14; Mt. 25:31-46; Lk. 1:32-33; Rev. 11:15; 20:1-10; 22:5-6.

- That Jerusalem will soon be the world's center of law and religion? This will be in the Millennium and eternal ages when Christ reigns in Jerusalem. Then the law and the Word of the Lord shall go out from Jerusalem to the ends of the Earth (Isa. 2:1-4; Mic. 4; Zech. 14; Joel 3; Ezek. 43:7; Jer. 17:25).

- That over one billion people who have never heard of Jesus Christ will be evangelized in less than one hundred years? There is no question but what we are in the very last days of this age, and we will be in the Millennium in less than one hundred years. It will be then that the knowledge of the Lord will fill the Earth as the waters cover the sea (Isa. 2:1-4; 11:9). This means that when Jesus comes, the billion or more people who have never heard the gospel will hear and be evangelized.

supplements.title: Supplement 20
supplements.content:
# Supplement Twenty

**For Lessons Thirty-nine and Forty**

In Lessons Thirty-nine and Forty, we really begin our deep dive into prophecy! I encourage you to use my book, *Revelation Expounded*, alongside these lessons and all the ones that follow. That book goes into more detail on some prophetic subjects than we can cover in the lessons themselves. These lessons walk through 110 great prophetic future wonders — from 1949 all the way into eternity. In this supplement, we want to answer more fully the modern theory that God is somehow responsible for sin, sickness, poverty, and want in the lives of Christians.

## God is Not Responsible for the Conditions of the Fall

Many modern preachers teach that God is the one responsible for original sin and for the sufferings of His children — that He actually appoints and calls people to suffer and even determines their suffering and how far it will go. This theory completely ignores all the satanic opposition and the conditions caused by the willful rebellion of man and the Fall. It places all the blame for these miserable conditions upon God and reasons that since they exist, they must be God's will — and that Christians should accept these things as "blessings in disguise."

The Bible does not teach any of the above theories. Such teaching impeaches the character of God and makes Him the most unjust, heartless, and despotic Being in the universe. The arguments used by these modern teachers to prove such fallacies are given and refuted in the points below.

### Suffering May Arise From Doing Good

This is no doubt true in the case of Christ and many others whose purpose in life was to do good. Because of holy living and preaching truth that condemned the lives of people around them, they had to undergo much suffering. But such suffering was not caused by God — it was caused by evil men and demons.

Neither was such suffering that of physical sickness, sin, and failure in life. Some of them had to go without material needs at times because Christianity was a new religion and had to become established in the world. However, today, since it is the prevailing religion of our country, there is no need for Christians to suffer for lack of material needs and undergo persecutions like some of the founders of Christianity did.

Therefore, such an argument — that suffering may result from doing good — does not prove that God wills and sends sin, sickness, poverty, and failure to Christians in order to keep them humble and godly.

If there is ever again persecution of Christians similar to what early believers endured, it will be necessary to suffer hardships, poverty, beatings, stonings, killings, and many other things people naturally suffer in such times. But even then, it will not be necessary for Christians to suffer sin, sickness, and failure in attaining the benefits of the gospel that belong to them under all circumstances.

### Bible Characters Furnish No Excuse to Fail God

The cases of Paul, Timothy, and Epaphroditus are given by some men today to prove that all Christians must suffer. It is true all must suffer some trials, but this does not mean Christians must suffer sickness, sin, and poverty, as we have seen in Lesson Fourteen, Point VIII, 6-9.

There is no statement in Scripture that Paul had sore eyes or some disease of the body. He was beaten with rods, stoned to death, and suffered many physical sufferings at the hands of mobs — and to these infirmities he does refer (2 Cor. 11:23-31; 12:7-9). But he has never even hinted in one passage that he bore sicknesses in his own body that Christ died to heal him of.

No other case is given in Scripture where a man was in the total will of God and yet was constantly sick because of God's will. All sicknesses, sins, and failures to get what the gospel provides are not the work of God. All such is the work of the devil and the failure of man to cooperate with God and have faith for the benefits of the Gospel.

### Two Kinds of Suffering

Consider the suffering of Paul and Silas "because of casting out the devil from the fortune-telling girl" (Acts 16:16-24); "the suffering and death of Stephen because he preached the truth to the Jews" (Acts 7:54-59); "suffering as the result of right living" (1 Pet. 3:14; 2 Tim. 3:12); "suffering persecution for the gospel" (2 Tim. 1:8; 1 Pet. 4:14); "suffering because of a life of prayer" (Dan. 6:10-12); "suffering because of fearless preaching" (Mt. 14:1-11). All like sufferings are listed by many Bible writers as proof that it is the will of God for Christians to live in sin and to suffer pain, sickness, and poverty.

There are two kinds of suffering, and it seems that some teachers do not know the difference between the two. Naturally, all Christians will suffer persecution to the extent they are different from the world. They will suffer like the early Christians when they get power from God to cast out demons and defy modern religion in certain churches who deny the many great truths of the gospel.

They will suffer in any definite and marked conflict between themselves and the ungodly of the world — and especially between themselves and the church members who oppose the gospel. But while it is true they will always suffer these things, it does not mean that they are to live in sin, suffer sicknesses, diseases, and pains for which Christ died to take away from believers. It does not mean that Christians cannot be prosperous during this present time of equal opportunity in business between themselves and sinners.

Think of it like two employees at the same company: one faces pushback from coworkers for doing the right thing, while the other avoids conflict by compromising. The first employee may face hardship for integrity — but that's different from the company itself making them sick or poor. Persecution for righteousness is one thing; bondage to sin and sickness is another entirely.

If Christ and the gospel cannot save from sin and deliver one from its power and dominion, then all men are sinners and all will go to Hell. If there is no power in Christianity to make men live free from sin and to make them different from sinners, then there is no difference and all are lost because of sinful lives.

If the gospel does not provide healing for the body and help in financial problems, Christians have no greater blessings than do sinners — so of what benefit is the gospel? If all the promises in the Bible are lies and God did not mean what He said, then the Bible should be thrown away and rejected as the rule of faith and practice. If God will not answer prayers when His people need Him, then they are no better off than sinners.

### Sin, Sickness, and Poverty Not God's Will

When men sin, it is not the will of God. It is their will in the matter, or sin would not be committed. When men are sick or fail in business and do not get answers to prayer, this does not prove that they are following the will of God.

On the contrary, it demonstrates that they are failing to appropriate the benefits of God's promises, and for this reason they are not blessed of God according to the gospel. All suffering for sin is not the will of God regardless of what kind of suffering it is. If God's will would have been done in the first place, there would be no suffering and defeat in life.

If men will only obey God and the gospel, they can and will be brought out of the sufferings which Christ bore to take away from them in this life. God does not promise to take away persecutions and warfare with demons and evil men, but He has promised to take away sin, disease, and failure in life.

If men want to suffer these things by following the doctrines of unbelief and failure — thinking they cannot live godly and humble lives — that is their loss. Many are seeing the truth and are getting free from sin, sickness, and poverty, and all other men can enjoy these blessings if they follow the truth of the Bible.

### God Permits or Allows Suffering

God permits or allows all kinds of sufferings, but this does not mean that He sends them. He does not send persecutions or warfare with demons and evil in the world, but He does permit it because His people live in a fallen and rebellious world.

Therefore, we repeat it again: this kind of suffering is not the same as the needless suffering of Christians when they give in to the devil, making them sinful, sick, and defeated in life, and which robs them of the benefits God has promised to give His people while in this life.
