Picture it this way: it's like trying to break free from quicksand while still holding onto the very roots that are anchoring you in it.

If you would resist when temptation first comes, victory would be won. The battle will never be fully won until you renounce all sin and refuse to tolerate being bothered by demons. You cannot play around with sin and temptation and escape yielding to it for long.

Temptation will grow stronger as thoughts are entertained about your former life. If you do have trouble with sin after turning to God, go to Him in earnest prayer. Read the Bible and build up your spiritual life to the point of attaining permanent and complete victory.

Concerning sickness, some get healed immediately and others don't. There must be a cause when healing doesn't come, for there's no respect of persons with God. He will heal and save any and all who come to Him with their whole heart.

The cause of failure is not in God—it's in the individual. God has plainly stated what the cause is: unbelief. This was the cause in Mt. 17:14-21. This is plainly stated as the cause of failure in all prayer (Jas. 1:4-8; Heb. 11:6).

## True Heart Faith Always Works

In Lesson Twenty-eight we studied the doctrine of faith and saw there's a difference between mental faith and heart faith. Heart faith is the active faith we've been learning about.

You can feel like you have lots of faith when you only have mental faith, but that's not enough. You must have true active faith that acts upon God's Word—faith that causes you to act as if prayer has already been answered the moment you pray. This kind of faith won't take no for an answer.

It won't blame God or try to excuse yourself for failure. It won't question or waver about the answer. It counts things done whether they're seen or not.

Mental faith, on the other hand, begins questioning God's will if the answer isn't given immediately. It reasons, wonders, begins to doubt, and constantly seeks ground to stand on—easily reconciled to defeat.

You can always tell your true state—whether you're exercising true faith—if you'll be honest and consider things in light of the Bible. True faith is taking God at His Word. It maintains that the answer to prayer has been granted regardless of what you see, think, or feel. It doesn't look to circumstances and possibilities in the least.

Think of how Abraham acted: he simply believed God and was fully persuaded by God's Word alone that what God had promised, He was able to perform. Abraham's faith was built upon what God said, not what he could see or feel. He was occupied with what God had said, not what he thought his possibilities were.

He refused to reduce his confidence in God in the least degree. He held firm to his conviction that God was true to His Word and would do as He promised. He even glorified God before receiving the answer and acted as if the request was already fulfilled.

True faith will always bring results. If you intelligently seek to know what God says, base your faith upon the Word of God, and determine to obey the Word of faith to the letter, you will have whatsoever you say (Mt. 21:21-22; Mk. 11:22-24; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26).

Don't be discouraged if you don't immediately receive what you ask of God. Keep reading the lessons on faith and prayer. Keep following the instructions in the supplements until your faith is built up to the point where you get what you ask.

It may take time to build up faith, but it's worth all the work necessary to develop strong, unwavering faith. It will provide rich rewards in the end. So keep up an intelligent program of finding out what to do and how to do it—and all the demons out of the Abyss cannot stop you from getting what you want from God in life.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Twenty-three. Do You Know:

- **That there was sin on Earth before Adam and Eve and even before Lucifer's subjects rebelled?** There was sin in Lucifer himself before he caused his disciples to sin. This is revealed in Isa. 14:12-14; Ezek. 28:11-17 where we read of Lucifer being perfect in his ways as ruler of the Earth until iniquity was found in him. Jesus said he sinned in the beginning and was the father of sin (Jn. 8:44; 1 Jn. 3:8; Rev. 12:9; Mt. 25:41). All this happened before Adam and Eve's days, as proven in Lesson Seven.

- **That the devil is a son of God?** He's a son of God by creation, like all the angels and Adam (Job 1:6; 2:1; 38:4-7; Lk. 3:38). He couldn't cease being a son of God, for then he would have to be uncreated—which is impossible. That he was a created being is clear from Ezek. 28:11-17; Col. 1:16-18. Sonship means nothing when sin is committed. God will damn His own sons when they rebel past reconciliation. This is clear because Hell is prepared for the devil and his angels, who are all created sons of God (Mt. 25:41, 46).

- **That the Lord calls women to preach?** Currently there's major controversy about whether God calls women to preach, but Scripture clears this up if we believe what it says. In the Gospels, several women messengers proclaimed the good news (Mt. 28:1-10; Lk. 24:9-11; Jn. 4:28-30; 20:16-18). In Acts 2:16-21 we have a prediction that God would pour out His Spirit in the last days "upon all flesh; and your sons and daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams; and upon my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out of my Spirit and they shall prophesy." Prophesying is explained in 1 Cor. 14:3-4 as "speaking unto men to edification, and exhortation, and comfort." The following passages prove to any unbiased mind that prophesying is for the Church (1 Cor. 12:1-31; 14:1-6, 12, 24-26, 29-33). In Acts 21:8-9 we read that Philip had four daughters who prophesied in the early Church. In Rom. 16 we find Phebe, Priscilla, Mary, Junia, Tryphena, Tryphosa, Persis—all female names—mentioned as laborers in the gospel. In Phil. 4:2-3, Euodias and Syntyche, two women, are mentioned as leaders in the local church at Philippi, referred to as "those women which labored with me in the gospel." Paul speaks of Corinthian women prophesying and praying in church (1 Cor. 11:4; 14:1-5, 23-26). When Paul said for women to "keep silence in the church," he wasn't speaking of women prophesying or praying, but referred to asking their husbands certain things at church. He said, "if they will learn anything, let them ask their husbands at home" and not in church (1 Cor. 14:34-35). But that they could speak, prophesy, and pray in church is clear from 1 Cor. 11:4; 14:3-4, 23-26; Acts 2:16-21. Women have been used by God in all ages (Exod. 15:20; Judg. 4:4; 2 Ki. 22:14; 2 Chron. 34:22; Neh. 6:14; Isa. 8:3; Lk. 1:39-56; 2:36). To say the least, God is sovereign and can use a woman when He pleases without asking any man whom He should use. Besides, if more men would obey God today, it might not be necessary to use so many women to preach.

- **That literal angels will come down from the heavenlies to live among men in the future tribulation?** This will happen when Satan and his angels are cast out of the heavenlies to Earth to be among men for 3½ years (Rev. 12:7-12). The devil and his angels will cause the great woe of Rev. 12:12. The devil will likely come down visibly to men at this time—otherwise they wouldn't know he's among them in a different sense than now. As we've seen in Lesson Six, angels have appeared to men in visible form, which is no doubt what will happen then. God's angels will also be seen and heard visibly by men at this time, as proven in Rev. 14:6-11.

- **That men cannot be saved after taking the mark of the beast?** This is plainly stated in Rev. 14:9-11, which says that when men take the mark, they're doomed to eternal torment—so they cannot be saved and still go to eternal Hell.

- **That one-third of men on Earth will be destroyed in one hour?** This will happen under the sixth trumpet judgment when 200 million demon horsemen will be liberated from the Abyss to slay one-third of men at a certain hour (Rev. 9:12-21).

- **That many men now living on Earth will personally stand before Christ to be judged before they die?** This will take place at the judgment of the living nations at Christ's second advent (Mt. 25:31-46). At that time many will be destroyed in eternal Hell (Mt. 25:41, 46; 13:40-42; 24:51). That we're living at the very end of this age, during the last generation before the second advent, is clear. Many signs point to Christ's soon return, and many prophecies are now being fulfilled, as we've seen in Lesson Forty-four. Many people living today will be alive when Christ comes to set up His kingdom—certainly many children now being born.

- **That the dead are now in a conscious state between death and resurrection?** We've abundantly explained this in Lesson Thirty-seven, which can also be seen in Lk. 16:19-31; Rev. 6:9-11; Isa. 14:9; Ps. 16:10; 1 Pet. 3:19; Heb. 12:23; Mt. 17:3-5; etc.

- **That none of the 144,000 Jews of Rev. 7 and 14 will ever die?** They will be translated to Heaven as the manchild of Rev. 12:5 without seeing death. They're all living Jews who will be taken up to Heaven from Israel when Antichrist breaks his covenant with Israel in the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week, as we've seen in Lesson Forty-five.

- **That it is not a sin today to eat pork?** Unclean animals were forbidden in the Law of Moses, but this law has been done away with, as proven in Lessons Seventeen and Thirty-two. Now under the New Testament, men are permitted to eat any animal, as proven in the following passages (Rom. 14:1-23; Col. 2:14-17; 1 Tim. 4:1-8).

supplements.title: Supplement 24
supplements.content:
# Supplement Twenty-four

## For Lessons Forty-seven and Forty-eight

In our last supplement, we covered a lot of ground together — why Christians sometimes fail, the importance of knowing the truth that sets us free, how God is visiting His people today, why it's God's will to heal, how Satan and demons operate and how to overcome them, and why some people don't get deliverance from demonic powers. Now, let's tackle a question that sparks a lot of debate: Is healing really part of Christ's atonement?

## Is Healing for the Body in the Atonement?

Many modern preachers argue that bodily healing isn't included in the atonement. One writer puts it this way:

> "This chapter is written to help the Christian who is seeking light on the subject of healing, and to expose the error which is taught by so-called divine healers. The author does not limit the power of God, nor does he deny the fact that at times God does heal a Christian. We believe that God heals whom He wills, when He wills, and how He wills. We do not believe that healing is in the atonement in the sense that each time a Christian is sick God must heal him and raise him up. God does not always heal and raise up Christians who are sick. It is not always the will of God to heal a Christian. If healing were in the atonement, a Christian would never die... There is not one verse in all of God's Word where atonement was made for sickness."

Let's be direct here — these statements are completely unscriptural and misleading for anyone genuinely seeking truth about healing. The whole tone of this theory destroys faith in God and His Word.

Think about what such statements actually imply: they make God out to be a liar, they make Him a respecter of persons, they limit His power to only a select few whom He chooses to heal at certain times, and they turn Bible promises into empty words and the atonement into a sham.

And that argument about Christians never dying if healing is in the atonement? That shows a willful ignorance of the truth. Here's the thing — Christ atoned for the sins of the whole world, so by that same logic, we could argue that no sinner will ever be lost. Just because atonement is made doesn't mean everyone automatically receives its benefits.

Think of it like a trust fund set up for you by a wealthy relative. The money is there, it's real, it's yours — but you still have to go to the bank and claim it. One must appropriate the blessings of atonement, and this is exactly why some people remain unsaved while others aren't healed or blessed — they haven't claimed by faith what Christ freely provided.

## Atonement is Made for Both Sin and Sickness

Scripture couldn't be clearer on this point. Every prayer answered and every benefit received through faith comes because of Christ's atoning work. All faith in God springs from hearing His Word, and God's Word has always been rooted in the highest good for everyone, as well as in Christ's redemptive work.

The entire Bible tells one story: creation, Fall, and then redemption of humanity through Jesus Christ. God doesn't do anything based on our own merits — He does everything because of His grace that flows through Christ.

The Old Testament revealed faith in the coming Christ through animal sacrifices. In Gen. 3:21; 4:1-7; Heb. 11:4, we find the first recorded instances of blood being shed in faith, pointing forward to Christ — the Seed of the woman who would crush the serpent's head and restore humanity's dominion.

From those first records throughout the Old Testament, people continued offering animal blood as evidence of their faith in Christ (Gen. 8:20; 12:8; 13:4, 18; 15:9-12; etc.). These altars where blood was shed stood as testimony that without the shedding of blood there can be no remission of sin (Heb. 9:22).

## Sickness is the Result of Sin

All of Scripture makes clear that if sin had never entered the world, there would be no sickness — death came through sin (Rom. 5:12-21). When all sin is finally removed from the Earth, sickness will be gone forever (Rev. 21:1-7).

Sickness has always resulted from sin — not always from the personal sin of the sick person, but because sin exists in the world. This is explained in Lesson Fourteen, Point VI, and illustrated by many cases of sickness in Points IV and V of that same lesson.

God stated plainly in His covenants with Israel that sickness would come when sin is committed (Exod. 15:26; Deut. 28:15-68; 30:15-20). Plague after plague has struck humanity because of sin, as we see throughout Lesson Fourteen.

## Remedies for Sickness Are Prayer and Atonement

As we've discussed before, all prayer is based on redemptive work. God doesn't promise to hear prayer except when it's offered in faith based on His Word and grace through Jesus Christ (Jn. 1:17; 3:14-16; Acts 3:16; Rom. 8:32; Gal. 3:6-29; Eph. 1:7; Col. 1:14-23).

Scripture records many instances of atonement being made during times of sickness (Exod. 32:1-35; Num. 16:21-30, 46-50; Num. 21:7-9 with Jn. 3:14-16; 1 Sam. 5:1–6:21; 2 Sam. 24:10-25). See "22 Proofs that Bodily Healing is in the Atonement" in Lesson Fourteen, point VII.

## Jesus Bore Both Sin and Sickness in His Body On the Cross

Since sickness entered the world as a result of sin, there can be no true remedy outside of Christ's redemption. Satan and demons propagate sickness (Acts 10:38). Nature has certain healing capabilities, and doctors can assist nature in many ways.

But when disease gets beyond what nature and medicine can handle, God must supernaturally intervene or the person will die. Honest physicians admit they can't actually heal — they can only assist nature. Drugs can destroy germs, and when that happens, people naturally get better.

However, certain diseases have no medical cure. No single drug has been developed that cures every disease or kills every germ. Sometimes a physician's help simply isn't enough.

On the other hand, God can kill every germ, cure every disease, and completely repair the body regardless of the problem. But God will do this only on the basis of atonement — when proper prayer, confession of sin, and faith in Christ's blood has been exercised.

The following passages prove Christ bore the sins and sicknesses of all people in His body on the cross:

> "They brought unto him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word, and healed all that were sick: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaiah the prophet, saying, himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses" (Mt. 8:16-17).

This quote comes from Isa. 53, which according to the literal text reads:

> "He was despised and forsaken of men; a man of pains, and acquainted with sickness... Surely he hath borne our sicknesses, and carried our pains, yet we did esteem him violently beaten, slain of God, and degraded. But he was slain for our crime, he was beat to pieces for our guilt; the corrections of our peace was upon him; and with his wounds we are healed... yet it pleased the Lord to beat him to pieces; he hath made him sick: when thou wilt make his soul an offering for sin" (Isa. 53:3-5, 10).

According to Dr. Young, this passage reads:

> "A man of pains and acquainted with sickness... Surely our sicknesses he hath borne, and our pains he hath carried them, and we—we have esteemed him plagued, smitten of God and afflicted... and by his bruise there is healing for us... And Jehovah hath delighted to bruise him; he hath made him sick."

Peter expresses the same truth:

> "Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sin, should live unto righteousness by whose stripes ye were healed" (1 Pet. 2:24).

If these Scriptures mean anything, they mean that on the cross Christ healed all who will believe. He took our pains and sicknesses in His own body on the tree so we wouldn't have to continue in them after we're redeemed. He was bruised, beaten, and wounded to heal our bodies. Healing is in the atonement.

All of our sins and sicknesses were forgiven and healed on the cross, and forgiveness and healing flow from the cross to all who believe.

The Hebrew word for "griefs" in Isa. 53:3-4 is *choli*. It's translated "disease" (2 Ki. 1:2; 8:8-9; 2 Chron. 16:12; 21:18; Job. 30:18; Ps. 38:7; Eccl. 6:2); "sick" (Isa. 1:5); "sickness" (Deut. 7:15; 28:61; 1 Ki. 17:17; 2 Ki. 13:14; 2 Chron. 21:15-19; Ps. 41:3; Eccl. 5:17; Isa. 38:9, 12; Hos. 5:13); and "sicknesses" (Deut. 28:59).

The root word from which *choli* comes is the common word for "sickness" and is rendered "sick" 34 times. It's never used of spiritual sickness — which some preachers claim is the only kind of sickness the atonement covers (Gen. 48:1; 1 Sam. 30:13; 1 Ki. 14:1; 2 Ki. 1:2; 8:7; 13:14; etc.).

The Hebrew word for "sorrows" in Isa. 53:3-4 is *makob*. It's translated "pain" (Job 33:19; Jer. 51:3); "grief" (2 Chron. 6:29; Ps. 69:26); and "sorrows" (Ps. 32:10; 38:17; Eccl. 1:18; 2:23; Jer. 30:15; 45:3; Lam. 1:12, 18; Exod. 3:7; Isa. 53:3-4). *Makob* comes from the root word *kaab*, meaning "to feel pain" (Job 14:22) and "to be sore" (Gen. 34:25; Job. 5:18).

The Hebrew words for "borne" and "carried" in Isa. 53:4 mean to "lift up, to convey, to carry away, remove, to take upon one's self the responsibility of, and to suffer that which one takes the responsibility to carry."

This certainly teaches that Christ took in His own body on the cross the sufferings caused by humanity's sin and rebellion. He became sin for our sin that we might be saved from our sins. He became sick for our sickness that we might be healed of all our diseases.

To teach that healing isn't in the atonement is to make the cross of Christ worthless as far as saving people from the sufferings caused by sin. He became our substitute for sickness just as He did for sin. He removed both sins and sicknesses in His own body on the cross so we might be free from both.

This is why it's just as easy to heal as it is to forgive sins — both were atoned for and borne by Christ so they could be completely removed from us.

If someone could prove Christ didn't bear our sicknesses in the atonement, they could also prove He didn't bear our sins in the atonement. Both our sins and sicknesses were borne by Christ on the cross at the same time.

What does the atonement cover if not sins and sicknesses? This prophecy in Isa. 53 teaches the same substitutionary and expiatory work of Christ for sicknesses that it does for sins. We can teach with full authority that healing of the body is in the atonement.

The fact that both Matthew and Peter quote Isa. 53 in connection with physical healing proves the doctrine of bodily healing is in the atonement. Both writers claim it was our sins and our sicknesses that Christ bore. When someone takes upon Himself what another should bear, that's substitution.

Bearing diseases, therefore, is part of Christ's atoning work. This reveals that Christ is the Saviour of the body as well as the soul and spirit. Healing of the body is in God's will and plan, and it's the right of every believer in the atonement.

It's the duty of every God-called preacher to declare that he believes in healing and then practice it by praying for the sick to be healed just as much as he prays for sinners to be saved.

## No Christian Needs to be Sick

In teaching this doctrine, we don't have to conclude — as the minister mentioned above does — that if healing is in the atonement, a Christian would never die. It's true that no Christian needs to be sick, but it's not true that death only comes through dreadful disease.

Under God's plan, people will continue to die until the rapture, when believers will be changed without experiencing death (Heb. 9:27; 1 Cor. 15:21-58). People can die — and should die — free from the diseases Christ bore for them.

Scripture gives many examples of people dying without having a disease. The first two people who died didn't die of disease (Gen. 4:8, 23). People can live long lives and die of old age without any disease or pain.

This is how Sarah (Gen. 23:1-2), Abraham (Gen. 25:7-8), Ishmael (Gen. 25:17), Isaac (Gen. 35:27-29), Moses (Deut. 34:7), and many other Bible characters died. Today, some still die of old age without disease or pain.

Long life and health are promised to all believers (Ps. 91; Isa. 58; Mt. 21:21-22; Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 1 Pet. 3:10-11).

It's similar to fruit on a tree — it's no more necessary for a person to die of sickness and disease than it is for fruit to be diseased and worm-eaten before it ripens and falls to the ground.

If people were taught the true gospel and Christians could have their faith built up to a healthy state, we would see multiplied thousands of victorious deaths without so much pain and suffering. But the average Christian today is taught so many fallacies that they're full of arguments against this truth. There's so much unbelief and doubt about the truth that we rarely find such victory in death.

## Christ Healed Before and After the Cross

Some argue that healing isn't in the atonement because Christ healed people before He died on the cross. This reasoning is illogical and unscriptural.

By the same logic, we could argue that forgiveness of sins isn't in the atonement because Christ forgave sins before He died on the cross (Mt. 9:1-8; Lk. 7:47-50).

As we saw in Lesson Eighteen, Point IX, sins were forgiven and sicknesses were healed throughout the Old Testament. This was done by faith in the coming redemption of the cross, just as we're saved and healed today by faith in the atonement, looking back to the cross.

## Christ Heals Today As Ever

If Christ were unwilling to heal today during His High Priesthood and exaltation as He did during His humiliation, His Word wouldn't be true. He promised that every believer could carry on His work throughout this entire age and do the same works He did (Jn. 14:12-15; Mt. 28:20; Mk. 16:15-20).

The doctrine of healing in the atonement makes necessary the continuation of the ministry of deliverance to all people who will believe. The atonement is for all people of all ages, and when people return to the true faith of the gospel, they'll get results just as the early Church did.

Wherever and whenever people have had revivals of primitive faith and apostolic simplicity, there we've seen New Testament power confirming God's Word to believers.

Any person today who will take God at His Word can have every promise in the Bible confirmed in their own experience. There's no reasonable excuse for continued unbelief and constant argument about the plain truths of God's Word being for us today.

## Why Bear in Our Bodies What Christ Has Borne For Us?

If Christ did bear our sins and sicknesses in His own body, then we don't have to bear them any longer. In fact, the whole purpose of Christ bearing them was to take them from us — not to enable us to sin and be sick.

In Isa. 53:5, 6, 11 we read:

> "He was bruised for our iniquities... the Lord hath laid upon him the iniquity of us all... he shall bear their iniquities."

The Hebrew word for "iniquity" is *avon*, meaning "evil," "sin," and "punishment." The Lord laid on Christ the punishment of us all — and this literally means sickness and all results or punishments for sin.

This word is translated "punishment" in Gen. 4:13; Lev. 26:41, 43; 1 Sam. 28:10; Ezek. 14:10; Amos 1:3, 6, 9, 11, 13; 2:1, 4, 6; Job 19:29; Ps. 149:7.

If Christ had all sins laid upon Him, then He also had all punishments for sin laid upon Him. Therefore, all people can go free from the punishment for sin. Sickness is a punishment for sin, as is clear from Gen. 20; Exod. 15:26; Lev. 26; Deut. 28; Isa. 58; etc.

If Christ took all of our punishments, this certainly includes sickness.

So in view of Christ's atonement for our sins and sicknesses, the big question now is: "Why are Christians sick?" If they don't have to bear sickness — which Christ bore for them — why do they?

The simple reason is they haven't come to know the truth that will set them free. As long as Christians listen to fallacies that build up unbelief in the atonement and its benefits, they'll tolerate sickness in their lives.

There's no need for them to have sickness, and they wouldn't have it if they would let Christ heal them and take away their diseases.

To be technical about it, it becomes sinful to bear in our bodies those things Christ has already borne for us. It's sinful to rob Him of the glory that rightly belongs to Him in healing people. It's wrong to refuse to let Him deliver us.

His work on the cross means nothing to us as far as the body is concerned until we let Him heal us — just like His work on the cross means nothing to people as far as forgiveness is concerned as long as they continue in sin.

In other words, the part of Christ's work that we fail to appropriate to ourselves — that part of the atonement means nothing to us.

Shall we limit Christ's atonement and refuse to accept the full benefits of the cross? Shall we believe we can have part of the benefits for which Christ died but not all of them? Shall we say Christ died to deliver us from all the penalties and consequences of sin except one?

Shall we believe Christ's work is incomplete and that part of our penalty will be paid by someone else later? Shall we believe God desires only to redeem the soul and not the body?

Away with such false concepts of the atonement! Jesus went to the cross with His whole spirit, soul, and body to redeem the spirits, souls, and bodies of all who are under the curse of the broken law.

Every kind of sin, pain, sickness, failure, and need was fully met by Christ on the cross. Paul declares that the believer is complete in Christ (Col. 2:10).

## Christ Has Redeemed Us From the Curse of the Law

Paul teaches:

> "Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith" (Gal. 3:13-14).

The blessing of Abraham was being justified by faith, having God as a friend to the extent that a whole nation was healed by prayer (Gen. 20), and having all the good things of this life that could come to a human being through the gospel (Gal. 3:6-9).

Didn't the curse of the law include sickness and disease as the result of sin? This is certainly clear from Exod. 15:26; Lev. 26; Deut. 28 and many other Scriptures.

If Christ died to redeem us from the curse of the law, then didn't He redeem us from all sin and sickness? This means no one should be sinful or sick if they have faith in Jesus Christ's redemption.

## Many Diseases Named as the Curse of the Law

Many specific diseases are mentioned as being the curse of the law:

- "terror"
- "consumption" (tuberculosis)
- "burning ague" (fever) that shall consume the eyes and cause sorrow of heart (Lev. 26:14-17)
- "pestilence"
- "consumption"
- "fever"
- "inflammation" (malaria)
- "extreme burning" (prickly heat)
- "botch" (elephantiasis Graecorum or leprosy)
- "emerods" (piles and tumors)
- "scab" (eczema and other skin diseases)
- "itch"
- "madness" (insanity)
- "blindness"
- "astonishment of heart" (heart trouble)
- "oppression" (mental worries and depressions)
- "great plagues"
- "sore sicknesses" of long continuance

Besides these named, we read "all the diseases of Egypt, and every sickness and every plague not written in this book" (Deut. 28:15-69; Exod. 15:26).

Many other physical diseases are mentioned in Scripture:

- "atrophy of the hand" (Mt. 12:10-13)
- "blains" (inflammatory abscesses, Exod. 9:9-10)
- "blemish" (physical deformities such as blindness, lameness, broken bones, crookback, dwarfishness, eye trouble, scurvy, and other imperfections, Lev. 21:17-23)
- "boils," "carbuncles," "ulcers," and "sores" (Exod. 9:9-10; Job 2:7; Rev. 16:2, 11; 2 Ki. 20:7)
- "deafness" (Lev. 19:14; Mt. 11:5; Mk. 7:32; 9:25)
- "dropsy" (Lk. 14:2)
- "dysentery" (Acts 28:8)
- "dyspepsia" (1 Tim. 5:23)
- "epilepsy" (Mk. 9:17-22)
- "running issues" (Lev. 15)
- "gout" (2 Chron. 16:12)
- "hemorrhage" (Lev. 15:19; Mk. 5:25-29)
- "murrain" (Exod. 9:3-6)
- "paralysis" (Mt. 8:6; 9:1-8)
- "scall" (Lev. 13:30)
- "scurvy" (Lev. 22:22)
- "spermatorrhea" (Lev. 15:16)
- "stammering" (Isa. 32:4; 33:19)
- "meningitis" caused by a sun stroke (2 Ki. 4:19)
- "worms" (Acts 12:23)
- "melancholia" (1 Sam. 16:14-23; 17:58)
- "cataract" (Jn. 9)
- "locomotor ataxia" (Jn. 5:1-9)
- "atrophy of the muscles" (Lk. 13:11-17)
- "spasms" (Mt. 8:6; Lk. 7:2)
- "infantile paralysis" (Mt. 12:9-13; Mk. 3:1-5; Lk. 6:6-11)

These and all other diseases were borne in Christ's body on the tree so that people might be free from them.

Since disease is part of the curse, healing of the body is part of salvation, as we saw in Lesson Thirty-three. Since sickness is a penalty for breaking the law, how could God will for us to remain under the curse of the broken law after we're freed from law breaking?

Why should anyone remain under the curse who is not under the old law but under grace? (Rom. 6:14). The fact that God has healed people throughout the ages proves that healing is in the atonement and is part of salvation. How could these people have been healed if God didn't provide for it in redemption?

## Men Are Commanded to Glorify God in Their Bodies

Paul said:

> "What? know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own? For ye are bought with a price: therefore glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God's" (1 Cor. 6:19-20).

He further says:

> "If any man defile the temple of God him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are" (1 Cor. 3:16-17).

These Scriptures certainly teach holiness of body — and that means freedom from disease.

Shall we believe demons are free to defile God's temple without facing God's judgment? Shall we believe the body is to be holy even though it's full of disease and corruptions of all kinds? Shall we believe it's God's will for us to live in sickly, diseased bodies in preference to clean, healthy ones?

Shall we believe God is unconcerned and has made no provision for bodily holiness? Can we possibly imagine that someone can glorify God better with a helpless, diseased, sickly body than with a well and healthy one?

Picture it this way: Normal people like living in clean houses and take pride in them. Are we then to believe the Holy Spirit is the only person content to live in a corrupt and diseased house, and that human beings have more pride and respect than God?

Would you rather live in a clean, healthy house than one that's unclean, diseased, and unfit for occupancy — or is God the only person who prefers this?

Shall we believe that after God bought us with such a great price, He's then willing to turn us over to demons to occupy our bodies and destroy us with torment, pain, and all kinds of diseases?

Would you buy a house to live in and then be willing to share it with drunkards, thieves, harlots, and drug addicts? Wouldn't you drive out such occupants if you could?

Shall we believe God has the power to make His temples clean and healthy, but He'd rather not because He delights to live in filth and disease?

Surely the logic of such theories seems impossible to believe, yet multitudes of Christians do believe these things about God. It's high time we wake up, display some intelligence in religion, and free God from all such accusations.

## Healing is Absolutely Sure

If what we've discussed is true — and it is according to the Bible — then for no other reason than that the Bible says so, we should believe we were healed on the cross by Christ's stripes.

It's true because God said so. It's just as true as the fact that we were saved on the cross.

Everyone can get healed now — right now by faith — just as much as they can be forgiven of sins now. The reason everyone isn't healed is because they don't believe this truth and accept it the way they accept forgiveness of sins.

Just as forgiveness is received by faith in the atonement when someone surrenders their life to God, healing is also received by faith in the atonement the moment someone decides to accept it and refuses to be denied what was freely provided on the cross.

Both forgiveness and healing were atoned for on the cross, but they're appropriated individually by faith when someone meets the necessary conditions of repentance and faith in the atonement. All of Hell cannot rob anyone of either blessing if they refuse to let demonic forces defeat them.

We conclude that all God's promises in Christ are yes and amen to those who believe (2 Cor. 1:20; 2 Pet. 1:4).

Let's lay aside unbelief in the gospel and fully appropriate the benefits of redemption through Christ according to our needs. Let's learn this lesson well: God is true and will meet His own obligations to each person who conforms to the gospel of Christ.

## Ten Questions For Supplement Twenty-four. Do You Know:

**That the Ten Commandments have been done away with?** This is such an important question that we felt it should be brought to your attention again. So many false doctrines are based on the teachings of the Law of Moses, and today we have so many cults that mix law and grace, that it can't be emphasized too much: the whole Law of Moses, including the Ten Commandments, was done away with and abolished in Christ on the cross. This is plainly stated in 2 Cor. 3:6-15; Gal. 3:19-25; 4:21-31; 5:1-5, 18; Eph. 2:15; Col. 2:14-17; Acts 15:5-29; Heb. 7:11, 12, 22, 28; 8:6-18; 9:1, 9, 10, 15-22; 10:1-18. In Lesson Thirty-two we abundantly proved this point.

**That the fig tree of Mt. 24:32-33 does not refer to the Jews?** This parable is a simple illustration of the nearness of Christ's second advent, as is clear in the passage itself. Therefore, it doesn't refer to the Jews at all (see Lesson Thirty-four).

**That Jesus will have scars on His hands and feet throughout eternity?** That He still has a flesh and bone body was demonstrated to Thomas and all the disciples (Lk. 24:39). That such scars on His body are for the purpose of convincing people forever that He actually died on the cross is plainly stated in Zech. 13:6. The phrase "in that day" in this passage refers to the time of the Messiah's eternal reign that we'll study in the next few lessons.

**That there has been a flood on the Earth worse than the flood of Noah?** This flood is recorded in Gen. 1:2; 2 Pet. 3:4-8. In this flood, all life on Earth was destroyed, as we saw in Lesson Seven. In Noah's flood, both people and animals, as well as all vegetation on Earth, were preserved. This proves the first flood was more severe and of longer duration than Noah's flood.

**That the devil now has access to Heaven?** The old theory that Satan can't go to Heaven because sin isn't allowed there is untrue. In both Job and Revelation, we have divine teaching that the devil has access to Heaven. In Job 1:6 and 2:1 we read that Satan appeared twice with the sons of God in Heaven. In Rev. 12:7-12 we read that he'll be cast down to Earth in the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week (the future tribulation), and he'll never again have access to Heaven. We also read in this passage that he's now the accuser of the brethren and accuses them before God day and night. This proves he still has access to Heaven and will continue to until the middle of the Tribulation — 3½ years before Christ's second coming to Earth.

**That saints will not spend eternity in Heaven?** The old-fashioned idea that all the redeemed will spend eternity in Heaven isn't taught anywhere in Scripture. It's true that at death, all righteous souls go to Heaven awaiting the resurrection of the body at the rapture. Then these people will be caught up to meet the Lord in the air to be forever with Him, as we proved in Lessons Thirty-seven and Forty-three. It's also true that the raptured saints will return from Heaven to reign with Christ on Earth for the Millennium. During this time, while their home will be in Heaven, they'll go back and forth between Heaven and Earth throughout the 1,000 years (Rev. 1:4-6; 5:10). Then after the renovation of the heavens and Earth, the resurrected saints will come to live on Earth forever when their home, the

supplements.title: Supplement 25
supplements.content:
# Supplement Twenty-five

## For Lessons Forty-nine and Fifty

Over the last few supplements, we've been tackling arguments from those who teach that God doesn't want to heal or prosper people—that healing and all God's blessings aren't part of the atonement, and that it's actually God's will for Christians to suffer through sickness, pain, poverty, and want. We've shown that it simply cannot be God's will for His own blood-bought children to suffer any of the curses that Christ died to save us from—both in this life and the life to come.

If you'll just believe the truth about these doctrines, you'll never have to fear demons or go without these benefits.

## Prosperity, Healing and Full Salvation for All

If these blessings are for one person, they're for everyone, because God is no respecter of persons (Rom. 2:11). Think about it—would it be fair of God to give these blessings to some but not to all?

Could it really be possible that so many modern ministers are right when they teach that it's not God's will for everyone to receive these blessings? The minister quoted in the last supplement says, "The author does not limit the power of God, nor does he deny the fact that at times God does heal a Christian. We believe God heals whom He wills, when He wills, and how He wills... God does not always heal and raise up Christians who are sick."

What kind of reasoning is this? How could God be just and be no respecter of persons if He only decides to manifest healing at certain times for certain people? How could you ever be sure you're living at that particular moment when God feels gracious enough to do what He's promised—what He promises to do for all and what Christ died to freely give to all?

How can you know if you're a special pet of God or if you belong to some exclusive class to whom God will "at times" feel merciful? Such reasoning and contradictions to the Scriptures aren't worthy of an intelligent person's consideration, to say the least.

## We Live in the Times of God's Mercy

The times of God's mercy have stretched from the fall of man until now, and they'll continue until the end of the Millennium when people will never be sick again. In other words, as long as there's a sick person, God's mercy will be extended to them if they believe the gospel and have faith in God to heal them.

God's salvation and compassion cover every human need now and forever. We saw in Lesson Fourteen that God has healed people in all past ages—before and after the cross—and that God has never turned down one person who came to Him in faith according to the gospel. We've seen that in the New Testament, there's provision for God's blessings for body, soul, and spirit—provision for prosperity, forgiveness, healing, and deliverance from all the works of the devil.

## God's Program is to Bless All Men Here and Now

Yes, it's true that God heals whom He wills, when He wills, and how He wills—but God's program is written out in black and white. Scripture plainly tells us that He wills to prosper, heal, and save all people here and now who will believe in Him.

It's revealed that the time is *now* that God wills to bless people. It's also made clear *how* He wills to help those in need. His program works through faith in the gospel, in the atonement and work of Christ on the cross, and in the resurrection from the dead.

Paul said by the Holy Ghost, "He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things" (Rom. 8:32). We can therefore boldly and scripturally declare that all things are ours here and now.

What good would a gospel be that provided benefits for people in the past and future but left out those living now? The Bible proves that any religion teaching there are no benefits for people of the present age—but that such benefits were experienced in the past and will be enjoyed in the future—is from the devil himself. It's been the program of demons in every generation to rob people of the blessings God has provided for all alike.

## The Great Hindrances to All Blessings of God

The great hindrance to any blessing of God is uncertainty in the mind and heart concerning God's will. Some people raise the question of whether or not it's God's will for them to be saved, to be healed, or to prosper in material things. Others question whether it's God's time to bless them. Still others question whether such blessings would even be best for them.

All such questioning and uncertainty will rob you of the benefits that are freely promised to all alike. How can you have faith if you're uncertain about God's will or if there's any wavering at all?

God has definitely told us that no one should expect an answer where there's wavering faith. This is stated in Jas. 1:4-8:

> "If any man lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him. But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed. For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord. A double minded man is unstable in all his ways."

## What To Do To Stop All Wavering

The first thing to do is study the Bible and get settled on what God promises for all people today. If He's made promises that cover every specific human need, then you can rely on it without further questioning—it *is* the will of God to give you what He has promised.

Get those particular promises that plainly state God's will in granting what you desire, then refuse to tolerate any wavering on the question. Don't listen to conflicting thoughts that oppose God's promises, and don't argue the case with people or devils.

Settle it once and for all that it is God's will to grant what He has promised. Also settle it that any time you truly pray in faith, the answer will be granted. This kind of attitude naturally implies that you believe God has made such provision—otherwise He wouldn't have made these promises.

It's impossible to receive God's blessings by faith if you're not sure He has promised them. Likewise, it's impossible to fail to receive what you claim by faith if you don't waver. God is true. His Word is yea and amen to those who believe, and no one can fail to get what they've asked for when they stand in faith and refuse to waver or question God's will as revealed in His promises.

## The Law of Faith Must Be Obeyed

The law of faith is laid out clearly in Scripture:

- "As thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee" (Mt. 8:13)
- "According to your faith be it unto you" (Mt. 9:27-29)
- "O Woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt" (Mt. 15:21-28)
- "And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive" (Mt. 21:21-22)
- "If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth" (Mk. 9:23)
- "What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them" (Mk. 11:22-24)
- "He that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him" (Heb. 11:6)
- "But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering" (Jas. 1:4-8)

It's an unchangeable law of God that you must believe exactly what you ask in order to receive it. It would be impossible for a sinner to be saved unless they first believe it's God's will to save them and that salvation takes place when they confess their sin (Rom. 10:9-10; 1 Jn. 1:9).

In the same way, it's impossible for you to be healed unless you believe it's God's will and that it's done when you pray. This applies to prayer for prosperity, health, or whatever else you ask of God.

Faith must rest upon the will of God that is fully known through the Word of God. It can't rest on mere human desires and wishes or on physical senses. True faith that appropriates what is asked isn't believing that God *can*—it's believing that God *will*.

## "If It Be Thy Will" Should Be Forgotten

The statement "If it be thy will" should never be used in prayer concerning anything God has already promised. If God has promised something, then it's already His will—otherwise He wouldn't have made the promise.

To ask Him if it's His will to do what He's already promised and made clear is to accuse Him of being a liar. In effect, we're telling God we know what He's promised but we don't believe He's true to His Word. We try to convince God that He should be true to His Word. This is all foolish, unnecessary, and sinful to say the least. God will do what He has promised. It's already His will to do what He says He'll do.

Think of it like this: suppose you had promised something to someone, and that person constantly threw it in your face that they didn't believe you were true to your word—that they didn't expect what was promised. Suppose they went around to others asking them to pray that it would be *your* will to do what you'd already promised, and that you would make it plain you'd actually made the promise.

What kind of feeling would you have toward such an unbelieving person who let you—and others—know they had no confidence in your word? What kind of reaction do you think God has toward those who constantly accuse Him in prayer of being untrue to His Word?

You'd probably stop all dealings with a person who had no faith in your word and character. And so it is with God, who promises He will not hear anyone under these circumstances (Heb. 11:6; Jas. 1:4-8; Mk. 11:22-24).

## Will Men Die if God Always Heals Them?

Certainly people must die their appointed deaths on Earth until the time of the rapture when this law that all must die will be broken (Heb. 9:27; 1 Cor. 15:51-58). God didn't promise to keep people from dying their appointed deaths—that would be breaking His own Word.

But He *has* promised to heal them and keep them in health until natural death (Exod. 15:26; Ps. 91:1-12; 103:1-4; Mt. 8:16-17; 21:21-22; Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; Jn. 15:7, 16; Jas. 5:14-16; 3 Jn. 2). He has promised to take away sickness from us and that the number of our days He will fulfill if we meet certain conditions (Exod. 23:25-26; Ps. 90:10; 91:1-12; 102:24; 1 Pet. 3:10-12).

## What is Included in Christ's Will and Testament?

We made clear in Lessons Ten and Twelve and in other supplements what's included in the will of God through Christ. We've seen that God has promised people all they want and need in this life as well as in the life to come. We've given many Scriptures proving that prosperity, healing, health, and all wants are included in God's will and testament.

All we have to do is read His will. It's similar to a wife who is in possession of her late husband's will but constantly argues, reasons, doubts, and questions what her husband left her—or whether he left her anything at all. Or worse still, after reading the will she worries all the time whether it was really meant for her, or whether he really desired and willed her to have what he left.

You'd say such a woman is very foolish indeed. But she's really no more foolish than the majority of Christians who have the plain terms of God's will in the Bible yet won't read and believe that the benefits of the gospel are for them. They hear people preach and read these lessons that God means what He says—that every promise of God is yea and amen to those who believe—yet it never seems to really dawn on them. They never get it firmly settled in their minds that everything promised in the Bible is for them.

## No Excuse for Ignorance of God's Will

There's no excuse for ignorance about God's will since everyone can read in the Bible what God has left for them through Christ. If you're in doubt about what you can have, read the will of God for yourself.

If someone is left things of value by a will and doesn't want to get the benefits simply because they don't want to touch or use any part of what's left them—that's their fault, not the testator's. If they constantly whine and worry about getting something needed that's been left to them, just because they don't believe it was actually left to them, then let them go without the benefits and keep their mouth shut about being in need.

So it is with Christians. If they don't want the benefits they read about in the Bible because they think it would be asking amiss or it might not please God, then let them go without these blessings. There's no excuse for lacking anything in life, now or hereafter, for all things are freely promised and abundantly provided for all in the Bible.

## Everyone Can Have All That is Promised

Since God is no respecter of persons, everyone can have all that is promised if they'll have faith in God. If you can have any part of what's promised, you can have all God has promised.

If some of God's promises are untrue, then we have no assurance that any of them are true. If you can't get one part of the benefits provided in the gospel, then there's no grounds for assurance you can get other promised benefits.

Paul said, "All things are yours... things present, or things to come; all are yours; and ye are Christ's; and Christ is God's" (1 Cor. 3:21-23). All things are ours that God has promised, for again Paul said, "all the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us" (2 Cor. 1:20). What could be clearer?

## All Answers to Prayer Are for God's Glory

Not only are we told that all things are ours, but we're told the reason—"unto the glory of God" (2 Cor. 1:20). This is the chief reason for answered prayer, whether it's for prosperity, healing, health, salvation from sin, or whatever it might be.

Jesus said, "And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son" (Jn. 14:12-15). When prayer has any other object, it's selfish and useless.

This doesn't exclude meeting your needs—even your wants—to the full. It might seem at first that if we ask for what we want, it's selfish and not for God's glory. But that's not the case. We've already proven it's God's will for people to ask for what they want. This isn't selfish—it's godly and perfectly natural. It's according to the divine and spiritual plan for people to ask what they desire (Mk. 11:22-24; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16).

When you do this, you glorify God because it gives Him a chance to show His power, vindicate His own character, and prove His love for people. It's the only way to confirm the Word of God.

How can God be glorified as long as His hands are tied by people's unbelief? How can He fulfill His Word to them when they don't believe and see God's glory? How can He be glorified when people make Him a liar concerning His promises? How can God prove His love when people refuse it by failing to cooperate with His plan of being glorified?

No prayer for any good thing is selfish, for it's to God's glory to grant all that is asked according to His promises.

## What is God's Desire for Men?

God's desire is that people yield completely to Him and cooperate wholeheartedly with Him for the highest good of all beings and of the universe. It's God's highest will to give to all people liberally without rebuking them for asking any and everything they want for their good and His glory (Jas. 1:4-8; 3 Jn. 2; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26; Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24).

When you ask, you can't ask amiss or selfishly when you're consecrated to the same end God is consecrated to—the best good of all creation. Those who ask amiss are described as adulterers and adulteresses who seek to consume God's blessings upon their own lusts (Jas. 4:1-5).

It's impossible to ask amiss when you ask in faith, nothing wavering, for you'll only ask in harmony with God and His promises. This implies you've been "born-again" and are fully consecrated to God and to the best good of your own being and the society in which you live. (See God's plan for the needs of man in Lesson Ten.)

## Who Has Been Authorized to Change God's Will?

It would seem from modern teachings about the Bible that someone has been authorized to change God's will. Or perhaps we could say everyone has been authorized to change it to suit themselves according to whatever church creed they want the Bible to conform to.

It's a universal idea that you can get any meaning you want out of the Bible or prove anything by the Scriptures. It's also widely accepted that people are free to interpret Scripture any way they choose.

Who gave them this authorization? It certainly couldn't be that God has authorized people to change His word and make it meaningless through every theory we can manufacture to excuse our sin and unbelief. If it wasn't God, then it must be the work of the devil making people think they're free to change God's Word to suit themselves.

It thus becomes clear that demons have inspired such fallacies that lead people away from the plain truths of the Bible and make the Word of God of no effect—worse than the fables of men. When you believe any part of any doctrine that contradicts God's Word, you deny truth to that extent.

God has no part in cheating people out of what He's willed them through Christ. Christ is the mediator of the New Testament. He's our advocate—our lawyer—and He won't cheat anyone out of any part of the will as earthly lawyers sometimes do.

Since the will has never been changed by God, all changes are made by demons and people who seek to break the will and keep the rightful heirs from their inheritance.

## Both Testaments Promise the Same Blessings

As we saw in Lesson Thirty-two, the Law of Moses has been abolished, but this doesn't do away with the many blessings God has promised people in all ages before the cross. Before the law was given, God promised to bless all nations through Jesus Christ (Gen. 12:1-2).

God enlarged upon His program for humanity as He revealed more of His will to bless people when the law was given. In the five books of the Law of Moses, we have many promises of God's providence in every phase of human life (Exod. 15:26; 23:25-26; Deut. 5:16, 29, 33; 6:3, 18; 12:28; 19:13; 28:1-14; Lev. 26:1-13).

In other books of the Old Testament, God continues to promise people prosperity, healing, health, salvation, and even the desires of their heart (Josh. 1:5-9; 1 Sam. 2:7-8; 1 Ki. 2:3-4; 1 Chron. 29:12; Ezra 8:22; Ps. 1:1-3; 23:1; 34:10; 84:11; 91:1-12; 103:1-4).

The Law of Moses—or the Old Covenant recorded in Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy—is the only part of the Old Testament's thirty-nine books that is abolished. The many truths in these books before and after the law was given that were given to people in general and that are taught in the New Testament are still applicable today.

Even the truths of the law that were made part of the New Covenant are still in force. It's only those parts of the Old Covenant that weren't made part of the New Covenant that aren't in force today for Christians.

Such truths as faith in God, salvation from sin and bad habits, healing of the body, health, prosperity, answers to prayer, and those benefits God has made clear are for people of all ages—these are still for us today even though they were part of the Old Covenant. We don't get these benefits by obedience to the old law but by obedience to the New Covenant.

All these blessings have been brought into the New Covenant and made part of it. Scriptures in the Old Testament can be used to prove doctrines that are clear in the New Covenant. Even Scriptures recorded in the Law of Moses can be used to prove that it's always been God's will in every age to bless people with all these good things.

In the New Covenant, however, we have an abundance of proof that we can get all the universal blessings of God that are for people of all ages—past, present, and future. In fact, the glory and blessings of the New Covenant are so much greater than those of the Law of Moses that there's no comparison.

This is what Paul taught in 2 Cor. 3:6-15 when he said the glory of the Old Covenant had no glory compared to that of the New Covenant. So you can see that all the benefits God has promised in all ages can be experienced today in greater fullness because of the power and glory of the New Covenant.

## We Have Exceeding Great and Precious Promises

This is what Peter taught in 2 Pet. 1:4:

> "Whereby are given unto us exceeding great and precious promises: that by these ye might be partakers of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world through lust."

He also said these promises give us "all things that pertain unto life and godliness" (2 Pet. 1:3-4). Such promises in the following passages reveal that nothing is impossible to any believer and that you can have whatever you desire (Mt. 6:31-33; 7:7-11; 21:21-22; Mk. 9:23; 11:22-24; Jn. 14:12-15; 15:7, 16; 16:23-26; 1 Jn. 3:21-22; 5:14-15; etc.).

It would be absolutely impossible for anyone to fail to get what they wanted in life that God has promised if they would take these promises and meet the conditions of their fulfillment. You can have full salvation from sin, sickness, poverty, and want by the gospel of Jesus Christ.

But you must accept these truths and get rid of all questioning, argument, unbelief, and doubt that such benefits are promised by God to any and all believers. Until this is settled, no aggressive faith is possible to attain these blessings.

You must freely and wholeheartedly accept these truths as God's will for you before such doubts can be erased from your mind. When this is settled and there remains no argument against this fact, then you're on sound footing to ask and receive.

## Common Sense Must Be Used

Don't expect that God will lay a million dollars at your feet the moment you pray, particularly if you're not being obedient to the laws of prosperity. God will begin to answer prayer and open up doors that will lead to prosperity.

If you do all you're supposed to do to get into God's will and be led by Him in business, and if you follow the laws we've given before, you'll soon see that these truths are practical and beneficial.

If you want salvation from sin, bad habits, and sickness, you can go immediately to God as we've explained before and receive immediate deliverance. In these things you must expect to do your part. You must renounce all sin and destroy all things that pertain to bad habits and have faith in God for deliverance before it will be realized.

You can't expect deliverance if you get up from prayer and start back into sin again. You can't start drinking, smoking, chewing, dancing, swearing, pleasure-going, or anything you've asked forgiveness and deliverance from and expect an answer. You must refuse to go back into sin if you want God to break the power of sin in your life.

It's not fair to God or to yourself to ask God to forgive and deliver you from the power of these sins and then go right on living in them when you get up from prayer.

It may be true that temptations will come at first. Such temptations will be weak or strong depending on your personal hatred and renunciation of the sinful habits and whether you mean business with God or not.

God won't supernaturally pick you up by the nape of the neck and keep you from going into any place of sin. He doesn't ordinarily knock the tobacco, whisky, or dope out of your hand if you're determined to continue in sin—unless by some special intervention to wake you up.

God can do all things, but we simply mean that in His ordinary dealings with people He demands cooperation to receive deliverance. When you're earnest and seek divine help to deliver you from these habits or to cleanse you from all sin, God will supernaturally deliver you from the power and dominion of sin.

In case of sickness, you must know that healing is for you and that God is your only help. You must turn to God with your whole heart and rebuke the disease and resist demons by faith in the name of Jesus Christ and His atonement.

Don't tolerate sickness and disease in your body. Claim healing and full deliverance and maintain that it is done—and it will be manifested.

In other words, if you want God's salvation for any trouble in life, you must meet the plain conditions laid down in connection with the promises and you will get full deliverance. Let's all have faith in God and His Word and ask in simple, unwavering faith for those benefits of the gospel that are for us all.

---

## Ten Questions For Supplement Twenty-five. Do You Know:

- **That there will not be a general judgment for both the righteous and the wicked?** As we saw in Lesson Thirty-eight, there will be a judgment for the righteous before the Millennium and one for the wicked after the Millennium. So contrary to common opinion, there won't be a general judgment for both classes at the same time and place. Only the wicked dead will be judged at the final judgment, as is clear in Rev. 20:7-15.

- **That Jesus had brothers and sisters?** In Mt. 13:55 and Mk. 6:1-3 it's clear that He had both brothers and sisters. His own home town people that knew Him said, "Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joses, and of Juda, and Simon? and are not all his sisters here with us?" He had at least four brothers (that is, half brothers). He also had more than two sisters, for if there were only two, the word "all" would have been "both." Jesus is called Mary's firstborn (Mt. 1:25; Lk. 2:7), which would imply she had other children. In Ps. 69:8-9 it's stated of the Messiah that He would be a stranger and an alien to his brethren, His "mother's children." That this passage refers to Christ is clear in Jn. 2:17. His brethren and mother came to him seeking to get him away from the crowds (Mt. 12:46-50). In Jn. 7:3-10 we read they showed lack of sympathy for His work. They later believed in Him and were greatly used of God (Acts 1:14; Jas. 1:1; Acts 12:17; 15:13-21; 21:18; Gal. 1:19; 2:9, 12; 1 Cor. 9:5). The natural meaning of "his brethren" never would have been questioned if it hadn't been for the doctrine of exalting Mary as the mother of God and to identify her with the great goddess of paganism, as we've seen in previous lessons and in my book Revelation Expounded.

- **That the devil knows he will be defeated?** This is clear from Rev. 12:12: "the devil is come down unto you, having great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a short time." His defeat was predicted by God as far back as Gen. 3:15. From there on are many prophecies of his defeat. His doom is predicted in Rev. 20:1-10; Mt. 25:41. God always fulfills His Word, so it's clear that Satan knows his coming defeat and doom.

- **That we are at the very end of this age?** As we saw in Lesson Forty-four, there are many signs now coming to pass that prove we're near the end of this age. Many prophecies are also coming to pass proving this fact. One of the most notable signs is the return of the Jews to their own land, as predicted by the prophets (Jer. 30:18-24; 31:1-40; 32:37-44; Ezek. 37; Hos. 2:4-5). This is now taking place and no one can dispute this fact. This and Mt. 24:37-39; 1 Tim. 4; 2 Tim. 3; 2 Pet. 3:1-3 and other passages prove we're living at the very end of this age.

- **That Satan will be bound for 1,000 years and sin will still continue on the Earth?** It's hard for some to understand how sin will continue on the Earth during the time that Satan will be bound for the 1,000 years of Rev. 20:1-10, but this is easy to understand when we see that sin is transgression of the law and that people will still be free moral agents during that time. There will be laws to obey in the Millennium just as there are today, and people will become sinners by transgressing them just as they do today. That there will be sinners in the Millennium is clear from Isa. 65:20-25; 1 Cor. 15:24-28; Rev. 20:7-10; Zech. 14:16-21.

- **That there is more than one paradise mentioned in Scripture?** One paradise is mentioned as the place where Christ and the penitent thief went, which was in the lower parts of the Earth (Lk. 23:43; Mt. 12:40; Eph. 4:7-11; Ps. 16:10). Another paradise is mentioned as being in the third Heaven (2 Cor. 12:1-3). Then the Garden of Eden is generally understood to be a paradise (Gen. 2:8-25; 3:1-19).

- **That we shall know each other in both Heaven and Hell?** That we'll know each other in Heaven is generally understood and is proven in 1 Cor. 13:12; 15:34-58, but that people will know each other in Hell isn't so universally known. That people will look into eternal Hell is clear from Isa. 66:22-24; Rev. 14:9-11, and as people will be fully conscious forever, as proven in Lesson Thirty-nine, it's also clear they will know each other in Hell.

- **That animals have souls and spirits?** As seen in Lesson Four, Point I, 9, the soul is that which feels and the spirit is that which knows. Animals have both feelings and knowledge, but on a lower scale than people. They weren't created in the likeness of God as people were. They're a lower type of life and have souls and spirits in the sense of feelings, emotions, appetites, desires, passions, and a low form of knowledge, as can be seen in many Scriptures in Lesson Six, Point IX, 2 and 3.

- **That both Jews and Gentiles will live on the Earth forever?** In the next chapter we'll see many Scriptures proving eternal generations of natural peoples of both Jews and Gentiles. God created all people to live forever on the Earth. The Fall of Adam cut off all humanity from natural life on the Earth forever, but in the final restitution of all things, natural people will again live on the Earth as intended by God before the Fall. They'll be the subjects of the resurrected saints in the eternal kingdom forever. See Scriptures in Supplement Two, Question 2, for Scriptures proving eternal generations of natural people.

- **That infant baptism is not taught in Scripture?** There's not one word that mentions any such practice. On the contrary, it's clear from Scripture that baptism comes only after people have repented and have been born-again by the Spirit of God, as we've seen in Lesson Twenty-nine, Point VIII. Infants aren't lost because they're not old enough to be responsible for their sins and therefore couldn't repent of known sins. Therefore, they can't be fit candidates for water baptism, which is a symbol of the death, burial and resurrection of Jesus Christ and a testimony to the world that the individual has been saved from a life of sin and is walking in newness of life. Only personal disciples and followers of Jesus Christ were commanded to be baptized (Mt. 28:19-20; Mk. 16:15-16; Acts 2:38-39).
